jobs.title: Content and Ad Creative Marketing Manager (Contract-to-Hire)
jobs.description:
Boardroom Salon for Men - Content and Ad Creative Marketing Manager (Contract-to-Hire)

Location: Dallas, Fort Worth, or Houston

Job Description:

We’re looking for a Content and Creative Marketing Manager who is obsessed with great marketing content and thumb-stopping ad creative. You’ll be the person who can write a headline, wireframe an ad, and then continue the story through a well-written email or landing page.

Initially we are looking to hire someone as a contractor, but plan to convert this person to full-time after a three month onboarding period.

This is a fully remote role, but because this role will be responsible for leading the creation of photo and video content of the Boardroom Salon experience, it’s important this person be in Texas, close to both Boardroom and Finley’s salon locations.

This is a hands-on role for a marketer who loves making things — someone who thrives at the intersection of brand, content, and creative execution.

We’re looking for a self-starter who’s comfortable running experiments to see what creative performs, then iterating quickly. While this role is part of a small, scrappy marketing team, it will also be working directly with leadership.

What You’ll Own:

Designing and writing content across the funnel: Meta static image ads, Meta video ads, search ads, landing pages, emails, and social posts.

Developing UGC Ads - source UGC creators, write briefs, develop hooks, provide feedback, make edits directly in tools like CapCut

Develop in-salon assets - brochures, wall clings, sandwich boards, etc.

Lead occasional video and photo shoots in salon

Evolve our brand style to create fresh, on-brand assets that stand out

Balance direct response with brand storytelling

Bring creative ideas to life quickly, testing them, and refining based on results.

You’ll own the outcome, but you’ll partner with a contract designer, work with video editor and source UGC creators

What Makes You a Fit:

3–6 years of experience creating marketing content and assets.

A portfolio that proves you can do UGC ads and direct response copy

Comfort with design tools such as Figma, Canva, or Adobe Suite.

Experience using a CMS like Hubspot and Wordpress, as well as email marketing tools like Hubspot or Iterable

Ability to write copy that’s punchy, persuasive, and performance-minded.

Extra credit if you have experience with: E-commerce or DTC content production.

About Boardroom

Boardroom Salon for Men is a fast-growing premium men’s grooming brand redefining the modern barbershop through elevated service, a refined membership model, and a commitment to exceptional client experience. Backed by a results-driven, entrepreneurial team, we operate with startup velocity—constantly testing, learning, and scaling what works to build the future of men’s grooming. Boardroom has expanded into new locations, becoming a national brand with over 55 locations, recently acquiring Austin’s Finley’s Barbershops.

Why join our team?

At Boardroom Styling Lounge, we believe in taking care of our team just as much as we do our clients. Here’s what you can look forward to when you join us:
Career Growth – Advance your career with ample opportunities to grow
Comprehensive Benefits – Health, Dental, Vision, Short- & Long-Term Disability, and Life Insurance after just 60 days*
401K with Match– Secure your future with a retirement plan that has a discretionary match
Paid Time Off – 3 weeks of paid time off!
Paid Holidays – Enjoy up to 7 paid holidays annually
Wellness Perks – Discounted gym and med-spa benefits to enhance your wellness and self-care
Exclusive Employee Discounts – Save on the services and products you love
Employee Assistance Program – Support when you need it most

jobs.title: Marketing Manager (Content, Social & Influencer Strategy)
jobs.description:
Job Title: Marketing Manager (Content, Social & Influencer Strategy)
Location: Boca Raton FL-Remote/Hybrid

About Us:

At Predictive Sales AI, we’re redefining how technology, creativity, and data intersect to help home service businesses grow smarter and faster. Our AI-powered solutions are transforming an entire industry — and we’re looking for a creative, data-driven marketing leader to help us tell that story at scale.

If you understand how to blend strategy with execution, can turn trends into growth engines, and know how to build a brand that actually connects online — this is your opportunity to lead.

Job Overview:

We’re looking for a Marketing Manager who doesn’t just oversee campaigns — they drive them from idea to execution. This role is equal parts strategist and creator, leading our social media, content, and influencer initiatives while shaping how Predictive Sales AI shows up across digital channels.

You’ll own the marketing engine behind our brand presence — developing strategy, creating content, managing collaborations, and optimizing performance to drive awareness, engagement, and growth.

Key Responsibilities:

Develop and execute a multi-platform content strategy across LinkedIn, Instagram, YouTube, and TikTok that builds brand awareness and drives engagement.
Concept, film, and edit short-form videos (Reels, TikToks, YouTube Shorts) that communicate our brand in a compelling, human, and engaging way.
Identify, partner with, and manage influencers and creators to expand reach, credibility, and audience growth.
Ensure consistency in messaging, tone, and visual identity across all channels while actively engaging with our audience.
Use Adobe Creative Cloud (or similar tools) to produce high-quality graphics, thumbnails, and visual content.
Test new content formats, messaging angles, and paid social strategies to continuously improve performance.
Track key metrics (engagement, reach, conversions) and refine strategy based on data, insights, and trends.
Monitor social trends, platform updates, and emerging content formats — and quickly adapt them to fit our brand.

Desired Traits:

We’re after a digital storyteller who’s as strategic as they are creative. You know the algorithms, love the analytics, and understand what makes online communities thrive.

A strategic marketer who can connect content efforts to business outcomes
A hands-on creator who enjoys being behind (and in front of) the camera
A digital storyteller who simplifies complex ideas into engaging content
A trend-savvy thinker who understands what performs on modern platforms
A data-driven decision-maker who tests, learns, and iterates quickly
A self-starter who thrives in a fast-paced, collaborative environment
Required Skills and Experience:
Proven experience in marketing, with a strong focus on content and social media execution
Demonstrated ability to create video content (portfolio required)
Proficiency in video editing and motion graphics (Premiere Pro, After Effects, or similar)
Experience with Adobe Creative Cloud (Photoshop, Illustrator, etc.)
Strong copywriting and storytelling skills — especially short-form hooks
Experience managing influencer partnerships or UGC campaigns
Understanding of SEO, social analytics, and paid social strategies
Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Communications, Film, or related field (or equivalent experience)
Why Join Us?

Innovative Environment: Be part of a forward-thinking company that values creativity and encourages the exploration of new ideas.
Professional Growth: Access opportunities for continuous learning and career advancement within a supportive and dynamic team.
Comprehensive Benefits: Enjoy a competitive salary, performance-based bonuses, flexible work arrangements, and a robust benefits package.
Collaborative Culture: Work in a team-oriented environment where collaboration and mutual respect drive our success.

If you're ready to be part of an innovative, growth-oriented team, apply today!

jobs.title: Associate Digital Marketing Manager
jobs.description:
The Company

Dexcom Corporation (NASDAQ DXCM) is a pioneer and global leader in continuous glucose monitoring (CGM). Dexcom began as a small company with a big dream: To forever change how diabetes is managed. To unlock information and insights that drive better health outcomes. Here we are 25 years later, having pioneered an industry. And we're just getting started. We are broadening our vision beyond diabetes to empower people to take control of health. That means personalized, actionable insights aimed at solving important health challenges. To continue what we've started: Improving human health.

We are driven by thousands of ambitious, passionate people worldwide who are willing to fight like warriors to earn the trust of our customers by listening, serving with integrity, thinking big, and being dependable. We've already changed millions of lives and we're ready to change millions more. Our future ambition is to become a leading consumer health technology company while continuing to develop solutions for serious health conditions. We'll get there by constantly reinventing unique biosensing-technology experiences. Though we've come a long way from our small company days, our dreams are bigger than ever. The opportunity to improve health on a global scale stands before us.

Meet the team:

This is a digital-first role responsible for the strategic ownership and ongoing optimization of Dexcom’s Provider (HCP) website, a core digital platform supporting healthcare professional engagement and adoption. The Associate Marketing Manager will partner cross-functionally to deliver seamless, compliant, and high-performing digital experiences that drive measurable growth in HCP engagement, lead generation, and conversion.

This role requires a strong digital marketer with experience managing CMS platforms, optimizing UX and conversion pathways, and leveraging data and insights to continuously improve performance. This is a remote position reporting to the Senior Digital Marketing Manager, HCP Experience.

Where you come in:

  • Serve as primary owner and internal subject matter expert for the Provider website CMS platform
  • Drive optimization of HCP digital conversion pathways, including forms, content journeys, and lead capture mechanisms
  • Partner with analytics and commercial teams to identify, capture and nurture qualified HCP leads
  • Expand reach and engagement across a broadening HCP audience through SEO-driven content and site optimization
  • Monitor, analyze, and report on website performance, user behavior and conversion metrics to inform ongoing improvements
  • Ensure connection between sales and marketing data touchpoints from responsible digital platforms
  • Identify and implement innovative tools and experiences on the Provider website (testing, enhanced UX, emerging technologies and advanced personalization tools)
  • Partners internally with Product Marketing Team, Creative Services, Commercial Forecasting and Analytics, Sales Operations, Sales Training as well as external vendors

What makes you successful:

  • Hands-on experience managing and optimizing website CMS platforms (Drupal, Wordpress, etc).
  • Strategic mindset with experience working on digital-based initiatives with marketing teams
  • Demonstrable experience driving business value through innovative digital campaigns
  • Experience expanding audience awareness in SEO channel
  • Ability to build strong relationships and collaborate within the team and cross functionally
  • Strong cross-functional collaboration, organizing, problem solving, presentation and decision-making skills

What you’ll get:

  • A front row seat to life changing CGM technology. Learn about our brave #dexcomwarriors community.
  • A full and comprehensive benefits program.
  • Growth opportunities on a global scale.
  • Access to career development through in-house learning programs and/or qualified tuition reimbursement.
  • An exciting and innovative, industry-leading organization committed to our employees, customers, and the communities we serve.

Travel Required:

  • 15-25%

Experience and Education Requirements:

  • Typically requires a Bachelors degree and 5 – 8 years of related expereince.

Remote Workplace: Your location will be a home office; you are not required to live within commuting distance of your assigned Dexcom site (typically 75 miles/120km). If you reside within commuting distance of a Dexcom site (typically 75 miles/120km) a hybrid working environment may be available. Ask about our Flex workplace option.

Please note: The information contained herein is not intended to be an all-inclusive list of the duties and responsibilities of the job, nor are they intended to be an all-inclusive list of the skills and abilities required to do the job. Management may, at its discretion, assign or reassign duties and responsibilities to this job at any time. The duties and responsibilities in this job description may be subject to change at any time due to reasonable accommodation or other reasons. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform essential functions.

An Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, national origin, or protected veteran status and will not be discriminated against on the basis of disability. Dexcom’s AAP may be viewed upon request by contacting Talent Acquisition at talentacquisition@dexcom.com.

If you are an individual with a disability and would like to request a reasonable accommodation as part of the employment selection process, please contact Dexcom Talent Acquisition at talentacquisition@dexcom.com.

Meritain, an Aetna Company, creates and publishes the Machine-Readable Files on behalf of Dexcom. To link to the Machine-Readable Files, please click on the URL provided: https://health1.meritain.com/app/public/#/one/insurerCode=MERITAIN_I&brandCode=MERITAINOVER/machine-readable-transparency-in-coverage?reportingEntityType=TPA_19874&lock=true

To all Staffing and Recruiting Agencies: Our Careers Site is only for individuals seeking a job at Dexcom. Only authorized staffing and recruiting agencies may use this site or to submit profiles, applications or resumes on specific requisitions. Dexcom does not accept unsolicited resumes or applications from agencies. Please do not forward resumes to the Talent Acquisition team, Dexcom employees or any other company location. Dexcom is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes/applications.

Salary:

$82,900.00 - $138,100.00

jobs.title: Digital Marketing Manager (Remote US)
jobs.description:
Directive Consulting is the leading B2B marketing agency for brands that are serious about the metrics that matter most.

With over a decade of expertise and 420+ B2B brands under our belt, we've earned our reputation on real outcomes. We run through 3 divisions: Performance, Communications, and Commerce, all wired to share intelligence and answer to revenue. That system spans everything from Paid Media and Content Marketing, to PR and Paid Social, to Marketplace and Lifecycle Marketing. Tying it all together is our methodology, DiscoverabilityOS, which aligns brand and demand to guide your ICP to choose you across every decision point.

We move fast. We think boldly. We hire people who challenge the status quo, build campaigns worth talking about, and make bold brands impossible to ignore. If you live for performance, love solving hard problems, and want your work to actually mean something, you'll fit right in.

*This role is listed internally as Demand Generation Manager*

About The Role

We are looking to offer a compelling and competitive new demand gen offering for our customers here at Directive. In this role, you will directly work with no more than seven accounts and be directly responsible for their performance marketing.

The ideal candidate for this role is an exceptionally strong T-Shaped Marketer with deep expertise in the SaaS marketplace across Paid, SEO, and CRO. A crucial part of this role will not only be executing campaigns; but also, interfacing with customers, pitching strategy, and ensuring that results are exceeded.

We value your ability to drive client results as an individual contributor and believe that the ideal candidate revels in owning the success of their client portfolio. You will only be successful in this role if you are strong at marketing strategy, execution, communication, and project management.

Roles & Responsibilities

Own the success of SMB clients, across channels

Handle client relationships with poise, confidence, and empathy

Project manage and organize your accounts

Build relationships with your point of contacts

Deep technical knowledge of Strategy, Paid Media, SEO, and CRO for tech companies

Strong SaaS background preferred

Skilled at having a large impact in a very specific role

Creative spark for marketing + deep passion for getting results

What You Offer

2+ years of experience in agency-side marketing

2+ years of experience in performance marketing

Brilliant strategist and a truly T-shaped marketer

World-class project manager who knows how to get things done on time and below cost

Availability to travel

Driven to stay ahead of industry trends, including actively learning how AI and automation can enhance marketing and operations.

What We Offer

 We have a set living wage at Directive; The annual base salary range for this position based in the United States is $80,000- $85,000 USD with potential for bonus eligibility. This salary range is an estimate, and the actual salary may vary based on Directive's compensation practices, job related skills, and depth of experience.

 Medical, dental, vision plans, disability, and life insurance coverage for you and your family that fit your lifestyle

Including a 100% employer-paid plan for you and a 50% employer contribution for your dependents

Benefits to Support the Whole Person:

 Mental - Access to certified therapists through Spring Health, membership to Headspace

 Physical - Gympass

Time Off - Unlimited PTO (2-week minimum), Paid Company Holidays, Your Birthday Off, End of Year Recharge (Closed December 24 - January 1), Paid Parental Leave

Financial - Traditional and Roth 401(k) with a 3% company match

 Bonus - Annual bonus based on tenure, which scales in total amount over time

 Annual Anniversary Trip to Newport Beach, CA with peers and executive leadership for fun and entertainment!

Work Environment Requirements

As a remote-first company, you’ll have the ability to work from anywhere in the US, with the option to enjoy our state-of-the-art offices in Irvine, California. For some positions, as posted, we will accommodate global opportunities where we have established businesses, including Canada, the UK, and Australia. For global locations, you must have established and current work authorization and permanently reside in that country.

This role has the opportunity to operate 100% virtually from your home office. We primarily collaborate with our colleagues through virtual meetings (Zoom), and Slack. In this role, you will be required to operate a laptop computer (PC or Mac available), computer software platforms, and other office productivity tools as necessary. Due to the nature of this role, you must be able to remain stationary for extended periods, must be able to observe and interpret written and/or verbal communication, must have reliable internet access, and a professional background.

To perform this job successfully, an individual must be able to perform each essential job duty satisfactorily. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable qualified individuals with disabilities to perform essential job functions.

If you require reasonable accommodations in completing this application, interviewing, completing any pre-employment testing, or otherwise participating in the employee selection process, direct your inquiries to careers@directiveconsulting.com.

Additional Information

At Directive, one of our core values is People First. We’re committed to fostering a more diverse and inclusive culture in the digital landscape. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status.

jobs.title: Digital Marketing Manager
jobs.description:
*Digital Marketing Manager, Demand Generation*

Company Overview

The Freedonia Group, Inc. (TFG) is a leading provider of global market intelligence products and services. We deliver data‑driven insights that support strategic decision‑making for clients worldwide.

Position Summary

TFG is seeking a Digital Marketing Manager whose primary responsibility is to generate new, qualified inbound leads that convert to revenue. This role owns digital demand‑generation execution and is directly accountable for contributing to sales pipeline and revenue goals.

This is not a branding, communications, or general marketing role. Success is measured by lead volume, lead quality, conversion to opportunity, and downstream sales impact.

Role Purpose

The Digital Marketing Manager plans, executes, and optimizes digital marketing programs designed to fill the sales pipeline. This role works in tight coordination with sales to ensure leads meet defined qualification standards and support bookings targets.

The position requires hands‑on ownership of campaign execution, performance tracking, and continuous optimization based on funnel results.

Key Responsibilities

Lead Generation and Revenue Support

* Own inbound lead‑generation strategy and execution with clear monthly and quarterly lead targets
* Build and run campaigns that support defined sales goals, target accounts, and priority industries
* Deliver marketing‑qualified leads that meet agreed‑upon sales acceptance criteria
* Continuously improve conversion from visitor to lead, lead to MQL, and MQL to opportunity

Campaign Execution and Marketing Automation

* Plan, build, and manage lead‑generation campaigns across email, paid search, paid social, content syndication, and landing pages
* Execute campaigns end to end in HubSpot, including forms, landing pages, workflows, lead scoring, and automated email sequences
* Maintain and optimize lead capture paths to maximize conversion and database growth
* Ensure leads are routed correctly and on time to sales

Sales Alignment and Pipeline Management

* Partner closely with sales leadership to align marketing programs with pipeline needs and revenue targets
* Participate in regular pipeline and performance reviews
* Adjust targeting, messaging, and spend based on sales feedback and close‑rate performance
* Take ownership of issues related to lead quality, volume shortfalls, or conversion gaps

Analytics, Reporting, and Optimization

* Track and report on performance metrics including traffic, conversion rates, CPL, MQL volume, SQL conversion, and campaign ROI
* Use HubSpot and Google Analytics to identify drop‑offs and optimization opportunities throughout the funnel
* Provide clear, actionable performance reporting to leadership focused on revenue impact
* Make data‑driven decisions on channel mix and campaign prioritization

Content, SEO, and Conversion Optimization

* Manage digital content used for lead capture including landing pages, gated assets, emails, and website updates
* Conduct keyword research and SEO optimization to increase qualified inbound traffic
* Optimize forms, CTAs, messaging, and page structure to improve conversion rates
* Evaluate content based on contribution to pipeline, not impressions or engagement alone

Required Skills and Experience

Must Have

* Hands‑on experience driving inbound lead generation tied to sales outcomes
* Demonstrated experience working directly with sales teams and pipeline goals
* Strong working knowledge of HubSpot including workflows, lead scoring, segmentation, and reporting
* Proven ability to analyze funnel performance and improve lead‑to‑opportunity conversion
* Experience with paid search, paid social, email marketing, and landing‑page optimization
* Comfort being accountable for numeric performance and adjusting strategy when goals are missed

Technical Requirements

* HubSpot marketing automation expertise
* Google Analytics and digital campaign reporting
* Google Ads and paid social platforms
* Working knowledge of SEO and conversion rate optimization

Education and Background

* Bachelor’s degree in marketing, communications, or a related field
* 2 to 5 years of experience in demand generation, digital marketing, or marketing operations with measurable lead results

What Success Looks Like

* Sales receives a steady flow of qualified inbound leads
* Marketing programs contribute directly to pipeline growth
* Conversion rates improve quarter over quarter
* Revenue targets are supported by predictable inbound performance

Work Environment

The Freedonia Group is a growing company with high performance standards. We value ownership, accountability, and results. We offer a casual, collaborative work environment and welcome professionals who take responsibility for outcomes.

The Freedonia Group is an equal opportunity employer.

Pay: $70,000.00 - $90,000.00 per year

Benefits:
* 401(k)
* 401(k) matching
* Dental insurance
* Health insurance
* Health savings account
* Life insurance
* Paid time off
* Retirement plan
* Vision insurance

Work Location: Remote

The ideal candidate for this role is an exceptionally strong T-Shaped Marketer with deep expertise in the SaaS marketplace across Paid, SEO, and CRO. A crucial part of this role will not only be executing campaigns; but also, interfacing with customers, pitching strategy, and ensuring that results are exceeded.

We value your ability to drive client results as an individual contributor and believe that the ideal candidate revels in owning the success of their client portfolio. You will only be successful in this role if you are strong at marketing strategy, execution, communication, and project management.

Roles & Responsibilities

Own the success of SMB clients, across channels

Handle client relationships with poise, confidence, and empathy

Project manage and organize your accounts

Build relationships with your point of contacts

Deep technical knowledge of Strategy, Paid Media, SEO, and CRO for tech companies

Strong SaaS background preferred

Skilled at having a large impact in a very specific role

Creative spark for marketing + deep passion for getting results

What You Offer

2+ years of experience in agency-side marketing

2+ years of experience in performance marketing

Brilliant strategist and a truly T-shaped marketer

World-class project manager who knows how to get things done on time and below cost

Availability to travel

Driven to stay ahead of industry trends, including actively learning how AI and automation can enhance marketing and operations.

What We Offer

 We have a set living wage at Directive; The annual base salary range for this position based in the United States is $80,000- $85,000 USD with potential for bonus eligibility. This salary range is an estimate, and the actual salary may vary based on Directive's compensation practices, job related skills, and depth of experience.

 Medical, dental, vision plans, disability, and life insurance coverage for you and your family that fit your lifestyle

Including a 100% employer-paid plan for you and a 50% employer contribution for your dependents

Benefits to Support the Whole Person:

 Mental - Access to certified therapists through Spring Health, membership to Headspace

 Physical - Gympass

Time Off - Unlimited PTO (2-week minimum), Paid Company Holidays, Your Birthday Off, End of Year Recharge (Closed December 24 - January 1), Paid Parental Leave

Financial - Traditional and Roth 401(k) with a 3% company match

 Bonus - Annual bonus based on tenure, which scales in total amount over time

 Annual Anniversary Trip to Newport Beach, CA with peers and executive leadership for fun and entertainment!

Work Environment Requirements

As a remote-first company, you’ll have the ability to work from anywhere in the US, with the option to enjoy our state-of-the-art offices in Irvine, California. For some positions, as posted, we will accommodate global opportunities where we have established businesses, including Canada, the UK, and Australia. For global locations, you must have established and current work authorization and permanently reside in that country.

This role has the opportunity to operate 100% virtually from your home office. We primarily collaborate with our colleagues through virtual meetings (Zoom), and Slack. In this role, you will be required to operate a laptop computer (PC or Mac available), computer software platforms, and other office productivity tools as necessary. Due to the nature of this role, you must be able to remain stationary for extended periods, must be able to observe and interpret written and/or verbal communication, must have reliable internet access, and a professional background.

jobs.title: Content Manager - Commercial Transactions
jobs.description:
REMOTE - Attorney Content Manager - Commercial Transactions

About the Team

Are you looking for an alternative legal career as a commercial transactions subject matter expert? Do you want to work remotely in a supportive team and company that promotes work‑life balance?

LexisNexis seeks a recently practicing attorney with 5+ years of experience as an attorney with experience in the commercial transactions practice area. The role supports the development of online legal content, products, tools, and solutions for attorneys.

About the Role

The person in this role will help deliver a compelling and all‑purpose practical guidance legal product across targeted markets as a member of a multi‑disciplined team of content developers, product planners, external consultants, IT specialists, and marketing and sales representatives.

As a Content Manager, you will apply your subject matter expertise to Practical Guidance , LexisNexis’s legal technology platform. You will collaborate with experienced attorneys and professionals dedicated to helping practitioners work more efficiently.

Responsibilities

Use practical legal knowledge, market awareness, and AI capabilities to inform content development and support strategic project work

Write and edit thought leadership content, practical guidance, and other materials aligned with customer and business needs

Support the development and refinement of AI-enabled tools, features, and content solutions

Contribute to the growth and development of a portfolio of content, products, or strategic initiatives

Build and maintain relationships with attorney authors and other subject matter experts

Stay current on legal, regulatory, and practice developments, as well as trends affecting customers and the business

Work across business units and cross-functional teams to develop content, improve offerings, and enhance customer satisfaction and usage

Use customer feedback, usage trends, and other data insights to inform content strategy and business priorities

Requirements

5+ years of experience as a transactional lawyer

Deep expertise in commercial transaction workflows

Ability to translate complex legal concepts for diverse audiences

Superior writing and analytical skills, with experience in drafting client newsletters/alerts, law journal articles, practical guidance, CLE presentations, or other legal writing a plus

Strong writing, editing, and analytical skills

Excellent communication and presentation skills

Highly organized, proactive, and detail‑oriented

Comfortable working independently and in teams

Proficiency in Microsoft Word, Excel, PowerPoint, AI technologies, and legal research tools

JD (or equivalent) with active bar membership in good standing

Work in a way that works for you:

We promote a healthy work/life balance across the organization. We offer an appealing working prospect for our people. With numerous wellbeing initiatives, shared parental leave, study assistance and sabbaticals, we will help you meet your immediate responsibilities and your long-term goals.

Working flexible hours - flexing the times when you work in the day to help you fit everything in and work when you are the most productive

Working for you

We know that your wellbeing and happiness are key to a long and successful career. These are some of the benefits we are delighted to offer:

Health Benefits: Comprehensive, multi-carrier program for medical, dental and vision benefits
Retirement Benefits: 401(k) with match and an Employee Share Purchase Plan
Wellbeing: Wellness platform with incentives, Headspace app subscription, Employee Assistance and Time-off Programs
Short-and-Long Term Disability, Life and Accidental Death Insurance, Critical Illness, and Hospital Indemnity
Family Benefits, including bonding and family care leaves, adoption and surrogacy benefits
Health Savings, Health Care, Dependent Care and Commuter Spending Accounts
Up to two days of paid leave each to participate in Employee Resource Groups and to volunteer with your charity of choice

About the Business

LexisNexis Legal & Professional (part of RELX) serves customers in more than 150 countries, delivering legal, regulatory, and business information and analytics to improve outcomes and advance the rule of law worldwide.

Base Pay Range for NY is $104,800 - $174,700. Base Pay Range for New York City is $114,300 - $190,500. Base Pay Range for Rochester, NY is $95,300 - $158,800. Base Pay Range for OH is $90,500 - $150,900. Base Pay Range for NJ is $107,646- $171,954.

jobs.title: Content Manager – Secondary Content Strategy
jobs.description:
Content Manager – Secondary Content Strategy

Are you looking for an alternative legal career that blends legal expertise with strategy, data, and innovation?

Do you want to shape the future of legal content in a collaborative, forward-thinking environment that values flexibility and impact?

Do you want to work remotely in a supportive team and company that promotes work-life balance?

About the Team

LexisNexis is seeking an experienced legal professional to join our team as a Content Manager – Secondary Content Strategy (U.S.). In this role, you will help drive the strategy, development, and commercial success of our U.S. secondary content portfolio.

You will combine your understanding of legal practice with a strong customer-first and data-driven mindset to inform portfolio decisions, enhance content quality, and support AI-enabled legal research solutions. This role plays a critical part in ensuring our secondary content remains authoritative, competitive, and aligned with evolving customer needs.

About the Role

As a Content Manager, you will contribute to the strategic direction and performance of our secondary content portfolio as part of a cross-functional team that includes Product, Sales, Marketing, Editorial Operations, and global stakeholders.

You will serve as the voice of the customer, leveraging usage data, direct engagement, and market insights to shape content strategy and investment decisions. You will evaluate competitive dynamics, identify growth opportunities, and help ensure our content is optimized for both traditional research and emerging AI-driven applications.

This role requires strong collaboration, strategic thinking, and the ability to translate insights into actionable plans that improve both customer outcomes and business performance.

Responsibilities:

Strategy & Market Analysis

Serve as the voice of the customer by analyzing usage data, participating in customer calls, and leading NPS follow-up efforts

Translate customer feedback and behavioral insights into actionable portfolio and product strategies

Monitor and assess the U.S. legal market and competitive landscape, including established publishers, legal tech platforms, startups, and alternative providers, to identify trends, risks, and opportunities

Conduct ongoing competitive and market analysis to inform portfolio priorities and investment decisions

Assess LexisNexis’ competitive position by practice area and identify opportunities for growth, differentiation, and content expansion

Identify and evaluate licensing and acquisition opportunities

Collaborate with global colleagues (UK, Canada, and beyond) to share insights and unlock cross-border content and commercialization opportunities

Portfolio Leadership & Content Development

Help lead the strategic direction of the U.S. secondary content portfolio, ensuring it delivers authoritative, high-quality content aligned with customer needs and business goals

Ensure content supports AI applications (e.g., Protégé grounding and answer quality), including coverage, authoritative sourcing, rights clearance, and robust metadata

Partner closely with Product teams to improve AI content retrieval, accuracy, and user experience

Develop and propose annual content development plans for proprietary titles

Review and evaluate author proposals for new titles and updates to existing content, ensuring alignment with portfolio strategy and market demand

Partner with Editorial Operations on Content Development plans for proprietary and licensed content, such as online placement, packaging, and pricing

Commercialization & Go-to-Market

Partner with Sales, Marketing, and Editorial Operations to develop and execute go-to-market strategies

Lead key go-to-market activities, including content development updates, product launches, and sales training

Support conference participation, author engagement, and promotion of authoritative brands and titles

Ensure clear value positioning and messaging that differentiates our secondary content in a competitive market to drive adoption and customer preference

Operational Excellence & Financial Management

Ensure compliance with rights, licensing, and internal governance standards, including approval of sources for AI usage

Partner with Legal and Editorial teams on licensing terms and content use

Support financial performance by aligning portfolio decisions with revenue and profitability goals

Contribute to special projects and continuous improvement initiatives across the portfolio

Requirements

Juris Doctor (JD) and active bar membership in good standing preferred

6+ years of experience practicing law or working in legal publishing, legal technology, or a related field

Strong understanding of the U.S. legal market and attorney workflows

Experience in content strategy, product management, or portfolio management is highly desirable

Demonstrated ability to use data and customer insights to inform strategic decisions

Familiarity with AI-driven legal research tools and digital content platforms preferred

Excellent writing, editing, and analytical skills, with the ability to write effectively and concisely

Strong communication and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to influence cross-functional teams

Highly organized, self-motivated, detail-oriented, and able to manage multiple priorities

Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a fast-paced environment

Work in a way that works for you

We promote a healthy work/life balance across the organization. We offer an appealing working prospect for our people. With numerous wellbeing initiatives, shared parental leave, study assistance and sabbaticals, we will help you meet your immediate responsibilities and your long-term goals.

Working for you

We know that your wellbeing and happiness are key to a long and successful career. These are some of the benefits we are delighted to offer:

Health Benefits: Comprehensive, multi-carrier program for medical, dental and vision benefits

Retirement Benefits: 401(k) with match and an Employee Share Purchase Plan

Wellbeing: Wellness platform with incentives, Headspace app subscription, Employee Assistance and Time-off Programs

Short-and-Long Term Disability, Life and Accidental Death Insurance, Critical Illness, and Hospital Indemnity

Family Benefits, including bonding and family care leaves, adoption and surrogacy benefits

•

Health Savings, Health Care, Dependent Care and Commuter Spending Accounts

Up to two days of paid leave annually to participate in Employee Resource Groups and to volunteer with your charity of choice

About the business

LexisNexis Legal & Professional® (LNLP), which serves customers in more than 150 countries with over 11k employees worldwide, is part of RELX, a global provider of information-based analytics and decision tools for professional and business customers. LNLP provides legal, regulatory, and business information and analytics that help customers increase their productivity, improve decision-making, achieve better outcomes, and advance the rule of law around the world. As a digital pioneer, the company was the first to bring legal and business information online with its Lexis® and Nexis® services.



Primary Location Base Pay Range: New York, NY $114,300 - $190,500. 

If performed in Illinois, the base pay range is $100,100 - $166,800.If performed in Chicago, IL, the base pay range is $104,800 - $174,700.If performed in Ohio, the base pay range is $90,500 - $150,900.U.S. National Base Pay Range: $95,300 - $158,800. Geographic differentials may apply in some locations to better reflect local market rates.


This job is eligible for an annual incentive bonus.

jobs.title: Attorney Content Manager - Labor and Employment
jobs.description:
Content Manager, Labor & Employment

Description

LexisNexis seeks a recently practicing L&E attorney with 5+ years of Labor & Employment experience to plan, draft, and edit practical guidance and secondary content and help develop and maintain online legal products for attorneys. This is a full-time role with competitive benefits; remote work feasible. We are looking for a self-motivated attorney with excellent writing and communication skills who can work both independently and collaboratively. If you are looking for an alternative legal career with opportunities to develop practice area expertise, innovate in legal technology, work with a high-caliber team of experienced professionals, and improve your work-life balance at a company that advances the rule of law and social responsibility, this is the job for you.

AI-Assisted Content Development

Leverage generative and agentic AI, automation, and data to streamline content development and editorial processes for content creation

Craft prompts, evaluate AI outputs, and iterate to achieve optimal results

Track and monitor changes and advances in legal technology and AI

Detailed Role Description:

The person in this role will help deliver a compelling and all-purpose practical guidance legal products and secondary materials across targeted markets as a member of a multi-disciplined team of content developers, product planners, external consultants, IT specialists, and marketing and sales representatives. This person will:

Develop, write, and edit how-to practical guidance content (including practice notes, checklists, and annotated templates) on a wide range of L&E topics

Recruit and manage internal editors and external authors to ensure timely delivery of content

Monitor trends in L&E law and practice

Make recommendations concerning overall content strategy and expansion and assist in product design and AI advancements by applying subject matter and practice knowledge

Monitor competitiveness of the product offering and support sales organization as a resource for practice, product, and competitive knowledge

Assist in delivering customer-facing messaging/marketing and customer training/customer service

Qualifications, Skills and Experience:

5+ years of recent experience (preferably at an Am Law 100/200 firm or in-house) practicing Labor & Employment law

Significant substantive experience in both labor & employment litigation and employment counseling

Excellent writing, editing, and research skills

Significant legal writing experience drafting employment litigation documents such as pleadings, discovery documents, memoranda of law, and L&E counseling documents, such as L&E policies, agreements, and other key L&E documents

A passion for building top notch legal practical guidance and tools in a digital and AI universe

Experience drafting client alerts or other legal articles, and/or drafting or editing practical guidance, and/or composing and delivering CLE presentations or associate training programs a plus

Excellent oral communication and presentation skills

Ability to make pitches to prospective customers and to present trainings to current customers

Strong professional network of L&E attorneys

Experience with L&E health and safety issues a plus

Experience with unions / labor management relations a plus

Experience with business immigration a plus

Experience with labor & employment aspects of M&A / corporate transactions a plus

Comfortable working in Microsoft Word, Excel, and PowerPoint, and conducting online research

Comfortable working in ChatGPT, Co-Pilot, and other AI tools

Must be highly organized, self-motivated, and detail-oriented

Ability to work independently and as part of a team

Law degree from an accredited law school

Active bar membership in good standing

Location: NYC metropolitan area or remote in the United States

About the Business
LexisNexis Legal & Professional® provides legal, regulatory, and business information and analytics that help customers increase their productivity, improve decision-making, achieve better outcomes, and advance the rule of law around the world. As a digital pioneer, the company was the first to bring legal and business information online with its Lexis® and Nexis® services.

LexisNexis seeks a recently practicing attorney with 5+ years of experience as an attorney with experience in the commercial transactions practice area. The role supports the development of online legal content, products, tools, and solutions for attorneys.

About the Role

The person in this role will help deliver a compelling and all‑purpose practical guidance legal product across targeted markets as a member of a multi‑disciplined team of content developers, product planners, external consultants, IT specialists, and marketing and sales representatives.

As a Content Manager, you will apply your subject matter expertise to Practical Guidance , LexisNexis’s legal technology platform. You will collaborate with experienced attorneys and professionals dedicated to helping practitioners work more efficiently.

Responsibilities

Use practical legal knowledge, market awareness, and AI capabilities to inform content development and support strategic project work

Write and edit thought leadership content, practical guidance, and other materials aligned with customer and business needs

Support the development and refinement of AI-enabled tools, features, and content solutions

Contribute to the growth and development of a portfolio of content, products, or strategic initiatives

Build and maintain relationships with attorney authors and other subject matter experts

Stay current on legal, regulatory, and practice developments, as well as trends affecting customers and the business

Work across business units and cross-functional teams to develop content, improve offerings, and enhance customer satisfaction and usage

Use customer feedback, usage trends, and other data insights to inform content strategy and business priorities

Requirements

5+ years of experience as a transactional lawyer

Deep expertise in commercial transaction workflows

Ability to translate complex legal concepts for diverse audiences

Superior writing and analytical skills, with experience in drafting client newsletters/alerts, law journal articles, practical guidance, CLE presentations, or other legal writing a plus

Strong writing, editing, and analytical skills

Excellent communication and presentation skills

Highly organized, proactive, and detail‑oriented

Comfortable working independently and in teams

Proficiency in Microsoft Word, Excel, PowerPoint, AI technologies, and legal research tools

JD (or equivalent) with active bar membership in good standing

Work in a way that works for you:

We promote a healthy work/life balance across the organization. We offer an appealing working prospect for our people. With numerous wellbeing initiatives, shared parental leave, study assistance and sabbaticals, we will help you meet your immediate responsibilities and your long-term goals.

Working flexible hours - flexing the times when you work in the day to help you fit everything in and work when you are the most productive

Working for you

We know that your wellbeing and happiness are key to a long and successful career. These are some of the benefits we are delighted to offer:

Health Benefits: Comprehensive, multi-carrier program for medical, dental and vision benefits
Retirement Benefits: 401(k) with match and an Employee Share Purchase Plan
Wellbeing: Wellness platform with incentives, Headspace app subscription, Employee Assistance and Time-off Programs
Short-and-Long Term Disability, Life and Accidental Death Insurance, Critical Illness, and Hospital Indemnity
Family Benefits, including bonding and family care leaves, adoption and surrogacy benefits
Health Savings, Health Care, Dependent Care and Commuter Spending Accounts
Up to two days of paid leave each to participate in Employee Resource Groups and to volunteer with your charity of choice

About the Business

LexisNexis Legal & Professional (part of RELX) serves customers in more than 150 countries, delivering legal, regulatory, and business information and analytics to improve outcomes and advance the rule of law worldwide.

jobs.title: Attorney Content Manager - Labor and Employment
jobs.description:
Content Manager, Labor & Employment

Description

LexisNexis seeks a recently practicing L&E attorney with 5+ years of Labor & Employment experience to plan, draft, and edit practical guidance and secondary content and help develop and maintain online legal products for attorneys. This is a full-time role with competitive benefits; remote work feasible. We are looking for a self-motivated attorney with excellent writing and communication skills who can work both independently and collaboratively. If you are looking for an alternative legal career with opportunities to develop practice area expertise, innovate in legal technology, work with a high-caliber team of experienced professionals, and improve your work-life balance at a company that advances the rule of law and social responsibility, this is the job for you.

AI-Assisted Content Development

Leverage generative and agentic AI, automation, and data to streamline content development and editorial processes for content creation

Craft prompts, evaluate AI outputs, and iterate to achieve optimal results

Track and monitor changes and advances in legal technology and AI

Detailed Role Description:

The person in this role will help deliver a compelling and all-purpose practical guidance legal products and secondary materials across targeted markets as a member of a multi-disciplined team of content developers, product planners, external consultants, IT specialists, and marketing and sales representatives. This person will:

Develop, write, and edit how-to practical guidance content (including practice notes, checklists, and annotated templates) on a wide range of L&E topics

Recruit and manage internal editors and external authors to ensure timely delivery of content

Monitor trends in L&E law and practice

Make recommendations concerning overall content strategy and expansion and assist in product design and AI advancements by applying subject matter and practice knowledge

Monitor competitiveness of the product offering and support sales organization as a resource for practice, product, and competitive knowledge

Assist in delivering customer-facing messaging/marketing and customer training/customer service

Qualifications, Skills and Experience:

5+ years of recent experience (preferably at an Am Law 100/200 firm or in-house) practicing Labor & Employment law

Significant substantive experience in both labor & employment litigation and employment counseling

Excellent writing, editing, and research skills

Significant legal writing experience drafting employment litigation documents such as pleadings, discovery documents, memoranda of law, and L&E counseling documents, such as L&E policies, agreements, and other key L&E documents

A passion for building top notch legal practical guidance and tools in a digital and AI universe

Experience drafting client alerts or other legal articles, and/or drafting or editing practical guidance, and/or composing and delivering CLE presentations or associate training programs a plus

Excellent oral communication and presentation skills

Ability to make pitches to prospective customers and to present trainings to current customers

Strong professional network of L&E attorneys

Experience with L&E health and safety issues a plus

Experience with unions / labor management relations a plus

Experience with business immigration a plus

Experience with labor & employment aspects of M&A / corporate transactions a plus

Comfortable working in Microsoft Word, Excel, and PowerPoint, and conducting online research

Comfortable working in ChatGPT, Co-Pilot, and other AI tools

Must be highly organized, self-motivated, and detail-oriented

Ability to work independently and as part of a team

Law degree from an accredited law school

Active bar membership in good standing

Location: NYC metropolitan area or remote in the United States

About the Business
LexisNexis Legal & Professional® provides legal, regulatory, and business information and analytics that help customers increase their productivity, improve decision-making, achieve better outcomes, and advance the rule of law around the world. As a digital pioneer, the company was the first to bring legal and business information online with its Lexis® and Nexis® services.

jobs.title: Principal Product Marketing Manager, Customer Engagement
jobs.description:
As a key member of the Creativity & Productivity Enterprise Product Marketing team, you will lead the customer engagement strategy and execution that drives adoption, retention, and expansion across Adobe’s Enterprise solutions. Your deep expertise in building and scaling customer product usage in partnership with marketing and customer success organ i zations , implementing customer adoption frameworks, and designing customer & marketing programs that drive product adoption—combined with a track record of leading large-scale, high-impact engagement initiatives—will enable you to deliver meaningful outcomes across the customer lifecycle.

In this role, you will partner closely with Product Marketing, Sales, Customer Success, Product, Engineering, and Operations to lead complex cross-functional programs that deepen customer engagement, accelerate product usage, and strengthen long-term retention and growth. You will also drive the implementation of product usage instrumentation to unlock data-driven insights that inform engagement strategies.

Reporting to the head of Enterprise GTM, this role will lead the development and execution of Enterprise customer engagement strategies across Adobe’s growing Enterprise portfolio. You will employ customer health metrics, and cross-functional partnerships to shape strategic priorities, inform executive decision-making, and operationalize engagement programs at scale.
Success in this role requires operating effectively in a large, complex data environment and synthesizing sophisticated product analytics, customer health data, and behavioral insights into clear recommendations, scalable programs, and measurable outcomes.

We are especially looking for a leader with proven experience driving product adoption at scale, who can bring structured problem-solving, executive-level storytelling, and a data-driven attitude into our customer engagement strategy.
Key Responsibilities

Own the Enterprise customer engagement strategy across the creative professional, business professional, and workflow and automation product groups. Ensure engagement programs are complementary and drive long-term customer value, adoption, and retention.

Build, scale, and lead enterprise product adoption procedures and frameworks that drive proactive customer engagement, health monitoring, and expansion across the Enterprise customer base.

Create and implement marketing programs specifically designed to drive produc t adoption, including onboarding campaigns, adoption f eature i nitiatives, community events , and re-engagement programs.

Lead the implementation of product insight instrumentation in partnership with Product and Engineering teams, ensuring the right telemetry is in place to track customer engagement , identi fy at-risk accounts, and surface expansion opportunities.

Partner with cross-matrix teams including Product Marketing, Sales, Customer Success, Product, Engineering, and Operations to develop and implement engagement strategies that accelerate time-to-value for customers.

Develop and maintain deep understanding of customer adoption patterns and behavioral segments, providing insights to Product and Product Marketing teams that result in improved engagement and monetization strategies.

Identify and prioritize engagement opportunities (e.g., low-adoption segments, underutilized features, expansion-ready accounts) and develop programs to deliver against these opportunities.

Preempt and problem-solve operational issues related to customer engagement; lead and integrate cross-functional efforts to remove barriers to adoption.
​
Qualifications / Experience

BS/BA preferred OR 12+ years of experience in customer engagement, customer success, or product marketing leadership OR equivalent practical experience. MBA or equivalent experience preferred.

Proven track record of building, scaling, and leading CSM organizations in Enterprise SaaS environments, with demonstrated impact on retention, adoption, and expansion metrics.

Deep experience designing and implementing application feature s to drive customer adoption and analytics frameworks that drive data-informed engagement strategies.

Demonstrated success creating and driving marketing programs that measurably increase product adoption and usage.

Hands-on experience driving product insight instrumentation initiatives in partnership with Product and Engineering teams.

Customer-first orientation, with deep global experience working across various customer segments and lifecycle stages.

Proven ability to translate complex adoption data and customer health metrics into actionable engagement strategies th at optim ize customer experience and unlock growth.

Demonstrated leadership success in dynamic environments, rapidly anticipating and adapting to shifting priorities.

Strong experience leading direct and cross-functional multidisciplinary teams to launch high-impact customer engagement initiatives.

Ability to rapidly assess a problem, define options and evaluate to address critical business issues.

Demonstrated excellence in conceptual thinking and analytical problem-solving.

The desire and ability to take a stand and advocate a course of action, presenting supporting facts and data to back up your position.

Extraordinary communication skills, both verbal and written, at all levels.

A solid sense of self, balanced with the drive to put team first.

Global cultural awareness and experience with global teams.

Experience with Generative AI driven engagement models and tools is a plus.

About Adobe
Adobe empowers everyone to create through innovative platforms and tools that unleash creativity, productivity and personalized customer experiences. Adobe’s industry-leading offerings including Adobe Acrobat Studio, Adobe Express, Adobe Firefly, Creative Cloud, Adobe Experience Platform, Adobe Experience Manager, and GenStudio enable people and businesses to turn ideas into impact, powered by AI and driven by human ingenuity.

Our 30,000+ employees worldwide are creating the future and raising the bar as we drive the next decade of growth. We’re on a mission to hire the very best and believe in creating a company culture where all employees are empowered to make an impact. At Adobe, we believe that great ideas can come from anywhere in the organization. The next big idea could be yours.

Let’s Adobe together
At Adobe, we believe in creating a company culture where all employees are empowered to make an impact. Learn more about Adobe life, including our values and culture , focus on people, purpose and community , Adobe for All , comprehensive benefits programs , the stories we tell , the customers we serve, and how you can help us advance our mission of empowering everyone to create.

Adobe is proud to be an Equal Employment Opportunity employer. We do not discriminate based on gender, race or color, ethnicity or national origin, age, disability, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, veteran status, or any other protected characteristic. Learn more.

Adobe aims to make our Careers website and recruiting process accessible to any and all users. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation to navigate our website or complete the application process, email accommodations@adobe.com or call +1 408-536-3015.

If these tools are used inappropriately or in a way that misrepresents your work, your application may not move forward in the process.

As part of the hiring experience, we provide clear guidance on where AI is encouraged during the process and where it’s restricted during live interviews. See how we think about AI in the hiring experience .

Your recruiter can share more about the specific salary range for the job location during the hiring process.

In California, the pay range for this position is $180,300 - $261,150

jobs.title: Senior Manager, Marketing Strategy (International Travel)
jobs.description:
About Us
Visa is a world leader in payments technology, facilitating transactions between consumers, merchants, financial institutions and government entities across more than 200 countries and territories, dedicated to uplifting everyone, everywhere by being the best way to pay and be paid.

At Visa, you'll have the opportunity to create impact at scale — tackling meaningful challenges, growing your skills and seeing your contributions impact lives around the world.

Join Visa and do work that matters – to you, to your community, and to the world. Progress starts with you.

Job Description

The Senior Manager of Marketing Strategy (International Travel) is primarily responsible for driving cross‑border payment volume growth for our business units, products, and markets that seek to build relevance and preference among international travelers. This role requires a deep understanding of the international travel payments industry, as well as the ability to translate complex insights from consumer travel behaviors, market dynamics, and travel payments economics into clear, actionable marketing strategies for global business units and markets around the world.

Success in this role depends on strong cross‑functional partnership with the Cross-border Business Unit, regional marketing partners, Corporate Strategy, and creative agencies, and the ability to influence marketing planning, investment prioritization, and execution at scale. Importantly, this position serves as a core contributor to the cross-border Marketing Center of Excellence, driving thought leadership, strategic rigor, and market enablement across all priority travel initiatives.

The position reports to the Senior Director – Travel Experiences and will be based in Visa’s San Francisco office.

Essential Functions:

Manage and execute the marketing strategy for international travel:

Lead the day-to-day operations of strategy development for growth travel corridors aligned to Visa’s strategic goals by translating actionable frameworks into scalable creative platforms and innovation initiatives across priority travel corridors.
Partner with regional marketing leads to optimize and evolve corridor‑level strategies, in lock step with changes to the business strategy, competitive dynamics or macro economic changes.
Refine traveler journey stages, trip motivations and consumer spend behavior as new learnings and insights are unlocked from priority travel cohorts.
Optimize marketing programs for ‘sending’ and ‘receiving’ markets to ensure ‘two-hands’ clapping in close coordination with Regional teams to minimize region-to-region coordination and efforts.
In collaboration with Global Brand Creative Studio, support strategy development for global cross border campaigns that are centrally managed, studio built and regionally executed.
Influence annual operating plans to ensure cross-border initiatives are appropriately prioritized and funded, and oversee budget management for global marketing workstreams.
Brief cross-border based executions within sponsorships to improve brand campaign business driven results – FIFA, OLY and F1 programs – going beyond the sponsorship itself and the full travel experience.

Act as the Cross-border subject matter expert and enable markets to deliver effectively and efficiently:

Evaluate and build new global channel strategy (e.g. airports) to determine new central optimization of media planning and buying.
Assess regionally led deals (e.g, sponsorships) to deploy more effective non-host market advertising of ‘sending markets’ and lift preference and usage of Visa – includes marketing strategy for Visa owned properties, such as Visa Live.
Build and advance cross-border thought leadership through research, market intelligence, and industry trend analysis, translating insights into best practices that improve marketing effectiveness.
Lead global‑to‑regional enablement and change management by hosting regular regional share‑outs and evolving the operating model and go‑to‑market approach
Develop and implement a global messaging framework that enables regions to confidently and consistently activate compliant, high‑impact brand and product claims across priority corridors.
Partner with Regions and Insights & Analytics to define a measurement framework and learning agenda for Travel initiatives, ensuring insights inform current optimization and future strategy development.
Support innovation pilots in partnership with the BU and Regions to drive new growth and acquisition for Visa and Visa clients
Drive innovation sprints with new AI platforms, partner platforms to push category superiority over competitive set.

This is a hybrid position. Expectation of days in office will be confirmed by your hiring manager.

Visa requires at least 3 days in office, expectations of these days will be confirmed by your Hiring Manager.

Qualifications

Basic Qualifications • 8 or more years of relevant work experience with a Bachelor Degree or at least 5 years of experience with an Advanced Degree (e.g. Masters, MBA, JD, MD) or 2 years of work experience with a PhD Preferred Qualifications • 9 or more years of relevant work experience with a Bachelor Degree or 7 or more relevant years of experience with an Advanced Degree (e.g. Masters, MBA, JD, MD) or 3 or more years of experience with a PhD • Degree in Marketing, Communications, Business, or related field. MBA or advanced degree preferred. • 7+ years of progressive experience in global brand strategy, brand marketing, or related field - travel related industry (airlines, hospitality, OTAs, etc.) strongly preferred. • Thought leader of effective creative, brand development, and multichannel marketing campaigns, with a focus on international travelers, as a subject matter expert. • Agency-side or client-side experience working with or at global brands focus on the international travel segments, such as OTAs, airlines, or hospitality companies, and having held a combination of global, regional or local market positions. • Experience working and living in multiple countries as well as collaborating with teams across geographies to have an informed market-first view to global marketing. • Embodies aspirational behaviors of leading courageously, obsessing about customers, collaborating as one Visa, and executing with excellence. • Strong executive presence with highly effective written and verbal communication skills including the ability to present with impact. • Self-motivated, resourceful and results oriented individual. • Strong planning, organizational and problem-solving skills. • Ability to drive opportunities independently across multiple complex projects that have strategic implications for the brand and company, while operating in an environment that changes quickly.

U.S. Applicants Only

The estimated salary range for this position is $153,700.00 to $ 246,200.00 USD per year, which may include potential sales incentive payments (if applicable). Salary may vary depending on job-related factors which may include knowledge, skills, experience, and location. In addition, this position may be eligible for bonus and equity.Visa has a comprehensive benefits package for which this position may be eligible that includes Medical, Dental, Vision, 401(k), FSA/HSA, Life Insurance, Paid Time Off, and Wellness Program.

Work Hours

Varies upon the needs of the department.

Travel Requirements

This position requires travel 5-10% of the time.

Mental/Physical Requirements

This position will be performed in an office setting. The position will require the incumbent to sit and stand at a desk, communicate in person and by telephone, frequently operate standard office equipment, such as telephones and computers.

Visa is an EEO Employer

Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, gender identity, disability or protect veteran status. Visa will also consider for employment qualified applicants with criminal histories in a manner consistent with the EEOC guidelines and applicable local law, including the requirements of Article 49 of the San Francisco Police Code.

jobs.description:
Adobe’s next billion dollar business is GenStudio. At the heart of this strategy is is GenStudio for Performance Marketing, Adobe’s groundbreaking generative AI product crafted to empower marketing and creative teams to develop high performing, on-brand omnichannel content.

jobs.title: Product Marketing Manager and GenAI Strategist
jobs.description:
About the Role
At Adobe, we’re reshaping how people create using the power of Generative AI—and GenStudio is at the heart of this transformation . We’re looking for a highly technical and customer-obsessed Product Marketing Manager to lead customer-facing initiatives that drive product adoption, gather actionable feedback, and translate insights into go-to-market strategies. This role is ideal for someone who thrives at the intersection of product, marketing, and user engagement.
Key Responsibilities

Lead on-site AI pilots engagements with strategic customers who will test Adobe's latest generative AI technology for their marketing organization.

Serve as the PMM lead for strategic accounts, ensuring their needs are represented and met.

Capture and synthesize customer feedback.

Partner closely with Product Management , Engineering and Sales to influence roadmap decisions and feature prioritization.

Partner with sales teams to build and scale workshop content and frameworks that influence both technical and business audiences.

Deliver repeatable, high-quality resources ( technical playbooks, enablement kits) for account teams.

jobs.title: Principal Product Marketing Manager
jobs.description:
The Opportunity
Adobe Firefly is transforming creativity through generative AI, enabling creative professionals (“ CPros ”) to ideate, design, and produce content in entirely new ways. As a Principal Product Marketing Manager for Firefly (Inbound), you will focus on understanding the needs of CPros and shaping how Firefly delivers value across Adobe’s creative ecosystem.

This is a high-impact role where you’ll help to drive strategic product marketing initiatives, influence product direction and go-to-market strategies and work cross-functionally to guide execution . You’ll collaborate deeply with product, outbound marketing, and cross-functional teams to ensure Firefly is discoverable, adopted, and loved by creative professionals worldwide.

What You’ll Do

Partner with Firefly and flagship app product teams to shape roadmap priorities based on customer insights and cross-app journey opportunities.

Ensure deep understanding of CPro workflows, needs, and aspirations through research, data analysis, and direct engagement.

Partner closely with outbound PMMs for CPro segments to co-develop marketing programs that drive awareness and adoption of Firefly.

Refine Firefly’s value proposition for creative professionals, ensuring it resonates across disciplines and use cases.

Represent the strategic vision of Firefly for CPros to senior leadership and cross-functional partners.
What You’ll Bring

10+ years of experience in product marketing, product management, or strategy roles, ideally in creative software or AI technologies.

Proven ability to influence and align teams to bring ideas to life across product , marketing and go-to-market.

Track record of driving critical initiatives end-to-end, from concept through launch.

Strong analytical and critical thinking skills; adept at using data to inform decisions.

Exceptional communication and storytelling abilities, with experience influencing executive partners.

Passion for creativity, design, and emerging technologies like generative AI.

BS degree in Marketing, Business, or a related field preferred OR equivalent practical experience.

Why Join Us?
You’ll be part of a team shaping the future of creativity at Adobe—building responsibly developed AI tools that empower millions of professionals. This is a rare opportunity to lead in a fast-moving space, drive meaningful impact, and help define how the next generation of creators work.

jobs.title: Director, Product Marketing - Sam's Club Member Access Platform (MAP)
jobs.description:
Position Summary...

What you'll do...
The Director of Product Marketing will lead the Product Marketing Management (PMM) function for Sam’s Club MAP, serving as the strategic bridge between product innovation and market adoption. This leader will define how MAP’s advertising and experience solutions are positioned, packaged, and brought to market—ensuring clear, compelling narratives that drive advertiser adoption, revenue, and market differentiation. Overseeing PMMs across Onsite Display, Search, Offsite Media, and Experiential and In-Club, this role ensures every offering is translated from product capability into customer value, with consistent go-to-market execution across the portfolio. Acting as both the voice of the product to the market and the voice of the customer back to the organization, this leader drives clarity, alignment, and measurable business impact.
What You'll Do... Product Positioning & Commercial Narrative
Define and own the end-to-end product marketing strategy across MAP’s portfolio (Onsite Display, Search, Offsite, Experiential and In-Club).
Develop clear, differentiated positioning, messaging, and value propositions that translate product capabilities into advertiser value and business outcomes.
Build a cohesive external narrative that connects MAP’s media, data, and experiences into a compelling, full-funnel story.
Go-to-Market Strategy & Revenue Enablement
Lead go-to-market planning, launches, and commercialization for all MAP products and solutions.
Partner with Product, AdTech, and Sales to align on packaging, pricing, and adoption strategies that drive revenue growth.
Ensure consistency and quality across all GTM materials, sales narratives, and market-facing touch points.
Sales Enablement & Adoption
Oversee the development of pitch decks, case studies, toolkits, and training materials that enable Sales to effectively sell MAP solutions.
Partner with Training & Enablement to drive readiness, education, and ongoing adoption across internal and external stakeholders.
Ensure teams are equipped to clearly communicate product value and differentiate MAP in market.
Cross-Functional Leadership & Product Alignment
Serve as the central point of alignment across Product, Sales, Marketing, Insights, AdTech, and Operations.
Represent the voice of the customer and market to inform product roadmap, innovation, and prioritization.
Lead cross-functional GTM processes, ensuring seamless coordination and execution across work streams.
PMM Team Leadership & Execution Excellence
Lead and develop a team of PMMs aligned to key product pillars: Onsite Display, Search, Offsite Media, Experiential and In-Club.
Establish best practices for positioning, GTM planning, measurement, and continuous optimization.
Drive accountability for adoption, performance, and impact across the PMM function.
You'll Sweep Us Off Our Feet If You Have...
10+ years of experience in product marketing, retail media, ad tech, or related fields.
Proven experience leading product marketing or go-to-market functions at scale.
Strong commercial acumen with a track record of driving product adoption and revenue growth.
Deep understanding of omnichannel media, retail ecosystems, and advertiser needs.
Ability to translate complex technical capabilities into clear, compelling narratives.
Experience leading cross-functional initiatives in a matrixed organization.
Exceptional communication, storytelling, and executive presence.

-Financial benefits include 401(k), stock purchase and company-paid life insurance

- Other benefits include short-term and long-term disability, company discounts, Military Leave Pay, adoption and surrogacy expense reimbursement, and more.
