jobs.title: Principal Product Marketing Manager (Acrobat for Students)
jobs.description:
The Opportunity
Adobe is seeking a Principal Product Marketing Manager to help scale Acrobat’s student business. This role will drive key pillars of the business from monetization and branding to end-to-end product experiences and go-to-market execution. You’ll lead critical workstreams while collaborating across an extended group, helping translate strategy into clear, high-impact execution.
What You’ll Do
Lead monetization strategy and execution

Define and evolve student packaging, pricing, and offer strategy across free and paid experiences

Partner alongside Growth and Lifecycle teams to build and optimize upgrade paths and conversion journeys

Support testing and scaling of student offers, including freemium entry points and paid SKUs Drive branding and positioning for students

Help define and refine how Acrobat shows up for students globally

Translate product capabilities into clear, differentiated value propositions and messaging grounded in real student needs

Ensure consistency across messaging from campaign to product experience Own full-cycle product marketing for key experiences

Lead PMM for core student experiences from concept through launch and iteration

Partner closely with Product to develop roadmap inputs, define use cases, and ensure strong product-market fit

Build clear narratives that connect features to meaningful user outcomes Influence the product development plan and priorities

Bring the voice of the student into product decisions through insights, research, and market context

Influence roadmap prioritization, ensuring alignment with growth and monetization goals

Identify gaps and opportunities across the student journey and translate them into actionable product inputs Guide outbound creative and campaign assets

Working with Outbound teams, drive development of high-quality and relevant creative assets across channels (web, paid media, social, partnerships)

Ensure creative reflects both product truth and student context Guide benchmarks, measurement, and performance tracking

Partner with analytics and growth teams to define success metrics across acquisition, activation, engagement, and monetization

Proven ability to lead complex workstreams within a broader team environment

Experience influencing product roadmap and cross-functional priorities

Experience with monetization strategy , including pricing, packaging, and lifecycle conversion

Strong foundation in branding, positioning, and go-to-market execution

Experience partnering closely with Product on end-to-end product marketing

Clear, structured communicator with the ability to influence across teams

jobs.title: Manager, Marketing Planning And Strategy - Producer
jobs.description:
Position Summary...

What you'll do...
Walmart Connect is Walmart’s rapidly growing advertising platform designed to help brands engage with consumers across its vast omnichannel ecosystem. Our mission is to connect brands more meaningfully with consumers through impactful advertising solutions. Leveraging Walmart’s unparalleled data and scale, we provide measurable results for our partners.

Lightbox Creative Solutions (LBCS) is transforming the retail media landscape by delivering innovative and creative marketing campaigns for top global brands. We produce creative executions that are insights-driven, on-brand, and ultimately lead to a memorable experience for the end-user while driving impact for advertisers.

Overview
The Producer independently leads and manages multiple high-visibility marketing and creative projects, working directly with business partners, creative leadership, enterprise teams, sales, legal, freelancers, and more. They own the full execution of the creative project lifecycle in a fast-paced environment, ensuring all deliverables meet objectives, quality standards, and timelines. They translate strategy into actionable plans, proactively manage risks and dependencies, and ensure seamless collaboration across stakeholders to bring high-impact, strategic creative solutions to life across the Walmart ecosystem.

Key Responsibilities
Independent Project Leadership
Lead and manage multiple creative projects from inception through completion.
Develop project scopes, timelines, and milestones and ensure alignment with creative strategy and business objectives.
Maintain ownership of day-to-day execution for all assigned campaigns.

Creative Project Lifecycle Management
Oversee the production lifecycle of video, site assets, emails, social content, in-store creative, and event experiences.
Ensure all deliverables meet project objectives and are accurate, high-quality, and approved by all stakeholders.

Cross-Functional Collaboration
Collaborate across Walmart business units and external partners to ensure alignment across marketing, sales, media, and creative teams.
Act as a liaison between internal teams and external partners to maintain project continuity.

External Partner Management
Work with agency partners and freelancers to execute creative deliverables.
Provide clear direction, feedback, and timelines.

Tool + Process Expertise
Apply strong operational and project management capabilities.
Use Airtable and/or other work management platforms to track and report on project data.

Quality & Consistency
Ensure all creative outputs reflect brief requirements, brand standards, and KPIs.
Route assets for internal and external approvals, ensuring seamless stakeholder engagement.

Team Collaboration
Demonstrate a consistent spirit of partnership, collaboration, and operational excellence.

Position Requirements:
Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Advertising, Fine Arts, Design, or related field.
Extensive experience across agency or in-house marketing (project management, account management, editorial management, integrated marketing).
Previous experience with shopper marketing, account management, and retail or digital media.
Highly organized with strong attention to detail.
Strong relationship-building skills and ability to work with diverse roles and disciplines.
Positive, collaborative approach to problem-solving.
Experience using Airtable required (or equivalent project/work tools).
Passion for Walmart, creativity, and impactful marketing.

jobs.title: Senior Manager - Growth Leader, Marketing Technology / AI and Enablement (Life Sciences)
jobs.description:
Senior Manager - Growth Leader, Marketing Technology / AI and Enablement (Life Sciences)
Throughout the health ecosystem, you'll find courageous and inspiring people who are committed to driving transformation, advancing health equity, and leading a well-being revolution. Where you find innovators committed to sustainable progress, you'll find Deloitte's Life Sciences & Health Care practice. Our leaders work side-by-side to orchestrate and deliver on the business of science and health. We bring trusted, flexible approaches that help foster innovation, harness new technologies, and formulate consumer-driven strategies to engineer a digitally enabled, equitable future of health - starting today.

Recruiting for this role ends on June 26, 2026
Work you'll do
As a Senior Manager, you will operate as a market-facing growth leader responsible for originating, shaping, and closing new business while delivering high-impact transformation programs across the Content Supply Chain (CSC) and Marketing Technology ecosystem.

Deliver programs across Life Sciences marketing, content supply chain, MarTech, and GenAI
Lead end-to-end pursuits including pipeline development, RFP responses, pricing strategy, and contract negotiation
Build and manage a robust pipeline with accountability for sales targets
Identify whitespace opportunities and develop new offerings and thought leadership
Lead large-scale transformation programs across DAM, MRM, MLR, and GenAI workflows
Manage global delivery teams using Agile methodologies
Act as a trusted advisor across Marketing, IT, and Operations stakeholders
Translate business needs into scalable technology and GenAI solutions
Recruit, mentor, and build high-performing teams
Manage engagement financials, pricing models, and SOW development
The team
An evolving customer and regulatory landscape, mounting pipeline of next-gen assets and portfolio pressures, and intensifying competition are presenting new organizational and operational hurdles for Life Sciences companies. These complex market dynamics are requiring new levels of rigor and insight in business strategy development and problem-solving, both domestically and globally.

We are looking for practitioners who will work alongside Life Sciences organizations from bio-technology firms to global big pharma to medical devices, to drive business value through end-to-end delivery of solutions based on strategy development for heart of business issues.
Qualifications
Required:

10-15+ years of consulting experience in Life Sciences or highly regulated industries
6+ years in a sales-driven role with proven success including pursuits and deal closing
6+ experience building and managing pipeline and leading RFPs
10+ years experience with MarTech platforms (Veeva Vault PromoMats, Adobe, Salesforce, etc.)
10+ years experience leading large-scale transformation programs
6+ years experience with SOWs, pricing models, and financial management
1+ years of experience with GenAI, LLMs, and agentic AI solutions
Must be legally authorized to work in the United States without the need for employer sponsorship, now or at any time in the future
Ability to travel up to 50% on average based on the work you do and the clients and industries/sectors you serve

Preferred:

Certifications in Adobe or Salesforce platforms
Experience with CDP, CRM, and broader MarTech ecosystem
Background in data science or AI/ML
Experience with modular content and taxonomy frameworks
Thought leadership experience (speaking, publishing, etc.)

The wage range for this role takes into account the wide range of factors that are considered in making compensation decisions including but not limited to skill sets; experience and training; licensure and certifications; and other business and organizational needs. The disclosed range estimate has not been adjusted for the applicable geographic differential associated with the location at which the position may be filled. At Deloitte, it is not typical for an individual to be hired at or near the top of the range for their role and compensation decisions are dependent on the facts and circumstances of each case. A reasonable estimate of the current range is $158,900 to $292,900.

You may also be eligible to participate in a discretionary annual incentive program, subject to the rules governing the program, whereby an award, if any, depends on various factors, including, without limitation, individual and organizational performance.

jobs.title: Senior Marketing Manager, Credit Karma Money - Credit Karma
jobs.description:
Overview

We’re looking for a Senior Marketing Manager to help grow Intuit Credit Karma Money’s primary banking product. You’ll support cross-channel lifecycle campaigns focused on acquiring new banking users, activating direct deposit, and driving long-term engagement. You will develop a deep understanding of member needs to craft highly personalized marketing messages and experiences that touch millions of members every day. You will have the opportunity to learn from best-in-class growth marketers, and continue to build a strong foundation for your career.

Responsibilities

Drive growth strategy & execution for core product initiatives from inception to launch across multiple channels, including email, push notifications and in-app
Design and implement A/B and multivariate tests, including hypothesis development, sample sizing, and success evaluation

Confident, emotionally intelligent, and professional with high integrity.

Coach and develop talent to become content and creative experts

Mindset & Work Style

Bias for action with ability to execute in fast-paced, changing environment.

Comfortable navigating ambiguity and evolving priorities.

eam-oriented, collaborative, and proactive.

Healthcare & Compliance

jobs.description:
Overview

The Global Business Solutions Group (“GBSG”) is Intuit’s largest business unit with $11B+ in revenue, serving 8M+ customers in the QuickBooks line of products, which includes accounting, payments, payroll, capital lending and banking. The US GBSG Finance team is critical in driving business growth and profitability through strategic, financial and operational leadership.

In service to enabling GBSG and Intuit to achieve its bold financial goals, the GBSG Investments Finance team is responsible for end-to-end oversight of GBSG headcount, operating expenses, and workforce-related financial planning. jobs.title: AI Marketing Manager, Marketing Futures
jobs.description:
Overview

We are looking for an AI Marketing Manager to join Intuit’s Marketing Futures Team, a group embedded in our broader marketing organization tasked with defining, piloting, and scaling AI enabled and future oriented marketing capabilities. You will own the strategy and execution of AI driven pilots inside live performance marketing campaigns and channels, with a mandate to compress time to launch, expand creative variant volume, and drive measurable lift in ROAS, CTR, CVR, and incrementality across paid social, paid search, programmatic, and owned channels.

This role sits at the intersection of performance media, generative creative production, and AI driven search. You will partner with paid media, creative, SEO, and web teams to embed AI into the campaign loop end to end, from creative variant generation through Adobe Gen Studio, Celtra, and other AI production platforms, to dynamic personalization at scale, to AEO and GEO optimization that captures incremental visibility in AI driven search surfaces. We are looking for a performance marketer who is an avid learner, experimenter, and builder ready to redefine how brands win in an AI native campaign environment.

Responsibilities

Lead AI pilots inside live performance marketing campaigns across paid social, paid search, programmatic, and owned channels, with measurable goals tied to ROAS, CTR, CVR, and incremental GNS.

Own creative production at scale workflows through Adobe Gen Studio, Celtra, and emerging AI creative platforms, generating high volume on brand variants for Meta, Google, TikTok, LinkedIn, and DV360 with downstream connection to Motion for performance analysis.

Partner with paid media, performance creative, and agency teams to design and run experiments that quantify AI driven lift over agency baselines on creative volume, time to launch, and channel performance.

Build personalization at scale workflows that pair Adobe Gen Studio creative output with audience targeting, dynamic creative optimization, and landing page variation, with the goal of moving from campaign level testing toward audience level personalization.

Develop and execute a content strategy optimized for AEO and GEO across Google AI Overviews, AI Mode, ChatGPT, Perplexity, Gemini, and emerging AI native browsers, with structured content frameworks, schema markup, and entity rich metadata that drive citation and ranking.

Track and analyze AI search performance using ProFound, Adobe LLM optimizer, including SGE SERP tracking, citation rates, and brand visibility across LLM platforms, and translate findings into prioritized content and SEO actions.

Build and maintain reusable prompt/skill libraries, AI assisted creative briefs, and campaign workflow templates that scale across the marketing org and reduce cost per asset.

Embed within performance marketing and creative teams during tentpole campaign moments to integrate AI from ideation through production, distribution, and post launch optimization.

Design and run experiments that measure AI generated versus traditionally produced creative across tentpole campaigns, tracking performance lift, production efficiency, creative quality, and operational time savings.

Partner with the Head of Marketing Futures and the Data and AI Systems Lead to graduate proven pilots from skill to MCP integration to production deployment within GTM Tech and engineering systems.

Build and maintain internal playbooks and knowledge assets that enable the broader marketing org to adopt AI enabled performance marketing and content practices.

Qualifications

4 to 6 years of experience in performance marketing, growth marketing, or paid media, with at least 2 years focused on AI driven creative production, AEO and GEO, or generative content workflows.

Hands on experience with AI creative production platforms such as Adobe Gen Studio, Celtra, Runway, Midjourney, or VEO, including building templates, training brand systems, and shipping production grade variants into ad platforms.

Working knowledge of paid media platforms (Meta, Google Ads, TikTok, LinkedIn, DV360) and how AI generated creative integrates with creative testing, dynamic creative optimization, and personalization workflows.

Deep understanding of how LLMs surface and cite content, with hands on experience in schema markup, structured data, entity based optimization, and knowledge graph design.

Proficiency with generative AI tools for content creation (ChatGPT, Claude, Jasper, Copy.ai) and experience building prompt libraries or AI assisted editorial and creative workflows.

Strong analytical skills with comfort working in data, metrics, and experimentation. Experience with performance marketing analytics (Datorama, GA4, Adobe Analytics, Amplitude) and SEO and GEO platforms (ProFound, Ahrefs, Semrush).

Experience working within or alongside performance creative and campaign teams, with comfort operating in fast moving production environments for large scale marketing moments.

Experience creating multi format content at scale (text, video, interactive) using AI enhanced production workflows.

Excellent communication skills and comfort working cross functionally with technical, creative, performance, and analytics teams.

Experience in fintech, SaaS, or regulated industries is a plus.

Intuit provides a competitive compensation package with a strong pay for performance rewards approach. This position may be eligible for a cash bonus, equity rewards and benefits, in accordance with our applicable plans and programs (see more about our compensation and benefits at Intuit®: Careers | Benefits). Pay offered is based on factors such as job-related knowledge, skills, experience, and work location. To drive ongoing fair pay for employees, Intuit conducts regular comparisons across categories of ethnicity and gender. The expected base pay range for this position is: Bay Area $169,000 - $229,000

The Opportunity

Structural Heart

Structural Heart Business Mission: Why We Exist

Our business purpose is to restore health and improve quality of life by designing and providing device and management solutions for treating structural heart disease.

The Product Marketing Manager - TMTI is the core marketing owner and driver of the success for the MitraClip and TriClip product lines in the Structural Heart business unit. This role will report to the Director of US Marketing Structural Heart and will have primary marketing and budget responsibility for designated products.

The Product Marketing Manager – TMTI will focus on product marketing for MitraClip and TriClip, MR and TR and the various products within the U.S. This position will work closely with the sales team and be responsible for aspects of US strategy development plus Forecasting, Financial and Marketing Plan Development, Sales Materials and Training, Pricing, and Materials Management. The Product Manager shall maintain US product expertise for MitraClip and TriClip products/franchises, including market sizes/trend share, customer needs and targeting, product performance/benefits/weaknesses/positioning. This role will also provide on-going field support and will focus on growing market awareness for and related disease states, including new product launches. Success will be measured against sales and margin expectations, sales force competency/knowledge level and market image.

What You’ll Work On

Maintain worldwide market expertise and relative Structural Heart technologies, including in-depth knowledge of US competitor products and strategies, market trends and dynamics, market/franchise critical success factors, and customer needs.
Develop and create best in market product messaging and collaborate with global marketing and field team to market test messaging feasibility.
Develop strategies to address product performance issues.
Develop and update annual marketing plans/strategies including market analysis, customer segmentation and targeting, product positioning, competitive assessments, and SWOT or other situation assessment.
Create and develop US specific strategies for new product launches.
Support launch readiness by developing product inventory timing, marketing programs, and working with communications manager to develop promotional collateral.
Take a leadership role on project teams to work closely with the project manager to drive accountability and team achievement of goals and milestones.
Manage creative agencies to plan, develop, and execute new US materials.
Partner with regulatory colleagues in the review and negotiation of materials to ensure competitiveness, accuracy, and compliance.
Develop plan & update US forecasts and justification for respective product lines worldwide and present to BU senior management.
Work with clinical team to develop and drive strategic publication initiatives within the US.
In this core Marketing role, the PM will drive and be held accountable to deliver Sales, Margin, and PEx goals for designated products.
The Product Manager will be expected to be responsive and able to directly address questions and requests of senior management, including the SH DVP.

Required Qualifications:

Bachelors’ Degree with 4+ years related experience, with at least one year of experience in marketing or product management. MBA with 1-3 years of related experience also considered.
Substantial financial skills, market analysis and advanced communication skills.
Looking for a self-starter, proactive by nature, interested in breaking the status quo, and innately curious.
Strong story telling skills to captivate and influence audiences.
Knowledge/ability to develop and execute a "new" product launch.
Requires strong analytical skills as well as written and oral communication skills.
Must be able to understand technical and scientific information. Must be willing to understand and comply with Regulatory requirements.
Must be able to influence and motivate individuals from sales, marketing, and other functional areas to drive product sales.
Must be able to complete multiple projects simultaneously with tight deadlines.
Ability to travel 30-40% of the time

jobs.title: Senior Technical Marketing Manager - GeForce Platform
jobs.description:
We are looking for a Senior Technical Marketing Manager to join our GeForce team — a high-impact role at the intersection of deep technical expertise and essential communication, where you will directly shape how the world understands and experiences our breakthrough gaming platforms.

What you'll be doing:

Lead technical verification initiatives for new GeForce and accelerated computing platforms, synthesizing sophisticated data into clear product launch readiness assessments for engineering, product management, and executive partners

Drive the development of compelling reviewer guides, white papers, and technical content that articulate key use cases, positioning, and value propositions of new GeForce releases to press, analysts, and the enthusiast community

Deliver authoritative product positioning and messaging for partners, VIPs, and press — including live demonstrations of our groundbreaking hardware and software solutions spanning our full product range

Collaborate multi-functionally with engineering, QA, and product teams to influence product direction, surface insights from evaluation cycles, and champion improvements ahead of launch

Mentor junior team members and provide strategic guidance on technical communication standards, demo content development, and messaging consistency across global teams

What we need to see:

Deep knowledge of PC hardware architectures, GPU technologies, and gaming software ecosystems!

Bachelors degree (or equivalent experience) with 8+ years of experience in technical marketing, product management, software QA or a related technical role — preferably within gaming, PC hardware, or semiconductor industry

Outstanding communication and presentation skills; ability to distill complex technical concepts for diverse audiences

Strong analytical skills and hands-on experience synthesizing performance data to inform product decisions

Hands-on use of AI tools — coding assistants, agentic workflows, productivity tools — to accelerate day-to-day work

Working understanding of modern AI systems: neural networks, model inference, transformer architectures, and GPU-accelerated computing

Ability to travel up to 20% for tradeshows and industry events both domestic and international

Ways to stand out from the crowd:

Hands-on experience applying data science to product performance workflows!

Familiarity with real-time rendering techniques, ray tracing, or simulation technologies

Background in game development or experience working with game studios on GPU optimization and performance

Proficiency with Linux environments and command-line tooling relevant to performance testing

Active involvement in PC gaming or enthusiast hardware communities

Your base salary will be determined based on your location, experience, and the pay of employees in similar positions. The base salary range is 148,000 USD - 235,750 USD for Level 4, and 184,000 USD - 287,500 USD for Level 5.

You will also be eligible for equity and benefits.

jobs.title: Life Sciences R&D Product Marketing Manager – Healthcare and Life Sciences
jobs.description:
NVIDIA is redefining the life sciences industry, where AI agents design experiments, robotic labs implement them, and data platforms collect the results to train the next generation of models. We are seeking a Product Marketing Manager to drive awareness and adoption across three vital pillars of our partner ecosystem - Scientific AI Agents, Autonomous Labs/ Manufacturing Facilities, and R&D Data Platforms.

In this role, you will bridge the gap between NVIDIA’s core product teams for agentic AI, physical AI for robotics, and many other industry partners building the future of drug discovery and therapy production. You will convert complex technical capabilities into clear narratives. These narratives will demonstrate how NVIDIA’s accelerated computing and AI stack serves as the operating system for the modern lab.

What you'll be doing:

The ideal candidate is an authority in positioning within this emerging space with a sharp eye for the "big picture." You will be responsible for identifying the most compelling ecosystem stories and translating them into high-quality content that helps our team strengthen NVIDIA’s narrative in the industry.

Ecosystem Activation: Lead go-to-market strategies for three core segments of our partner ecosystem that collaborates with NVIDIA to accelerate how they build Scientific Reasoning Agents, Autonomous Labs and Manufacturing workflows, and pharma R&D Platforms.

In this role, you will bridge the gap between NVIDIA’s core product teams for agentic AI, data processing and model training, and industry partners building the future of financial services. jobs.title: Senior Product Marketing Manager - Data Processing
jobs.description:
NVIDIA has been transforming computer graphics, PC gaming, and accelerated computing for more than 25 years. It’s a unique legacy of innovation that’s fueled by great technology—and amazing people. Today, we’re tapping into the unlimited potential of AI to define the next era of computing. An era in which our GPU acts as the brains of computers, robots, and self-driving cars that can understand the world. Doing what’s never been done before takes vision, innovation, and the world’s best talent. jobs.title: Senior Product Marketing Manager, Cloud Accelerator
jobs.description:
We are looking for a Senior Product Marketing Manager to join the NVIDIA Cloud Accelerator (NCX) team. This role is for a strong technical marketer who understands AI infrastructure and can turn complex platform capabilities into clear positioning, compelling narratives, and high-quality go-to-market execution. NVIDIA Cloud Accelerator isn’t just a product; it’s the blueprint for modern AI infrastructure. It spans the IaaS and PaaS layers, providing the building blocks for provisioning, GPU health, multi-tenancy, and observability. NCX is a portfolio of open-source, modular, composable infrastructure software that helps cloud partners and infrastructure ISVs build, scale, and operate AI factories faster. The portfolio spans IaaS and PaaS layers, with building blocks for provisioning, lifecycle management, GPU health, observability, resiliency, and multi-tenant operations.

NCX is crafted to strengthen partner platforms, not replace them. In this role, you will lead product marketing for key NCX initiatives across infrastructure software, ecosystem enablement, and partner adoption. You will define positioning and messaging, develop launch plans, compose technical and business narratives, and build high-impact assets for NVIDIA Cloud Partners (NCPs), cloud service providers (CSPs), infrastructure ISVs, and internal field teams. You will work closely with product management, engineering, developer relations, business development, PR, analyst relations, campaigns, and sales to bring new infrastructure software and ecosystem stories to market. This is a highly cross-functional role that requires both strategic judgment and hands-on execution. The right candidate can go deep on architecture, understand operator struggles, and translate platform capabilities into clear customer value.

What You’ll Be Doing:

Orchestrate the positioning and narrative for NCX, building value propositions that connect with both technical operators and business leaders.

We will work together to launch new infrastructure software, where you will guide the go-to-market execution and ensure our messaging is consistent across blogs, web content, and partner materials.

Cultivate deep collaborations with NVIDIA Cloud Partners (NCPs), CSPs, and infrastructure ISVs to develop joint stories that highlight how we accelerate time to deployment and operational maturity.

By translating sophisticated GPU clusters and Kubernetes architectures into clear business value, your work will serve as the essential bridge between our engineering breakthroughs and the broader AI ecosystem.

What We Need To See:

10+ years of experience in B2B product marketing, technical marketing, or related roles within cloud/AI infrastructure or platform software.

Bachelor’s degree in a technical or business field, or equivalent experience.

Technical Curiosity: A strong understanding of areas like Kubernetes, GPU clusters, inference platforms, or distributed systems.

Strategic Execution: A proven ability to shape GTM plans and positioning for highly technical platforms.

Communication: Outstanding writing and storytelling skills, with the ability to unite cross-functional stakeholders and executives.

Adaptability: The ability to work proactively, navigate evolving projects, and thrive in a highly collaborative setting.

Ways To Stand Out from the Crowd:

Specialized Insight: Deep familiarity with "AI Factory" infrastructure or cloud-native AI platforms.

Ecosystem Focus: Experience marketing software specifically for service providers or platform operators.

Community Mindset: Understanding of open-source models and ecosystem-led growth.

Learning Orientation: An MBA or an equivalent blend of business and technical growth.

jobs.title: Principal Marketing Manager - Credit Karma
jobs.description:
Overview

Intuit Credit Karma is a mission-driven company, focused on championing financial progress for our more than 140 million members globally. While we're best known for pioneering free credit scores, our members turn to us for everything related to their financial goals, including identity monitoring, applying for credit cards, shopping for insurance and loans (car, home and personal) and savings accounts and checking accounts* – all for free. Credit Karma has grown significantly through the years: we now have more than 1,700 employees across our offices in Oakland, Charlotte, Culver City, San Diego, London, Bangalore, and New York City.

*Banking services provided by MVB Bank, Inc., Member FDIC

We’re looking for a strategic, cross-functional leader to join our marketing organization to drive cross strategy through multiple initiatives across Credit Karma and TurboTax. As the leader of Marketing Strategy & Operations, you’ll partner closely with senior leaders across Marketing, Product, Corporate Strategy, and Analytics to align end-to-end go-to-market plans, lead resourcing and investment strategy, establish performance reporting frameworks, and own executive opmechs.

This role is ideal for someone with a background in management consulting, in-house marketing strategy, and a track record of enabling high-growth product launches. You’ll be equally comfortable writing strategy papers, diving into data, resolving GTM conflicts, and aligning multiple senior leader stakeholders behind a shared narrative.

Responsibilities

Partner with cross-functional teams to build and maintain integrated GTM strategies across high-priority company bets that deliver cohesive member journeys and drive ecosystem growth across TurboTax and Credit Karma
Identify and resolve GTM conflicts across messaging, timing, or resource allocation
Represent Marketing in cross-functional strategic planning forums, including direct contribution to strategy papers and executive business reviews
Develop and maintain a cohesive narrative across all GTM efforts, connecting fragmented workstreams into a single, executive-ready story
Reduce executive burden by owning high-quality inputs for monthly and quarterly business reviews
Define and operationalize a marketing performance framework across strategic and core initiatives in partnership with Finance and Analytics
Lead operating mechanics (e.g. reviews, forums, milestone updates) that keep work aligned and on track across GTM teams
Partner with the Program Management and Operations leader to align execution within strategic programs

Qualifications

10+ years of experience in marketing strategy, business operations, or management consulting
Proven ability to operate at both the strategic and executional level
Executive presence and strong storytelling ability across written and verbal formats
Deep familiarity with go-to-market planning, product launch cycles, and performance reporting
Track record of influencing cross-functional teams and driving alignment across complex, ambiguous environments
Experience working directly with senior executives (CMO, GM, Head of Product, etc.)
Experience in high-growth consumer tech or agency-side marketing strategy preferred
MBA is a plus

Intuit provides a competitive compensation package with a strong pay for performance rewards approach. This position may be eligible for a cash bonus, equity rewards and benefits, in accordance with our applicable plans and programs (see more about our compensation and benefits at Intuit®: Careers | Benefits). Pay offered is based on factors such as job-related knowledge, skills, experience, and work location. To drive ongoing fair pay for employees, Intuit conducts regular comparisons across categories of ethnicity and gender. The expected base pay range for this position is:

Bay Area: $173,000 - $234,000

jobs.description:
Sales

Vertical Product Marketing Manager - Global Business Solutions - San Francisco

:

San Francisco

Responsibilities

About the role and team:
The North America Monetization Product Strategy & Operations team helps ensure that the US advertisers (our clients) are inspired, excited and empowered to partner with us to build their brands and drive outcomes on the TikTok platform. We are the connective tissue between our clients, our sales people, and the product & engineering teams. We are responsible for building verticalized go-to market strategies & activating sales to scale adoption of TikTok's full suite of innovative advertising solutions.

Are you passionate about identifying problems and building solutions that help clients grow their business? TikTok is seeking a Vertical Product Marketing Manager within the Retail vertical to join our North America Monetization Product Strategy & Operations team. This individual will focus on our retail vertical, and on garnering adoption for products & bundles that drive full-funnel revenue for TikTok. They will strategically put shape to ambitious, ambiguous projects that drive revenue and adoption, and be partners with Sales and Product leadership in executing the chosen sub-vertical's go-to-market strategies. They will support and be part of a best-in-class team that has an in-depth understanding of the clients, can aggressively take our current product & solutions sets to market, identify future product opportunities and gaps, and influence the leadership across TikTok.

Responsibilities:
- Plan, define, and lead a Retail go-to-market strategy along with the key sales leadership
- Inclusive of sales enablement, product positioning, and partnering with XFN teams to drive full-funnel plans
- Partner closely with the Sales team to deeply understand user pain points, form business requirements and prioritize solutions to influence the direction of product development
- Lead market understanding, opportunity assessment, analysis, customer feedback, and represent the vertical & market with Product Management and engineering
- Demonstrate a strong understanding of the broader TikTok ecosystem and landscape to develop strategic feedback and positioning.

Qualifications

Minimum Qualifications:
- 5+ years of experience in direct sales, product marketing, strategy or product management with advertising technology products
- 5+ years of significant experience in a specific industry vertical (either as a sales person, or a vertical product marketing manager, or another similar role)
- Experience partnering with and influencing product teams and/or engineering to represent market requirements
- Experience partnering with and influencing sales teams and developing go-to-market strategies.

Preferred Qualifications:
- Preferred experience: full-funnel sales experience with a product lens and eye for technical details
-Preferred experience: excellent organization and strong communication skills at all levels of seniority
- Be revenue-driven and revenue-guided in all recommendations.

Job Information

【For Pay Transparency】Compensation Description (Annually)

The base salary range for this position in the selected city is $121600 - $220400 annually.

​

Compensation may vary outside of this range depending on a number of factors, including a candidate’s qualifications, skills, competencies and experience, and location. Base pay is one part of the Total Package that is provided to compensate and recognize employees for their work, and this role may be eligible for additional discretionary bonuses/incentives, and restricted stock units.

​

Benefits may vary depending on the nature of employment and the country work location. Employees have day one access to medical, dental, and vision insurance, a 401(k) savings plan with company match, paid parental leave, short-term and long-term disability coverage, life insurance, wellbeing benefits, among others. Employees also receive 10 paid holidays per year, 10 paid sick days per year and 17 days of Paid Personal Time (prorated upon hire with increasing accruals by tenure).

​

The Company reserves the right to modify or change these benefits programs at any time, with or without notice.

​

For Los Angeles County (unincorporated) Candidates:

​

Qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records will be considered for employment in accordance with all federal, state, and local laws including the Los Angeles County Fair Chance Ordinance for Employers and the California Fair Chance Act. Our company believes that criminal history may have a direct, adverse and negative relationship on the following job duties, potentially resulting in the withdrawal of the conditional offer of employment:

​

1. Interacting and occasionally having unsupervised contact with internal/external clients and/or colleagues;

​

2. Appropriately handling and managing confidential information including proprietary and trade secret information and access to information technology systems; and

​

3. Exercising sound judgment.

​

About TikTok

TikTok is the leading destination for short-form mobile video. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. TikTok's global headquarters are in Los Angeles and Singapore, and we also have offices in New York City, London, Dublin, Paris, Berlin, Dubai, Jakarta, Seoul, and Tokyo.

​

Why Join Us

Inspiring creativity is at the core of TikTok's mission. Our innovative product is built to help people authentically express themselves, discover and connect – and our global, diverse teams make that possible. Together, we create value for our communities, inspire creativity and bring joy - a mission we work towards every day.

​

We strive to do great things with great people. We lead with curiosity, humility, and a desire to make impact in a rapidly growing tech company. Every challenge is an opportunity to learn and innovate as one team. We're resilient and embrace challenges as they come. By constantly iterating and fostering an "Always Day 1" mindset, we achieve meaningful breakthroughs for ourselves, our company, and our users. When we create and grow together, the possibilities are limitless. Join us.

​

​

Diversity & Inclusion

​

TikTok is committed to creating an inclusive space where employees are valued for their skills, experiences, and unique perspectives. Our platform connects people from across the globe and so does our workplace. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. To achieve that goal, we are committed to celebrating our diverse voices and to creating an environment that reflects the many communities we reach. We are passionate about this and hope you are too.

​

TikTok Accommodation

TikTok is committed to providing reasonable accommodations in our recruitment processes for candidates with disabilities, pregnancy, sincerely held religious beliefs or other reasons protected by applicable laws. If you need assistance or a reasonable accommodation, please reach out to us at

jobs.title: Fintech Marketing Services Manager, Business Development
jobs.description:
About Us
Visa is a world leader in payments technology, facilitating transactions between consumers, merchants, financial institutions and government entities across more than 200 countries and territories, dedicated to uplifting everyone, everywhere by being the best way to pay and be paid.

At Visa, you'll have the opportunity to create impact at scale — tackling meaningful challenges, growing your skills and seeing your contributions impact lives around the world.

Join Visa and do work that matters – to you, to your community, and to the world. Progress starts with you.

Job Description

This is an exciting time to join Visa’s North America Marketing Services team. We are evolving how we partner with fintech clients—moving to a more proactive, results‑driven approach that deepens relationships and delivers measurable impact. Our team leverages Visa’s insights, platforms, and sponsorship assets to help clients build competitive advantage and stronger customer connections.

Our Fintech clients span a wide range of industries, including consumer fintech, fintech issuers, B2B, and remitters. The Marketing Manager reports to the Marketing Director and plays a key role in shaping and executing client marketing strategy.

The Manager leads consultative discovery to identify product and payment marketing opportunities within the Fintech segment, while supporting business development efforts that drive Visa purchase volume, Value‑Added Services revenue, and client brand growth. Marketing engagements may include customer research and insights, marketing strategy, creative development, channel activation (email, paid social, paid display, search, OOH), influencer marketing, sponsorship and event marketing.

This role partners closely with Sales, Product, Visa Consulting & Analytics, Research, Marketing, Legal, Sponsorship and agency teams to deliver meaningful business outcomes for Visa and our clients.

Essential Functions:

Work cross-functionally to support the sell in and seamless implementation of marketing programs that leverage Visa resources and expertise. Key stakeholders include Account Sales Team, Consulting & Analytics, Product Marketing, Research and Insights, Brand, Product, Communications, Digital Media and Legal
Manage internal Visa processes to support the implementation of marketing projects including business development pipeline tracking, project pricing reviews/approvals, agency/client SOWs, vendor invoicing and project revenue recognition.
Support business development team responsible for selling B2B2C, B2B and B2C marketing programs and participate in ideation of marketing programs which may include marketing research & insights, marketing strategy, creative development, channel delivery and Visa sponsorships
Responsible for building partnership relationships with the cross-functional team, including Sales, Product, Marketing and agencies, to deliver best-in-class marketing plans that drive business goals for both Visa and its clients.
Ability to thrive in a fast-moving, highly matrixed environment and be results-oriented, flexible, and possess a “can do”, positive attitude.

This is a hybrid position. Expectation of days in office will be confirmed by your hiring manager.

Visa requires at least 3 days in office, expectations of these days will be confirmed by your Hiring Manager.

Qualifications

Basic Qualifications • 5 or more years of relevant work experience with a Bachelors Degree or at least 2 years of work experience with an Advanced degree (e.g. Masters, MBA, JD, MD) or 0 years of work experience with a PhD Preferred Qualifications • 6 or more years of work experience with a Bachelors Degree or 4 or more years of relevant experience with an Advanced Degree (e.g. Masters, MBA, JD, MD) or up to 3 years of relevant experience with a PhD • General knowledge and basic understanding of marketing principles and campaign management • Experience working in, with and/or managing creative agencies • Experience successfully prioritizing and managing multiple projects/deliverables • Capable of working cross-functionally to ensure project deadlines and budgets are met • Ability to self-manage and support multiple projects, as needed • Employs sound judgment and rationale when problem solving and making decisions • Communicates effectively with internal and external stakeholders • Strong written and oral communication skills • Strong project management skills • Proficient in MS Excel and PowerPoint • 6-8 years of experience in financial services marketing, consumer marketing, payments industry, financial services, and/or consulting with clients that involved launching new brands/products, lead generation, loyalty campaigns, new customer acquisition and growing existing customer portfolio • Experience in digital, social, paid search, influencer, and sponsorship or events marketing • Strong presentation skills, written and in person communication skills, executive presence, building relationship, influence decision makers and translate complex data into actionable insights • Ability and initiative to self-manage when necessary and ability to multitask to support multiple efforts as needed • Team player with experience coordinating cross functional, end-to-end projects that included a high level of detail and ability to ensure project deadlines and budgets are met • Self-motivated and proactive in asking questions, proposing solutions and can communicate clearly to manage various internal and external stakeholders’ expectations • Strong strategic thinker and problem solver focused on results who approaches any challenge with thoughtfulness, drive and appropriate urgency • Extensive knowledge of Microsoft Office Excel, Power Point and Word

U.S. Applicants Only

The estimated salary range for this position is $134,600.00 to $ 215,300.00 USD per year, which may include potential sales incentive payments (if applicable). Salary may vary depending on job-related factors which may include knowledge, skills, experience, and location. In addition, this position may be eligible for bonus and equity.Visa has a comprehensive benefits package for which this position may be eligible that includes Medical, Dental, Vision, 401(k), FSA/HSA, Life Insurance, Paid Time Off, and Wellness Program.

Work Hours

Varies upon the needs of the department.

Travel Requirements

This position requires travel 5-10% of the time.

Mental/Physical Requirements

This position will be performed in an office setting. The position will require the incumbent to sit and stand at a desk, communicate in person and by telephone, frequently operate standard office equipment, such as telephones and computers.

Visa is an EEO Employer

Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, gender identity, disability or protect veteran status. Visa will also consider for employment qualified applicants with criminal histories in a manner consistent with the EEOC guidelines and applicable local law, including the requirements of Article 49 of the San Francisco Police Code.

jobs.title: Website Experience & Growth Manager
jobs.description:
*Role Overview*

We are seeking a hands-on Website Experience and Growth Manager to own and optimize our company’s web presence end-to-end. This role is responsible for ensuring our website delivers an exceptional user experience, drives qualified lead generation, engages prospective partners, and is fully optimized for modern search – including both traditional SEO and Answer Engine Optimization (AEO) across AI-powered search platforms.

You will serve as the central owner of the website, bridging executive leadership and marketing to continuously improve performance, visibility, and conversion.

*Success Metrics*

* Growth in qualified inbound leads from the website
* Growth in engagement of prospective partners
* Improved organic traffic (SEO + AI search visibility)
* Increased conversion rates of high-quality leads on key pages
* Improved engagement metrics (bounce rate, time on site, etc.)
* Faster site performance and strong technical health

*Key Responsibilities*

*1. Website Ownership & Strategy*

* Own the overall strategy, performance, structure, and effectiveness of the company website
* Define and execute a roadmap to improve engagement, conversion rates, and lead generation
* Ensure alignment with brand, messaging, and go-to-market strategy

*2. User Experience (UX) & Conversion Optimization*

* Continuously improve site navigation, layout, and user journeys
* Optimize key pages and conversion paths (forms, demos, CTAs)
* Run A/B tests and experiments to improve conversion rates
* Ensure mobile responsiveness, accessibility, and page performance
* Scale global web experiences with localization in select geographies

*3. Content & Information Architecture*

* Partner with executive leadership and marketing to plan and publish web content
* Own site structure, page hierarchy, and internal linking strategy
* Ensure content is clear, compelling, and aligned to buyer intent
* Maintain consistency across pages, messaging, and calls to action

*4. SEO & Answer Engine Optimization (AEO)*

* Develop and execute SEO strategy (technical, on-page, and content)
* Optimize content for AI-driven search experiences (AI Overviews, Gemini, ChatGPT, Copilot, Perplexity, and Claude)
* Structure content for answerability (FAQs, schema markup, structured data)
* Conduct keyword and intent research to guide content creation
* Monitor rankings, traffic, and search visibility

*5. Technical Website Management*

* Oversee CMS (e.g., WordPress, Webflow, etc.) and ensure content can be easily managed
* Manage site performance, uptime, and page speed optimization
* Coordinate with developers for enhancements, integrations, and fixes
* Maintain SEO technical health (crawlability, indexing, structured data, etc.)
* Ensure proper tracking (Google Analytics, Tag Manager, etc.)

*6. Analytics & Performance Tracking*

* Define and track KPIs (traffic, engagement, conversion rates, leads)
* Build dashboards and regularly report on website performance
* Use data to inform optimization decisions and roadmap priorities

*7. Cross-Functional Collaboration*

* Work closely with executive leadership, marketing, sales, and development teams
* Translate business goals into website improvements
* Manage external vendors or agencies as needed

*Qualifications*

*Required*

* 5+ years managing or optimizing websites in a B2B or SaaS environment
* Strong understanding of AEO and SEO (technical + content)
* Experience with AEO / AI search optimization strategies
* Experience with CMS platforms (WordPress, Webflow, etc.)
* Working knowledge of HTML, CSS, and basic JavaScript (not necessarily a developer)
* Experience with analytics tools (Google Analytics, Search Console, etc.)
* Proven ability to improve conversion rates and drive lead generation

*Preferred*

* Familiarity with schema markup and structured data
* Experience with A/B testing tools (Optimizely, VWO, etc.)
* Background in growth marketing or demand generation
* Experience working cross-functionally in mid-sized companies

Pay: $80,000.00 - $100,000.00 per year

Work Location: In person

jobs.title: Paid Search Marketing Manager (PPC)
jobs.description:
Company and Vision

PlanetArt’s vision is to be the leading seller of personalized and make-on-demand products worldwide. We provide consumers with unmatched tools and content and an unparalleled end-to-end customer experience that result in high-quality, meaningful finished products and memorable celebrations of life events.

The company’s brands include the popular FreePrints and FreePrints Photobooks apps and the industry leading SimplytoImpress card and stationery site, as well as Personal Creations, CafePress and ISeeMe! Visit www.planetart.com to learn more about our brands.

We have more than 500 team members across multiple offices, primarily in Calabasas CA, San Diego CA, Woodridge IL, Minneapolis, MN and Pleasanton, CA. We also have team members in two company-owned offices in China, as well as in Europe.

Life Insurance
