jobs.title: Senior Operator / Director of Operations | Founder-Led Behavioral Health Marketing Agency
jobs.description:
*This is not a corporate operations role.*

We are looking for a senior operator who has built systems inside a large firm or corporate marketing organization and is ready to apply that discipline somewhere faster, leaner, and more direct.

BHP is under two years old. We crossed $1,000,000 in revenue in our first twelve months and are now operating at a run rate approaching $3,000,000. Growth, however, is not the metric we are most proud of. Our clients average 34 percent month-over-month growth in organic traffic, and we executed the most successful treatment center launch in industry history, a 68-bed full continuum facility.

Our founder is on a mission to make treatment accessible to everyone who seeks it, and to make treatment marketing accessible to the centers that serve them. We need an operator who can bring structure without bringing bureaucracy, and help scale this work without diluting the standard that built it.

*About Behavioral Health Partners*

Behavioral Health Partners (BHP) is a digital marketing and SEO agency built exclusively for addiction treatment and mental health treatment programs across the United States and Canada. We exist because the people searching online for help with addiction, mental illness, and trauma are often at the lowest point of their lives, and they deserve to find treatment programs that are real, qualified, and ready to help. We work behind the scenes to make sure those programs show up at the right moment, in the right place, with the right message.

Our mission is simple: help more people access effective, ethical, and life-changing treatment. We believe commercial treatment should be available to all demographics, and we build business models that make that vision a reality.

We live by our core values:

*Live With Integrity.*

*Serve With Excellence.*

*Change The World.*

We run the SEO programs, paid search campaigns, content strategies, and marketing analytics that help addiction treatment and mental health programs reach the families and individuals who need them. Marketing in this space requires deep technical and strategic skill. The keywords are restricted, the regulations are complex, and the stakes are high. We are the agency that does this work properly.

*The Mission of This Role*

*Your mission is to turn founder vision into consistent execution.*

You will own the operating rhythm across client delivery, team accountability, onboarding, communication, internal systems, and follow-through. You will be measured on impact, clarity, and execution. Not activity. Not meeting attendance. Not how busy your calendar looks.

BHP has strong demand, proven results, and real momentum. What we need now is the operational backbone that lets us scale without every decision routing through the founder's desk. That backbone is you.

*What You Will OwnClient Delivery and Accountability*

Run the execution rhythm across SEO, content, web, paid media, attribution, and client success. Every client should have clear owners, deadlines, blockers, and next steps. You are the person who makes sure that is true every week, on every account.

*Onboarding and Fulfillment Systems*

Build simple, repeatable processes for onboarding, fulfillment, communication, reporting, and escalation. New clients should launch cleanly. Active clients should never have to wonder what is happening on their account.

*Team Management*

Hold team members accountable to outcomes, deadlines, and quality standards. Surface underperformance early. Drive clarity around what needs to improve and by when. We have a strong team. They will perform even better with someone who runs a tight, fair, predictable rhythm.

*Founder Translation*

Take fast-moving founder ideas, client direction, and strategic priorities, and convert them into tasks, owners, timelines, and repeatable processes. The founder operates at speed. Your job is not to slow that down. Your job is to translate it into systems the rest of the company can run.

*Systems and Visibility*

Clean up and improve HubSpot, task management, reporting rhythms, handoffs, and internal visibility. Leadership should be able to see what is happening across the business without chasing updates and without inspecting every account manually.

*Hiring Support*

Help build and run hiring processes for key execution roles, particularly attribution, paid media, client delivery, and operations. We are growing. We need to hire well.

*What Success Looks LikePhase 1: Diagnose and Prioritize*

Understand the team, the clients, the delivery flow, the systems, and the biggest operational bottlenecks. Identify the highest-impact fixes that will reduce founder involvement and improve client delivery fastest.

*Phase 2: Install the Operating Rhythm*

Create a simple cadence for client delivery, team accountability, priorities, blockers, and escalations. Everyone should know what they own, what is due, and what needs attention. No one should be guessing.

*Phase 3: Drive Measurable Operational Improvement*

Improve delivery consistency. Reduce dropped balls. Surface issues earlier. Give the founder better visibility without requiring him to inspect every account manually.

*The 90-Day Milestone*

Within ninety days, the business should feel materially more organized. Clients should have clearer ownership and status visibility. The founder should be spending meaningfully less time chasing execution and meaningfully more time on strategy, growth, and the work only he can do.

_If we get to day ninety and that has not happened, this is not the right fit. We will both know it._

*Who You Are*

* *An Execution Owner.* You can run projects, people, deadlines, and client delivery without the founder chasing every detail.
* *A Founder Translator.* You can take fast-moving founder ideas and turn them into tasks, owners, timelines, and repeatable processes.
* *A Client Delivery Operator.* You have owned onboarding, fulfillment, client communication, and retention inside a services or agency environment.
* *A Systems Builder.* You can clean up HubSpot, task management, reporting rhythms, handoffs, and team accountability without needing six months of consulting before you start.
* *A High-Accountability Leader.* You flag issues early. You hold people to standards. You do not need constant follow-up.
* *Calm Under Chaos.* You can step into a messy, fast-growth business and create order without slowing the founder down.

*Nice-to-Have Experience*

* Digital marketing agency operations
* SEO, PPC, lead generation, or client delivery leadership
* Healthcare, behavioral health, or other regulated industries
* HubSpot, ClickUp, Asana, Monday, or comparable tools
* Client onboarding and retention systems
* Managing US-based and offshore team members
* Hiring and interviewing for marketing or operations roles

*Who This Is Not For*

This role is not a fit for someone who:

* Needs a fully built corporate structure to operate inside
* Wants to manage from a distance
* Dislikes ambiguity
* Avoids hard conversations
* Wants to strategize more than execute
* Is uncomfortable holding people accountable
* Is looking for a quiet seat at a polished agency

_If any of the above describes the role you want next, we are not it, and that is genuinely okay._

*Compensation*

*Base Salary: *$125,000 to $165,000, depending on experience.

*Bonus Target: *15 to 30 percent of base, tied to:

* Clients fully onboarded on time with clean handoffs
* Reduction in founder ops and escalation load
* Client retention and delivery consistency
* Team accountability and execution reliability

*Total Target Compensation, Fully Earned: up to $214,500.*

_This is a real bonus, not a discretionary one. The metrics are observable. If you do the work, you earn it._

*Application Questions*

_Honest, specific, story-based answers will move you faster through our process than polished generalities._

* *1. Founder-Led Chaos.* Tell us about a time you stepped into a messy founder-led business and created operating clarity. What was broken, what did you fix first, and what measurable improvement followed?
* *2. First 30 Days.* If you joined BHP and found unclear ownership, inconsistent client delivery, and a founder still pulled into execution, what would you do in your first thirty days?
* *3. Founder Leverage.* Describe a time you materially reduced a founder's day-to-day involvement without slowing the business down. What did you take off their plate, and how did you maintain quality?
* *4. Client Delivery System.* Walk us through how you would manage onboarding, delivery, blockers, accountability, and reporting across a ten-person services team running multiple active client engagements.
* *5. Accountability and Underperformance.* Tell us about a time a team member was not meeting the standard. How did you address it, what changed, and what was the outcome?

*Final Step*

Final candidates may be asked to complete a paid working session reviewing our current team and client delivery structure, then presenting a practical thirty-day operating plan. We pay for that work because your time is worth it, and because the quality of that session tells us more than any interview will.

*A Closing Note*

If you have read this far, you are probably the kind of operator we want to talk to.

The people we serve, the addiction treatment programs, the mental health programs, and underneath them the patients and families who find those programs in their hardest moments, deserve an agency that runs cleanly. That is what this role exists to build.

*If that lands, apply.*

Pay: $125,000.00 - $214,500.00 per year

Benefits:
* 401(k)
* 401(k) matching
* Dental insurance
* Health insurance
* Vision insurance



Application Question(s):
* Tell us exactly why we should consider your application in under 300 characters.

Work Location: Remote

jobs.title: Vice President, Commercial Strategy & Marketing
jobs.description:
Position Overview

Nektar Therapeutics has an exciting opportunity for a Vice President, Commercial Strategy & Marketing.

POSITION SUMMARY

The Vice President, Commercial Strategy and Marketing will lead strategic commercial efforts for a growing late stage biotechnology company with a focused immunology and inflammation pipeline, including its first‑in‑class investigational biologic therapy (rezpegaldesleukin) in Atopic Dermatitis and Alopecia Areata, entering phase 3 clinical trials (with an anticipated first launch in Atopic Dermatitis in 2029/30) and an additional multi‑asset antibody program in earlier stage development. This role is responsible for supporting the ongoing planning and execution of commercial and marketing roadmaps across a variety of initiatives, workstreams and projects. As the Company’s initial brand champion for rezpegaldesleukin, this role will oversee the marketing strategy, branding, launch planning, and market preparation to ensure the successful introduction of this novel, first‑in‑class Treg mechanism in the US first and then guide strategies for subsequent global markets. In addition, the role will be responsible for building a highly functioning marketing team as rezpegaldesleukin proceeds through Phase 3 and BLA filing. As VP, this role requires exceptional leadership, strategic thinking, and a strong background in commercial strategy and brand development within the pharmaceutical or biopharmaceutical industry. Ideally, this person will have experience in the immunology, dermatology, and/or inflammation space, with the ability to work cross‑functionally to understand evolving US and other global market dynamics, and synthesize these insights into an aligned strategic plan. This role reports to the Chief Business Officer.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
Lead development of the market research plan to support launch and measure launch effectiveness.
Work closely with Chief Business Officer and Executive Team to develop a comprehensive commercial and marketing strategy, as well as a pre‑launch plan, for the Company’s lead asset in moderate‑to‑severe Atopic Dermatitis and Alopecia Areata, along with follow‑on indications.
Develop and execute marketing strategies that support the launch of Nektar product(s).
Lead cross‑functional commercial projects and initiatives that will define the go‑to‑market strategy.
Partner with company leadership in the evaluation, assessment, and implementation of the commercial operating model, which includes commercial processes, systems, and capabilities, as well as the organizational design and structure to support commercialization.
Serve as a member of cross‑functional program team workstreams across various portfolio development programs to ensure a commercial perspective is accounted for early and effectively in the plans of Clinical Development, Medical Affairs, and Regulatory functions, and throughout earlier development programs.
Lead the development of the long‑range product operating plan.
Lead the acquisition, storage, and analysis of key secondary data to inform business strategies, tactics, and decisions.
Establish a market and commercial competitive intelligence process to help the organization stay up to date on the latest market changes and competitive dynamics that will shape asset positioning and target product profiles (TPPs) of pipeline candidates.
Assist with life cycle management and label expansion strategy.
Contribute to a strategy for maximizing value with health economics and outcomes research (HEOR), including building cost‑effectiveness models and value dossiers for payors and HTA submissions.
IDEAL CANDIDATE
Bachelor’s degree required; advanced degree (MD, PhD, or other relevant graduate degree) preferred, particularly for candidates with management consulting or strategic advisory experience.
Approximately 10+ years of progressive commercial leadership in life sciences, including 5+ years of strategic consulting experience or commercial strategy experience at a pharmaceutical or biotech company.
Broad experience across relevant commercial roles and functions, including at least one role with primary responsibility for commercial strategy development and execution.
Proven track record of shaping and executing effective marketing brands and/or commercial strategies in rapidly evolving biotech organizations, resulting in meaningful commercial impact.
Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
Advanced skills in Excel and PowerPoint, including the ability to build clear, data‑driven analyses and create executive‑ready presentation decks.
Ability to thrive in a remote work environment by actively engaging and building rapport with colleagues through virtual meetings and collaboration tools.
Position requires up to 30% travel.
ADDITIONAL INFORMATION

Nektar currently anticipates the base salary for the Vice President, Commercial Strategy and Marketing to range from $330,000 to $360,000 for candidates in the Bay Area and will depend, in part, on the successful candidate location and qualifications for the role, including education and experience. This position will also be eligible for an annual performance bonus in accordance with the terms of the applicable plan, depending in part on company and individual performance and at the Company’s discretion. The compensation described above is subject to change and could be higher or lower than the range described based on the market survey data.

Qualifying employees are eligible to participate in benefit programs such as:

Health Insurance (Medical/Dental/Vision)
Disability Insurance
Holiday Pay
Paid Time Off (PTO)
401(k) Match
Employee Stock Purchase Plan
Wellness Programs
Parental Leave Benefits (in accordance with the terms of applicable plans)

For general information on company benefits, please go to https://www.nektar.com/careers.

We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate against applicants due to race, ethnicity, gender, veteran status, or on the basis of disability or any other federal, state or local protected class. Nektar Therapeutics will consider for employment qualified applicants with criminal histories in the manner proscribed by the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance.

jobs.title: Director of Performance Marketing, Lodging
jobs.description:
The opportunity

Delaware North Parks and Resorts is hiring a full-time Director of Performance Marketing, Lodging to join our team based in either the Washington, DC area or Buffalo, New York. As the Director of Performance Marketing, you will be responsible for overseeing digital demand generation and optimization strategies for our lodging business across paid media, paid social, metasearch, OTA placements, and franchiser commercial platforms. If you like to innovate, enact change, and seek growth opportunities, apply now to join our team. We are open to well-qualified candidates on a remote basis from other areas with more extensive travel to our core locations.

Pay

Minimum – Anticipated Maximum Base Salary: $111,900 - $151,000 / year

In addition to base salary, we offer an annual bonus plan based on company and individual performance, or a role-based, uncapped sales incentive plan. The advertised pay range represents what we believe at the time of this job posting, that we would be willing to pay for this position. Only in special circumstances, where a candidate has education, training, or experience that far exceeds the requirements for the position, would we consider paying higher than the stated range. Information on our comprehensive benefits package can be found at https://careers.delawarenorth.com/benefits/.

What we offer

At Delaware North, we care about our team members' personal and professional journeys. These are just some of the benefits we offer:

Medical, dental, and vision insurance
401(k) with up to 4% company match
Annual performance bonus based on level, as well as individual, company, and location performance
Paid vacation days and holidays
Paid parental bonding leave
Tuition and/or professional certification reimbursement
Generous friends-and-family discounts at many of our hotels and resorts

What will you do?
Develop demand and channel strategies that improve RevPAR, occupancy pacing, ADR, loyalty contribution, and channel profitability
Align paid media investments with revenue and distribution strategies to drive efficient, measurable growth
Direct activation and optimization across paid search, PMAX, paid social, programmatic media, OTA merchandising, and chain commercial systems
Guide agency and shared digital teams on targeting, pacing, testing frameworks, and efficiency improvements while maintaining alignment with campaign priorities
Translate channel performance, demand trends, pacing shifts, and audience insights into actionable revenue‑driving decisions and clear communication for stakeholders
Coordinate timing of paid media, promotions, and campaign launches while ensuring cohesion between upper‑funnel brand awareness and lower‑funnel demand tactics
Advance adoption of Marriott and IHG demand systems, identify integration opportunities, track platform innovations, and lead test‑and‑learn roadmaps that improve conversion and direct share

More about you
Bachelor’s degree required; MBA preferred
Minimum of 8 years of digital or performance marketing experience across independent and branded hotel environments
Strong fluency with Marriott and IHG’s commercial ecosystem tools and data platforms such as Ignite, Boost, and Koddi
Proven success driving topline revenue through integrated optimization and digital investment strategy
Experience directing enterprise-level digital resources and agency partners

#LI-Remote

Who we are

Our business is all about people, and that includes you. At Delaware North, you’re not just part of a team — you’re part of a global legacy: a family-owned company with 100+ years of history behind it. Our operations span the world, offering you unique paths to growth and success.

Who says you can’t love where you work? With jobs in iconic sports arenas, stunning national parks, exciting casinos, and more, we pride ourselves on giving the world great times in great places. And whether you’re interested in restaurants, hotels, sports, gaming, operations, or retail, part-time or full-time, we’re invested in helping you achieve your career goals.

Together, we’re shaping the future of hospitality — come grow with us!

Delaware North, along with its subsidiaries, is an equal opportunity employer, showcasing job opportunities and considering applicants for all positions without regard to race, color, religion, sex, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, protected veteran status, sexual orientation, or any other legally protected status.

jobs.title: Senior Demand Generation Strategist (Remote US)
jobs.description:
Directive Consulting is the leading B2B marketing agency for brands that are serious about the metrics that matter most.
With over a decade of expertise and 420+ B2B brands under our belt, we've earned our reputation on real outcomes. We run through 3 divisions: Performance, Communications, and Commerce, all wired to share intelligence and answer to revenue. That system spans everything from Paid Media and Content Marketing, to PR and Paid Social, to Marketplace and Lifecycle Marketing. Tying it all together is our methodology, DiscoverabilityOS, which aligns brand and demand to guide your ICP to choose you across every decision point.

We move fast. We think boldly. We hire people who challenge the status quo, build campaigns worth talking about, and make bold brands impossible to ignore. If you live for performance, love solving hard problems, and want your work to actually mean something, you'll fit right in.

*This role is listed internally as Demand Generation Manager*

About The Role

We are looking to offer a compelling and competitive new demand gen offering for our customers here at Directive. In this role, you will directly work with no more than seven accounts and be directly responsible for their performance marketing.

The ideal candidate for this role is an exceptionally strong T-Shaped Marketer with deep expertise in the SaaS marketplace across Paid, SEO, and CRO. A crucial part of this role will not only be executing campaigns; but also, interfacing with customers, pitching strategy, and ensuring that results are exceeded.

We value your ability to drive client results as an individual contributor and believe that the ideal candidate revels in owning the success of their client portfolio. You will only be successful in this role if you are strong at marketing strategy, execution, communication, and project management.

Roles & Responsibilities

Own the success of SMB clients, across channels

Handle client relationships with poise, confidence, and empathy

Project manage and organize your accounts

Build relationships with your point of contacts

Deep technical knowledge of Strategy, Paid Media, SEO, and CRO for tech companies

Strong SaaS background preferred

Skilled at having a large impact in a very specific role

Creative spark for marketing + deep passion for getting results

What You Offer

2+ years of experience in agency-side marketing

2+ years of experience in performance marketing

Brilliant strategist and a truly T-shaped marketer

World-class project manager who knows how to get things done on time and below cost

Availability to travel

Driven to stay ahead of industry trends, including actively learning how AI and automation can enhance marketing and operations.

What We Offer

 We have a set living wage at Directive; The annual base salary range for this position based in the United States is $80,000- $85,000 USD with potential for bonus eligibility. This salary range is an estimate, and the actual salary may vary based on Directive's compensation practices, job related skills, and depth of experience.

 Medical, dental, vision plans, disability, and life insurance coverage for you and your family that fit your lifestyle

Including a 100% employer-paid plan for you and a 50% employer contribution for your dependents

Benefits to Support the Whole Person:

 Mental - Access to certified therapists through Spring Health, membership to Headspace

 Physical - Gympass

Time Off - Unlimited PTO (2-week minimum), Paid Company Holidays, Your Birthday Off, End of Year Recharge (Closed December 24 - January 1), Paid Parental Leave

Financial - Traditional and Roth 401(k) with a 3% company match

 Bonus - Annual bonus based on tenure, which scales in total amount over time

 Annual Anniversary Trip to Newport Beach, CA with peers and executive leadership for fun and entertainment!

Work Environment Requirements

As a remote-first company, you’ll have the ability to work from anywhere in the US, with the option to enjoy our state-of-the-art offices in Irvine, California. For some positions, as posted, we will accommodate global opportunities where we have established businesses, including Canada, the UK, and Australia. For global locations, you must have established and current work authorization and permanently reside in that country.

This role has the opportunity to operate 100% virtually from your home office. We primarily collaborate with our colleagues through virtual meetings (Zoom), and Slack. In this role, you will be required to operate a laptop computer (PC or Mac available), computer software platforms, and other office productivity tools as necessary. Due to the nature of this role, you must be able to remain stationary for extended periods, must be able to observe and interpret written and/or verbal communication, must have reliable internet access, and a professional background.

To perform this job successfully, an individual must be able to perform each essential job duty satisfactorily. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable qualified individuals with disabilities to perform essential job functions.

If you require reasonable accommodations in completing this application, interviewing, completing any pre-employment testing, or otherwise participating in the employee selection process, direct your inquiries to careers@directiveconsulting.com.

Additional Information

At Directive, one of our core values is People First. We’re committed to fostering a more diverse and inclusive culture in the digital landscape. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status.

jobs.title: Enterprise Director of Digital Marketing-Remote
jobs.description:
About Us

HEI manages an unparalleled portfolio of leading full-service branded hotels and luxury and lifestyle independent hotels across the U.S. We are the acknowledged experts on upper-upscale, luxury, and premium select-serve hotels in urban, super-suburban, and destination locations. At HEI, we recognize that our enormous success is solely the result of our greatest asset: Our People. Where people work is a choice. Against that choice, our greatest obligation is to ensure day in and day out that our associates have the compensation and benefits they deserve and industry leading tools, training and support that they need to excel. At HEI, simply said, we honor the privilege of our associates choosing us by investing in and making them more valuable.

Don't meet every single requirement of this job? At HEI we are dedicated to building a diverse and inclusive workplace, so if you're excited about this role but your experience doesn't align perfectly with every qualification in the job description, we encourage you to still send in your information. You may be the perfect candidate for this or for other roles within our organization!

We value U.S. military experience and invite all qualified military candidates to apply.

Overview

HEI Hotels & Resorts is seeking a collaborative, analytically driven Enterprise Marketing Director to support a portfolio of three to four hotels across multiple markets. This role will serve as the primary marketing partner for each property—developing and executing comprehensive, segment-focused marketing strategies that drive revenue across Transient, Group, Business Travel, F&B, weddings/events, and ancillary outlets.

Working closely with property leadership, sales, and revenue management, this position ensures each hotel’s marketing efforts are strategically aligned, brand-compliant, and optimized for performance. Regular property travel is expected to support commercial planning, photo shoots, site visits, and cross-functional alignment.

Strategic Commercial Marketing

• Build and execute integrated marketing plans that support all revenue segments, including Transient, Group, BT, meetings, weddings, F&B, and experiential offerings.
• Ensure each property’s brand voice, positioning, and content remain competitive and aligned with market demand.

Direct Channel Growth & Digital Performance

• Improve direct booking share through continuous optimization of website, paid media, metasearch, SEO, email/CRM, and social channels.
• Partner with Revenue Management to activate targeted campaigns addressing demand shifts, pace needs, and compression opportunities.

Cross-Functional Collaboration

• Act as a key partner to GMs, DOSMs, revenue teams, and ownership groups; participate in commercial calls, planning sessions, and business reviews.
• Support sales initiatives through segment-specific campaigns, BT visibility strategies, group lead-generation initiatives, and event-driven marketing.

Agency & Vendor Management

• Oversee creative, media, SEO, social, and PR partner execution, ensuring consistency with HEI standards and strong ROI.
• Manage asset development including photography, videography, and brand collateral.

Performance Insights & Reporting

• Monitor KPIs across channels and segments, providing monthly reporting with insights, trends, and actionable optimizations.
• Provide forward-looking recommendations to maximize revenue, enhance visibility, and strengthen competitive positioning.

Qualifications

• 4–5+ years of progressive marketing experience, preferably in hospitality, travel, or multi-unit consumer brands
• Strong understanding of digital marketing, brand strategy, and hotel commercial performance (Transient, Group, BT)
• Ability to manage multiple properties and priorities in a fast-paced environment
• Highly analytical with experience in performance tracking and data-driven decision making
• Exceptional communication and project management skills
• Familiarity with hotel CRMs, booking engines, metasearch, and brand systems is a plus
• Comfortable with regular travel (approximately 25–40%)

Compensation

Salary Range: $115,000.00 - $125,000.00 Annually

Tipped/Service Charge Eligible? No

Discretionary Performance Bonus Eligible? Yes

Benefits

HEI Hotels and Resorts is committed to providing a comprehensive benefit program that offers you choices for your physical, mental and financial wellness, creating value in your most important investment - you!

For your physical and mental wellness we offer competitive Medical and Dental programs through Anthem Blue Cross Blue Shield as well as Vision insurance programs through EyeMed. Our Vacation, Sick and Holiday programs are available for you to rejuvenate with time off. HEI also provides pet insurance through the ASPCA. For your financial wellness, HEI provides a wide array of coverage, including Supplemental, Spousal and Child Life insurance as well as Short and Long-Term Disability plans. Our 401(k) Savings Plan with matching funds, and discounts through our 'YouDecide' and Hotel Room Discount programs provide additional incentives for choosing HEI as the employer of your future. Specific details and eligibility of these programs vary by location and employment status.

HEI Hotels and Resorts is an equal opportunity employer. We evaluate qualified applicants without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, veteran status, and other legally protected characteristics.

Why Work at Lenovo
We are Lenovo. We do what we say. We own what we do. We WOW our customers.

Lenovo is a US$69 billion revenue global technology powerhouse, ranked #196 in the Fortune Global 500, and serving millions of customers every day in 180 markets. Focused on a bold vision to deliver Smarter Technology for All, Lenovo has built on its success as the world’s largest PC company with a full-stack portfolio of AI-enabled, AI-ready, and AI-optimized devices (PCs, workstations, smartphones, tablets), infrastructure (server, storage, edge, high performance computing and software defined infrastructure), software, solutions, and services. Lenovo’s continued investment in world-changing innovation is building a more equitable, trustworthy, and smarter future for everyone, everywhere. Lenovo is listed on the Hong Kong stock exchange under Lenovo Group Limited (HKSE: 992) (ADR: LNVGY).

This transformation together with Lenovo’s world-changing innovation is building a more inclusive, trustworthy, and smarter future for everyone, everywhere. To find out more visit www.lenovo.com, and read about the latest news via our StoryHub.
Description and Requirements

About Lenovo

Focused on a bold vision to deliver smarter technology for all, we are developing world-changing technologies that create a more inclusive, trustworthy, and sustainable digital society. By designing, engineering, and building the world’s most complete portfolio of smart devices and infrastructure, we are also leading an Intelligent Transformation – to create better experiences and opportunities for millions of customers around the world. Join us in defining our world of tomorrow and creating smarter technology for all!

About the Position

Lenovo is seeking a Marketing Director to lead marketing strategy for the International Sales Organization (ISO). This role will define and drive the end-to-end marketing strategy for Lenovo’s cross-business group solutions, enabling sales growth and advancing Lenovo’s transformation to a solutions-led company. As a strategic leader, you will own the holistic marketing plan across strategy, messaging, demand generation, sales enablement, and lead management, ensuring alignment between Lenovo’s global portfolio strategy and ISO sales priorities. This position is remote with a 10% traveling expectation.

Responsibilities

Marketing Strategy & Transformation

Define and lead the global marketing strategy for ISO, aligning messaging, demand generation, and sales enablement to support Lenovo’s cross-business group solutions and growth priorities.
Drive marketing initiatives that support Lenovo’s ongoing transformation toward solutions-led selling and integrated portfolio value.

Customer Journey & Demand Generation

Develop strategies that influence the full customer buying journey, using insights and analytics to identify propensity to buy and optimize demand generation programs.
Establish performance frameworks that connect marketing investments to pipeline creation, conversion, and revenue impact.

Sales Enablement & Lead Lifecycle

Lead development of solutions sales enablement that equips sellers to articulate Lenovo’s differentiated value and accelerate customer engagement.
Own the lead management strategy and process, ensuring alignment between marketing and sales to improve lead quality, pipeline visibility, and conversion.

Global Collaboration & Leadership

Partner with business groups, geo marketing leaders, and ISO sales leadership to deliver integrated marketing programs at global scale.
Lead through influence in a highly matrixed environment, mobilizing cross-functional teams and shared resources to deliver business outcomes.
Identify gaps and advocate for incremental investment, building business cases to scale successful programs and capabilities.
Build strong cross-organizational relationships to drive alignment, execution, and measurable business impact.

Basic Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Business, Strategy, or related field; MBA preferred
10+ years of global experience leading marketing, strategy, demand generation, or sales enablement within a technology organization
Demonstrated experience leading end-to-end marketing strategies that drive measurable pipeline and revenue impact
Strong understanding of B2B marketing, customer buying journeys, and demand generation models

Preferred Qualifications

15+ years of global experience leading marketing, strategy, demand generation, or sales enablement within a technology organization
Proven ability to influence and collaborate across multiple business groups, geographies, and executive stakeholders
Experience managing complex cross-functional initiatives and marketing programs at scale
Exceptional ability to operate with an entrepreneurial mindset, driving impact in resource-constrained environments through influence, prioritization, and creative problem-solving
Exceptional analytical, strategic thinking, and problem-solving skills
High degree of ownership, business acumen, and ability to operate effectively in complex global organizations
Outstanding executive communication, stakeholder management, and leadership capabilities
Ability to visualize, craft and articulate complex concepts into actionable plans
Ability to present to large groups and Executives utilizing MS Office (PowerPoint)
Strong business acumen with the ability to connect strategy to execution
Ability to build and manage relationships with internal and external partners
Strong written and spoken presence
Ability to grow and manage global teams across B2B marketing, strategy, demand generation, or sales enablement
Cultural Competency working across Chinese, American, Desi, European, and South American cultural contexts, ensuring messages are communicated with clarity and tact
Advanced Degree preferred (MBA)

The base salary budgeted range for this position is $180-200k. Individuals may also be considered for bonus and/or commission. This position is remote with 10% travel.

Lenovo’s various benefits can be found on www.lenovobenefits.com.

In compliance with Colorado's EPEWA, the expected application deadline for this position is June 2, 2025. This applies to both external and internal candidates.

Description and Requirements

About the Position

Lenovo’s IDG (Intelligent Devices Group) Global Marketing team is hiring a Director of Worldwide Strategy & Planning. This leadership position is responsible for defining and driving Lenovo IDG’s global marketing strategy and leading annual and multi-year planning for the PC business. Operating at the intersection of global, geo-level, and business unit teams, this role ensures alignment, investment rigor, and measurable business impact.

A key focus of this position is its role in IDG Marketing’s multi-year AI transformation agenda. You will contribute to strategy, alignment, and impact assessment of key initiatives across the organization (segment marketing, PR & Comms, events, Media COE), in partnership with central teams.

This position is remote with EST working hours. Our ideal candidate brings strong strategy and planning experience, excellent communication skills, and the ability to drive results in a fast-paced, matrixed environment.

Key Responsibilities

Own the development and evolution of the IDG global marketing strategy, building and operationalizing a strategic framework (objectives, goals, strategies, and measures) that connects business priorities to focused marketing pillars, and influencing adoption across global and geo stakeholders
Lead the annual planning process across the IDG global Marketing organization in partnership with geo marketing teams, business units, and corporate marketing to ensure alignment
Work closely with the global marketing teams to ensure plans align to and support the global marketing transformation priorities
Establish ROI framework and ensure consistent ROI measurement across marketing initiatives to ensure performance is measured, tracked, and used as input to create a closed loop planning process
Establish principles for alignment of marketing investment with business priorities and marketing priorities across organizations (Global & Geo)
Partner with research and analytics teams to embed insights into strategy setting and on-going planning
Support leadership meetings by developing clear, compelling presentations that synthesize customer, competitive and marketing intelligence
Steward brand positioning and architecture across products and segments to ensure consistency of messaging and value propositions across touchpoints
Apply leadership skills to demonstrate initiative, effect change, and drive progress in this transformational period to support the overall IDG marketing imperatives

Basic Qualifications

10+ years of Experience leading strategic marketing plans in technology or relevant industries
Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Business, Strategy or related field

15+ years of Experience leading strategic marketing plans in technology or relevant industries
10+ years of Experience leading global and cross-functional teams across multiple teams, geographies and stakeholder levels
7+ years of Agency or Consulting industry experience
Desire to collaborate across organizational boundaries and demonstrate partnership
Proven track record for leading through influence
Strong understanding of technology trends and marketing best practices in PC sector
Strong sense of ownership, transparency and accountability
Strong sense of team success, including recognition
Strong people management skills
Strong written and spoken presence
Cultural Competency working across Chinese, American, Desi, European, and South American cultural contexts, ensuring messages are communicated with clarity and tact

jobs.title: Marketing Technology Manager II, Brand
jobs.description:
About the Team

Our team supports brand marketing globally, architecting innovative strategies and leading high-impact activations across our paid digital channels. Our strategic vision is built on a foundation of data-driven testing, allowing us to refine our marketing portfolio for both long-term brand building and immediate acquisition goals. We are looking for an individual who can translate high-level strategy into flawless execution-someone with a sharp analytical mind, a forward-thinking outlook, and the project management skills to lead complex initiatives.

About the Role

The Marketing Technology Manager will work across Uber's global brand marketing campaigns, transforming data into strategy across our key digital platforms. This role requires a technically proficient and strategic leader who can effectively collaborate with Global Media, agency, and analytics teams to build, optimize and scale campaigns. The ideal candidate is a hybrid performance marketer and brand strategist-a strategic thinker who is passionate about the intersection of data, technology, and brand-building. You thrive in a fast-paced, global environment and are driven to constantly test, learn, and automate for greater impact.

What You'll Do

Lead full-cycle execution of global brand and direct response paid digital campaigns across Facebook, Snap, TikTok, Google ads and other social/programmatic channels.
Translate business goals into marketing strategies, using experimentation frameworks and forecasting models to influence investment decisions and drive marketing KPIs.
Craft outstanding brand marketing campaigns with our media team, involving all the latest capabilities developed by advertising platforms (AR, lenses, commercials, videos, and more).
Serve as a subject matter expert on mobile attribution, measurement, pixel implementation-ensuring Uber remains ahead of industry and platform changes.
Determine areas of opportunity, build and implement experiments and present data-driven recommendations that improve current marketing and business practices in order to drive customer acquisition, engagement, and retention.
Basic Qualifications
7+ years hands-on experience with tools such as Ads Manager, Smartly, MMPs (e.g., Appsflyer, Branch).
Bachelor's degree
Preferred Qualifications
Experience managing 8- to 9-figure annual budgets across multiple platforms and geographies.
Strong planning and organizational skills; able to juggle multiple priorities with attention to detail.
Advanced proficiency in Excel / Google Sheets.
Background in strategic account management and developing media plans.
Excellent project management skills with the ability to manage stakeholders and stay within deadlines.
Strong analytical skills (Excel, SQL a plus) with multivariate testing experience.
Understanding of ad-tech, including integration, tracking, both pixel and mobile app.
Ability to adapt to quick changes within both the company and industry.

For New York, NY-based roles: The base salary range for this role is USD$169,000 per year - USD$188,000 per year. For San Francisco, CA-based roles: The base salary range for this role is USD$169,000 per year - USD$188,000 per year. For all US locations, you will be eligible to participate in Uber's bonus program, and may be offered an equity award & other types of comp. All full-time employees are eligible to participate in a 401(k) plan. You will also be eligible for various benefits. More details can be found at the following link https://jobs.uber.com/en/benefits.

jobs.title: Customer Growth Strategy Manager | Communications, Media, and Technology
jobs.description:
We Are
Accenture Song accelerates growth and value for our clients through sustained customer relevance. We bring together strategy, creativity, and technology to help organizations imagine and deliver growth across brand, business, product, marketing, commerce, and service. With deep industry expertise and strong client partnerships, we help Communications, Media and Technology (CMT) leaders design and execute growth strategies that solve real customer needs and deliver measurable outcomes.

You Are
A growth-minded strategist with experience helping clients translate bold visions into executional reality. You are passionate about solving customer problems in ways that drive business impact, whether through brand, product, marketing, commerce, or service levers. You thrive at the intersection of strategy and execution, comfortable designing a growth strategy, but equally strong at guiding clients through the operating models, processes, and technologies that bring it to life. You lead teams to deliver high-quality outcomes while staying close to the work and the client.

You proactively identify opportunities within client engagements, contributing to proposal development, and helping shape compelling narratives that expand the scope and impact of our work, partnering closely with senior leaders to grow accounts, while developing your own point of view on growth topics and bringing ideas forward that spark new client conversations.

The Work
As a Manager in Customer Growth Strategy, you will:

Partner with clients to define holistic growth strategies that span brand, business, product, marketing, commerce, and service.
Lead cross-functional teams in identifying high-value opportunities through customer insight, market analysis, and competitive scanning.
Translate growth visions into executable roadmaps and operating models aligned to customer needs.
Apply proprietary, best-in-class protocols to rapidly test and validate concepts with real and synthetic customer input.
Build compelling business cases and track the value realization of growth strategies, regardless of the lever (brand, digital, product, experience, etc.).
Serve as a trusted partner to client executives by providing clear insights, pragmatic solutions, and thought leadership on growth and customer strategy.
Manage and coach team members to strengthen their strategic and client engagement skills.
Collaborate across Accenture Song to bring together creativity, technology, and intelligence to deliver end-to-end solutions for clients.

Basic Qualifications

6+ years of relevant experience in growth strategy, customer strategy, or related consulting/advisory roles.
Proven experience executing strategies that drive measurable business outcomes in the CMT industry (e.g., revenue growth, improved customer engagement, brand impact, cost efficiency).
Strong problem-solving skills, with the ability to identify insights, structure challenges, and recommend actionable solutions.
Demonstrated ability to lead workstreams and teams in client-facing environments.

Preferred Qualifications

MBA or advanced degree in innovation, strategy, or related field a plus.
Strategic thinker who can clearly communicate ideas in simple, compelling ways.
Deep CMT industry experience (telecoms, media, software & platforms) with knowledge of current and emerging trends.
Comfort with data-led strategy, value realization, and business case development.
Experience designing and implementing organizational or customer-facing transformations.
Ability to build long-term, trusted client relationships.
Experience facilitating workshops and collaborating with multidisciplinary teams (strategy, design, tech, marketing).

What We Offer
At Accenture Song, you’ll join a global collective of strategists, creatives, technologists, and industry experts. We offer continuous learning, diverse career experiences, and the opportunity to shape the future of CMT by delivering customer relevance and growth at scale.

Compensation at Accenture varies depending on a wide array of factors, which may include but are not limited to the specific office location, role, skill set, and level of experience. jobs.title: Staff Marketing Event Strategist (Mid-Market)
jobs.description:
Overview

About the Team:

To predict the future, we must create it—and at Intuit, that’s exactly what we’re doing.

The CEO Transformation Office is a newly formed, high-impact organization reporting directly to the CEO. Its mission is to mobilize the company around what matters most, shape the market, and accelerate Intuit’s transformation into the world’s leading global financial platform and system of intelligence.

This team sits at the intersection of strategy, storytelling, creativity, and execution. We bring together communications, creative, narrative development, and flagship events and experiences under one unified organization to ensure Intuit shows up with clarity, consistency, and conviction—internally and externally. From customers and prospects to media and investors, we shape how Intuit’s vision comes to life in the world.

At the heart of our work is a bold future: surrounding customers with a virtual team of AI agents and human intelligence that does the work for them and powers their prosperity. To deliver on that promise, we operate with a high say/do ratio, a bias for action, and an uncompromising focus on impact. We don’t just tell the story—we help drive the outcomes.

Joining the CEO Transformation Office means working on the company’s most visible, mission-critical initiatives, partnering with senior leaders across the business, and helping define how Intuit’s strategy is experienced by the world. This is a team for builders, storytellers, and operators who want to shape the future—not react to it.

The opportunity

Intuit is entering a pivotal new chapter with the formation of our Mid-Market Group, unlocking a ~$185B global Total Addressable Market and positioning Intuit as a critical partner for larger, more complex businesses. Winning in this space requires more than execution excellence—it requires strategic experiences intentionally designed to advance business priorities, shape market perception, and accelerate go-to-market outcomes.

The Event Strategist, Mid-Market Experiences operates at the intersection of experience strategy and business strategy. This role translates mid-market insights and GTM priorities into experience strategies that are both creatively compelling and commercially impactful.

Partnering closely with the Events Manager, Mid-Market and cross-functional GTM leaders, you will ensure experiences are not only intentionally designed, but clearly anchored to business intent—driving adoption, pipeline, expansion, and long-term customer value.

Responsibilities

What you’ll do

Connect Business Strategy to Experience Strategy

Partner with Mid-Market GTM, Sales, Product, and Marketing leaders to understand growth priorities, success metrics, and customer motions.

Translate business objectives into clear experience strategies that define the role experiences play in driving awareness, consideration, adoption, and expansion.

Ensure every experience has a defined strategic purpose, audience, and measurable outcome.

Lead with Insight and Design Thinking

Develop a deep understanding of mid-market customers, their operating realities, and decision-making dynamics.

Use research, synthesis, and design thinking methodologies to uncover insights that inform experience design.

Co-Create Experiences That Drive Business Impact

Help shape end-to-end experiences—not just events—across the mid-market portfolio, including flagship moments, executive engagements, field programs, ABM activations, partner experiences, and industry events.

Partner tightly with the event producers to ensure strategic intent carries from concept through execution.

Activate Storytelling in Service of GTM

Define how Intuit’s mid-market strategy and value proposition come to life through storytelling, demos, and immersive moments.

Ensure experiences reinforce Intuit’s market POV and support how mid-market buyers evaluate and adopt solutions.

Measure, Learn, and Evolve

Use data and qualitative feedback to refine experience strategies over time.

Champion experimentation and continuous improvement across formats, technologies, and engagement models.

Qualifications

What you bring

At least 12-15 years experience in design-thinking, strategy, product storytelling, immersive experience design, or related fields.

Embraces design thinking as a way of working, not just a process. Demonstrated track record of scrappy prototyping, piloting, and experimenting to get to the right audience experience.

Strong grounding in design thinking, research, synthesis, and facilitation as tools for solving business problems.

Proven ability to translate business goals into experience strategies that drive measurable outcomes.

Experience designing experiences in service of B2B GTM motions (pipeline growth, deal acceleration, adoption, partner enablement).

Experience working with or marketing to mid-market businesses is a strong plus.

Exceptional storytelling and communication skills, including comfort influencing senior leaders.

Experiences are one of Intuit’s most powerful levers to align teams, influence markets, and accelerate growth in the Mid-Market space.

As the Event Strategist for Mid-Market Experiences, you will define how design strategy and business strategy come together to create experiences that are purposeful, differentiated, and outcome-driven. You will help shape how Intuit’s strategy is understood, experienced, and acted upon—by customers, partners, and the market.

Intuit provides a competitive compensation package with a strong pay for performance rewards approach. This position will be eligible for a cash bonus, equity rewards and benefits, in accordance with our applicable plans and programs (see more about our compensation and benefits at [1] Intuit®: Careers | Benefits). Pay offered is based on factors such as job-related knowledge, skills, experience, and work location. To drive ongoing fair pay for employees, Intuit conducts regular comparisons across categories of ethnicity and gender. The expected base pay range for this position is: Bay Area California $ 169,000- 229,000 References Visible links 1. https://www.intuit.com/careers/benefits/full-time-employees/ Mountain View $169000 - $229000
Mountain View, CA $169000- $229000
San Francisco Howard Street, CA $169000- $229000

jobs.title: Director, Digital Marketing
jobs.description:
Abbott is a global healthcare leader that helps people live more fully at all stages of life. Our portfolio of life-changing technologies spans the spectrum of healthcare, with leading businesses and products in diagnostics, medical devices, nutritionals and branded generic medicines. Our 115,000 colleagues serve people in more than 160 countries.

About Abbott

Abbott is a global healthcare leader, creating breakthrough science to improve people’s health. We’re always looking towards the future, anticipating changes in medical science and technology.

Working at Abbott

At Abbott, you can do work that matters, grow, and learn, care for yourself and your family, be your true self, and live a full life. You’ll also have access to:

Career development with an international company where you can grow the career you dream of.

Employees can qualify for free medical coverage in our Health Investment Plan (HIP) PPO medical plan in the next calendar year.

An excellent retirement savings plan with a high employer contribution

Tuition reimbursement, the Freedom 2 Save student debt program, and FreeU education benefit - an affordable and convenient path to getting a bachelor’s degree.

A company recognized as a great place to work in dozens of countries worldwide and named one of the most admired companies in the world by Fortune.

A company that is recognized as one of the best big companies to work for as well as the best place to work for diversity, working mothers, female executives, and scientists.

The Opportunity

We are seeking an experienced Digital Marketing Communications Director to manage a high‑performing team and drive global digital marketing content strategy across our digital marketing channels. This role combines hands‑on leadership with strong digital marketing expertise to ensure cohesive, compelling, and measurable communications that support business goals. He / she will also disseminate and champion best practices and optimization of digital marketing strategies and tactics as tools evolve, particularly gen-AI-based tools and platforms.

What You’ll Work On

Team Leadership & Talent Development

Lead, coach, and develop a team of digital marketing communications professionals
Set clear priorities, provide actionable feedback, and ensure high‑quality, on‑time delivery of digital marketing content as global baselines
Foster collaboration, accountability, and continuous skill development across the team
Partner with resource managers and leadership on capacity planning and hiring needs

Digital Strategy & Planning

Own the global digital marketing communications content strategy and calendar across priority channels (e.g. web, email, paid media, social, customer relationship and campaign platforms), including digital customer journeys
Translate business objectives into integrated digital marketing plans and campaign roadmaps
Ensure messaging and experiences are optimized for audience, channel, and journey stage and are compliant, as applicable
Champion a data‑driven mindset, using performance insights to refine strategy and global baselines
Serve as a trusted advisor to Commercial teams on digital best practices and performance
Influence Commercial team execution of digital marketing content to ensure alignment to business and customer needs
Align digital marketing execution with brand, PR, and other global marketing communications teams

Emerging Tools & GenAI Optimization (Add These Bullets)

Leverage emerging digital marketing tools, including genAI‑based platforms, to optimize content creation, campaign optimization, personalization, and operational efficiency across global markets
Partner with analytics, marketing technology, and other applicable teams to evaluate, pilot, and scale AI‑enabled solutions that enhance speed to market while maintaining brand quality, governance, and compliance
Champion a test‑and‑learn approach to automation, AI‑assisted insights, and workflow optimization to continuously improve digital performance and team productivity at scale

Performance, Optimization & Governance

Define success metrics and KPIs for digital marketing efforts
Establish and maintain best practices, guidelines, and workflows for digital execution
Utilize a learning mindset for new practices, tools, and platforms within the digital marketing space

Experience You'll Bring

Required Qualifications

Forward Deployed Strategist

About the Company

This venture-backed AI company is building a new layer of human-computer interaction — combining advanced AI with the reliability of traditional software. The platform enables enterprises to automate complex, high-stakes workflows using an English-based programming model that blends structured logic with natural language.

The result: processes that once took hours can now run in minutes with exceptional accuracy. Today, the platform powers mission-critical operations across Fortune 500 organizations with consistently high production quality at global scale.

The Role

As a Forward Deployed Strategist, you’ll work directly with some of the world’s largest enterprises to implement automation that fundamentally changes how teams operate. This is not incremental improvement — you’ll help replace legacy workflows that organizations have relied on for decades.

You’ll operate at the intersection of strategy, operations, and customer leadership, owning deployments end-to-end — from early pilots through global rollouts. This role requires navigating complex organizations, aligning stakeholders from operational teams to executive leadership, and ensuring successful adoption at scale.

If you enjoy solving ambiguous problems, building trusted relationships inside major organizations, and delivering real operational transformation, this role offers a rare opportunity to help define how modern enterprises run.

Why This Role

jobs.title: Senior Brand Strategist
jobs.description:
Overview

Microsoft AI is the superintelligence lab at Microsoft — a global network of researchers, engineers, scientists, and creative minds dedicated to advancing the science of artificial intelligence. We build and scale foundational AI models and push the research frontiers that power breakthrough capabilities across Microsoft's portfolio. Guided by our Humanist AI values, everything we do is oriented toward earning trust and amplifying human potential

As a Brand Strategist in the MAI Creative Studio, you will be working alongside the Brand Strategy Lead (your manager) and partner with Creative, Design and Product Leaders to build communications planning strategies that bring Microsoft AI's research and scientific mission to life — translating complex, highly-technical decisions into coherent stories that build cultural relevance and lasting brand equity. You will sit at the intersection of research, engineering, brand strategy, product marketing, and creative expression to ensure MAI's scientific story is told with clarity, consistency, and high creative ambition.

This is an IC-level role, with 75% of time dedicated to Microsoft AI brand & communications efforts and 25% supporting Copilot brand and product marketing.

Mission And Culture

Microsoft's mission is to empower every person and every organization on the planet to achieve more.

As employees, we come together with a growth mindset, innovate to empower others, and collaborate to realize our shared goals. Each day we build on our values of respect, integrity, and accountability to create a culture of inclusion where everyone can thrive at work and beyond.

Responsibilities

MAI Brand Strategy, Portfolio Narrative & IP Management

Partner with key stakeholders across MAI to lead or contribute to building the foundational Microsoft AI brand strategy and expression, with a specific focus on how MAI's research and scientific, humanist identity that differentiates against other labs.
Steward relationships across AI product leads, marketing, and MAI Creative Studio to create a coherent portfolio narrative of MAI research and scientific initiatives that fit together within a larger story.
Lead or contribute to workshops and training to educate teams on Microsoft AI's brand strategy and expression.

Cultural Planning & Activation

Partner with MAI product leads and MAI brand leads - Writers, Designers, and Project Management - to translate MAI's research and scientific portfolio of initiatives into semi-annual cultural calendars for MAI brand moment campaigns based on product roadmap.
Partner with researchers, scientists, and product leads to translate technical milestones into audience-appropriate narratives that drive understanding, trust, and cultural relevance.
Proactively anticipate major research moments and model launches, building communications plans that sequence messaging across internal and external channels.
Serve as a connective tissue between MAI Creative Studio, product marketing, and research teams — ensuring brand voice, scientific credibility, and creative expression are never in conflict.
Guide brief writing, provide direction to cross-group teams, and develop strategies to engage cross-company stakeholders.

Cultural Insights

Partner with UXR and Research & Insights teams to track brand perceptions and reporting through various types of studies (e.g., qualitative, quantitative) to support broader MAI strategy and initiatives.
Partner with internal teams and commission studies with agencies to support research and insight identification as needed.
Monitor brand performance and brand impact, and look for emerging opportunities across the company and portfolio.

Naming

In partnership with MAI product teams (e.g., Product Marketing, Engineering), lead or support naming efforts of Models, Research, and other work across the Microsoft AI portfolio.
Direct naming efforts and naming rhythm of business, as needed.
Drive approvals with senior management and work with external partners to support the naming process.

Qualifications

Required/Minimum Qualifications

6+ years work experience at a branding agency or with product marketing, brand building, and/or strategy development OR equivalent experience

Additional Or Preferred Qualifications

6+ years brand/creative strategy and communications/connections strategy experience, which could include brief writing and/or managing strategy on the corporate or agency side.
6+ years of communications/connections planning experience building integrated campaigns that bring brands into culture.
Strong insight into the AI discourse, tensions and implications across various parts of culture (media, arts, etc.)
Genuine technical fluency in AI/ML: you can read a research paper, follow a model architecture conversation, and know enough to ask the right questions that inform how we go to market.
Comfort operating in ambiguity: this role will define its own shape, and that's a feature, not a bug.
Luxury, lifestyle, or entertainment experience preferred.
6+ years experience managing projects (e.g., internal stakeholder management, timelines, prioritization, road mapping).
A strategic mindset, able to balance short- and long-term goals, and to translate business objectives into brand solutions.
Effective communication and presentation skills, able to articulate and justify ideas and recommendations with clarity and confidence.
A collaborative and inclusive work style, able to work effectively with diverse teams and stakeholders across different levels and functions.
A customer-centric and empathetic approach, able to understand and anticipate the needs and preferences of target audiences.
A growth mindset and a curiosity for learning, willing to experiment, iterate, and adapt to changing environments and feedback.

Brand IC4 - The typical base pay range for this role across the U.S. is USD $106,400 - $203,600 per year. There is a different range applicable to specific work locations, within the San Francisco Bay area and New York City metropolitan area, and the base pay range for this role in those locations is USD $137,600 - $222,600 per year.

Certain roles may be eligible for benefits and other compensation. Find additional benefits and pay information here:
https://careers.microsoft.com/us/en/us-corporate-pay

This position will be open for a minimum of 5 days, with applications accepted on an ongoing basis until the position is filled.

jobs.title: Product Marketing Manager, NVIDIA NIM
jobs.description:
As a Product Marketing Manager for NVIDIA NIM, you will play a pivotal role in helping enterprises deploy AI. NVIDIA NIM is revolutionizing AI from experimentation to production, packaging optimized, production-ready models for rapid deployment. Our product marketing team operates at the intersection of developer tooling and enterprise go-to-market, ensuring that our narrative resonates with ML engineers and CTOs alike. If you relish diving into technical depth and translating it into compelling stories, this is the perfect opportunity!

What you will be doing:

Launching products: You'll build and manage launch plans for high-visibility NIM releases, coordinating assets, timelines, keynote slides, demos, and press materials while driving cross-functional execution with product management, engineering, technical marketing, and PR or equivalent experience.

You'll align the team on messaging and positioning for NIM across developer and enterprise audiences. You will develop messaging docs, sales enablement materials, customer presentations, solution overviews, and web content.

Driving awareness: You'll identify target audiences and content gaps, then build the assets that fill them — blogs, webinars, demos, solution briefs, and more.

Crafting the ecosystem story: You'll drive co-marketing engagements with NVIDIA's model providers, cloud partners, and ISV ecosystem to showcase the full range of possibilities with NIM.

Collaborating with PR: You'll work with PR on press launches to ensure the NIM story is accurate, compelling, and consistent across every channel.

What we need to see:

Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with a proven track record of articulating technical value to both developer and executive audiences.

College degree or equivalent experience.

More than 8 years proven track record in product marketing, product management, or a similar role within a technology company.

Highly independent — you manage your own priorities, drive cross-functional alignment, and don't need tactical oversight to complete.

Ability to collaborate across functions, influence without authority, and drive decisions in a dynamic environment.

An entrepreneurial approach: you adapt when priorities shift, you learn continuously, and you make things happen.

Writing samples of public-facing technical content (required with application).

A strong mix of technical and business skills — equally comfortable in a conversation with a platform engineer and a VP of Engineering.

Ways to Stand Out from the Crowd:

Direct experience with AI inference, model deployment, or developer-facing AI platforms.

Experience building launch plans for sophisticated, high-visibility products — including keynote assets, press launches, and demos.

Experience going deep enough on a technical product to understand its strengths and weaknesses — and translate that into clear positioning for a developer audience.

Widely considered to be one of the technology world’s most desirable employers, NVIDIA offers highly competitive salaries and a comprehensive benefits package. As you plan your future, see what we can offer to you and your family ! www.nvidiabenefits.com/

Your base salary will be determined based on your location, experience, and the pay of employees in similar positions. The base salary range is 152,000 USD - 230,000 USD for Level 4, and 184,000 USD - 287,500 USD for Level 5.

You will also be eligible for equity and benefits.

Applications for this job will be accepted at least until April 4, 2026.

This posting is for an existing vacancy.

NVIDIA uses AI tools in its recruiting processes.

NVIDIA is committed to fostering a diverse work environment and proud to be an equal opportunity employer. As we highly value diversity in our current and future employees, we do not discriminate (including in our hiring and promotion practices) on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, gender expression, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, disability status or any other characteristic protected by law.

jobs.title: Product Marketing Manager
jobs.description:
The Opportunity

We are looking for a strategic and experienced Product Marketing Manager to lead go-to-market efforts for Adobe CX Analytics , a unified set of intelligence solutions within Adobe CX Enterprise. This is a high-impact position dedicated to growing enterprise adoption of our technology and improving customer data value with analytics modernization.

You’ll connect real customer needs with product strategy and collaborate across Adobe’s enterprise ecosystem. You’ll help position Adobe customer experience analytics as the intelligence foundation for crafting data-driven customer experiences.

What You’ll Do

Lead Go-to-Market (GTM) : Build messaging, positioning, and launch strategies to drive differentiation in the market. Collaborate with all corporate functions to deliver innovative Go-To-Market plans, showcase our products value, and drive engagement & monetization for the category.
Champion Customer-Led Innovation : Construct data-driven market and customer analysis. Collaborate with enterprise customers to gather insights, validate use cases, and ensure our solutions evolve with customer needs.
Drive Strategic Deal Support : Partner with our top strategic account teams and specialists to support deal cycles for AI transformation and customer analytics initiatives. Support multi-cloud and cross-solution deals.
Enable Sales & Subject Matter Expertise : Develop deep expertise and build sales enablement materials, enterprise pitch decks, and training content. Equip internal teams to effectively communicate solution value to technical and business audiences.
Work Cross-Functionally : Collaborate across Product Management, Engineering, GTM, Field Sales, Enablement, Customer Success, and broader Enterprise teams to build alignment and execution.
Lead with Influence : Bring clarity and focus across a matrixed organization. Align cross-functional team members around a unified vision and strategy.

Role Requirements

5+ years of experience in product marketing, enterprise GTM, or solution marketing, preferably in AI, SaaS, MarTech, or data/analytics products.
Exceptional communication skills , capable of conveying complex ideas effectively to diverse audiences.
Proven ability to support enterprise sales teams with content, training, and deal strategy.
Analytical and strategic problem solver , adept at using data to inform decisions.
Strong ability to lead and collaborate in a dynamic, matrixed environment .
Outstanding record of driving complex GTM motions , partnering with strategic accounts, and working closely with technical team members such as enterprise architects and data teams.
Strong cross-functional leadership : Develop alignment and momentum across product, engineering, sales, and marketing teams in a complex, matrixed environment.

About Adobe
Adobe empowers everyone to create through innovative platforms and tools that unleash creativity, productivity and personalized customer experiences. Adobe’s industry-leading offerings including Adobe Acrobat Studio, Adobe Express, Adobe Firefly, Creative Cloud, Adobe Experience Platform, Adobe Experience Manager, and GenStudio enable people and businesses to turn ideas into impact, powered by AI and driven by human ingenuity.

Our 30,000+ employees worldwide are creating the future and raising the bar as we drive the next decade of growth. We’re on a mission to hire the very best and believe in creating a company culture where all employees are empowered to make an impact. At Adobe, we believe that great ideas can come from anywhere in the organization. The next big idea could be yours.

Let’s Adobe together
At Adobe, we believe in creating a company culture where all employees are empowered to make an impact. Learn more about Adobe life, including our values and culture , focus on people, purpose and community , Adobe for All , comprehensive benefits programs , the stories we tell , the customers we serve, and how you can help us advance our mission of empowering everyone to create.

Adobe is proud to be an Equal Employment Opportunity employer. We do not discriminate based on gender, race or color, ethnicity or national origin, age, disability, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, veteran status, or any other protected characteristic. Learn more.
