Appian's Applicant & Candidate Privacy Notice

jobs.title: Brand & Growth Lead
jobs.description:
About Goodfire

Goodfire is a research company using interpretability to understand, learn from, and design AI systems. Our mission is to build the next generation of safe and powerful AI—not by scaling alone, but by understanding the intelligence we're building.

Scaling has proven powerful, but today's approach is fundamentally limited: we can't meaningfully understand, debug, or shape what models learn. Every engineering discipline has been gated by fundamental science and AI is at that inflection point now.

We're advancing the science of how AI systems actually work. Treating models as black boxes is an unnecessary handicap—we have access to the structures inside them, and understanding those structures lets us steer what models learn, make them safer and more useful, and extract the vast knowledge they contain. Our goal is to make AI that can be understood, debugged, and shaped like software.

Goodfire is a public benefit corporation headquartered in San Francisco with a team of the world’s top interpretability researchers and engineers from organizations like OpenAI and DeepMind. We're backed by over $200M from B Capital, Menlo Ventures, Lightspeed, Eric Schmidt, and others.

About the role

We are looking for a Brand & Growth Lead to own Goodfire’s public voice, particularly on X. This is a unique position responsible for communicating how our research advances are creating a new paradigm in deep learning that allows researchers to understand, debug, and design AI models like written software.

You will work closely with both our research and go-to-market teams to translate our research results into compelling stories that shape the public conversation, drive inbound interest, and attract world-class researchers and engineers.

You should have deep fluency in the AI landscape, exceptional storytelling ability, excellent taste, and high agency. You should have built a significant following on X before and have a track record of public communications that you're proud of and can share.

Key responsibilities
Own Goodfire's social media strategy and execution. Build a distinctive, high-credibility presence that increases awareness of Goodfire and our capabilities.
Turn complex research into threads, essays, blog posts, and explainers that technical audiences love to read, in topics from from core research breakthroughs to applied work with our partners in areas like life sciences.
Drive measurable inbound demand and recruiting interest through brand and content efforts.
Shape Goodfire's external narrative: how we talk about our capabilities, why it matters, and where the field is going.
Partner with founders and researchers to amplify their voices, helping them develop their social presence and translating their thinking into public-facing content.
What you’ll bring
Required experience
5+ years in brand, marketing, communications, growth, or adjacent roles – though we're also open to candidates from AI research without a traditional marketing background.
Strong ability to communicate complex technical ideas clearly and persuasively.
Demonstrated success building audiences and driving engagement on social media
Familiarity with the AI landscape and the narratives shaping the field.
Strong taste and judgment around messaging, storytelling, and public communication.
Excellent long and short form written communication skills
Preferred qualifications
Experience working in AI, machine learning, developer tools, or research-driven companies.
Experience working closely with researchers or technical founders.
Early-stage startup experience
Experience marketing or communicating to technical audiences in life sciences or biotech
Our values

Goodfire is looking for individuals who embody our values and share our deep commitment to making interpretability accessible. We are building a team first and foremost.

Put mission and team first

All we do is in service of our mission. We trust each other, deeply care about the success of the organization, and choose to put our team above ourselves.

Improve constantly

We are constantly looking to improve every piece of the business. We proactively critique ourselves and others in a kind and thoughtful way that translates to practical improvements in the organization. We are pragmatic and consistently implement the obvious fixes that work.

Take ownership and initiative

There are no bystanders here. We proactively identify problems and take full responsibility over getting a strong result. We are self-driven, own our mistakes, and feel deep responsibility over what we’re building.

Action today

We have a small amount of time to do something incredibly hard and meaningful. The pace and intensity of the organization is high. If we can take action today or tomorrow, we will choose to do it today.

Where we work

We are hiring for this position in our San Francisco HQ. We are in person 5 days a week, with one company-wide remote week per month.

What we offer

This role offers market competitive salary, equity, and competitive benefits.
The expected salary range for this position is $150,000 - $300,000 USD

Most importantly, you'll have the opportunity to join a vital mission at an important point in its trajectory — we are developing groundbreaking technology with a world-class team on the critical path to ensuring a safe and beneficial future for humanity. If you want to do your life’s work with us, even if you believe you do not meet every single requirement, apply now.

jobs.title: Founding Marketing Lead
jobs.description:
About Phonely

At some point in the future, every business will answer their phone with voice AI. We are building the platform that lets them do that - and optimize every conversation along the way.

Phonely builds conversational voice AI agents for high volume phone workflows. Our customers use us to qualify leads, book appointments, route calls, and resolve real customer conversations in production. We are growing fast and just getting started.

About the role

We are looking for our first Marketing Lead - someone who is sharp, creative, and genuinely excited about building something from scratch. You will own how the world understands and thinks about Phonely, and you will have the trust and freedom to do it your way.

You will work closely with the founders and across the whole team. If you love the early stage - the speed, the ownership, the chance to actually shape something - this is the role for you.

What you will do

Brand and messaging: Define who we are and what we stand for. Own the website narrative, buyer personas, and ICP.

Organic growth: Build content pillars, a publishing cadence, and thought leadership across social, blog, newsletter, and events.

Demand generation: Layer SEO, PR, partnerships, and paid onto a strong organic base. Brief and manage an external agency for campaigns.

Onboarding and nurture: Build sequences for different segments of our pipeline that support conversion and retention.

What you bring

5+ years of progressive marketing experience

A generalist: brand, content, growth, and demand gen

You have built marketing from 0 to 1 at an early-stage company

Equally strong in strategy and execution — you can write the playbook and run the plays

Familiarity with tech or B2B SaaS; genuine curiosity about the space at minimum

Key outcomes

Within your first 6 to 12 months, success looks like:

Brand clarity: clear messaging, a strong website, and a defined ICP

Organic engine: consistent publishing, growing social presence, and inbound leads from content

Pipeline impact: measurable top-of-funnel growth and enterprise client conversions driven by the channels you built

Agency relationship: a functioning demand-gen program running with an external partner

Why join

Report directly to and collaborate with the founding team

Full ownership of the marketing function - strategy, voice, channels, and process

A product that is already working in production, with customers seeing real results

The opportunity to shape how a breakout company shows up in the world

If you are a hands-on marketing leader ready to build something from the ground up - we would love to meet you.

About the interview

Intro call to evaluate fit

Deep dive on your 0-to-1 experience and marketing strategy

Case study to see how you think

Final conversation with leadership

Compensation Range: $140K - $200K

jobs.title: Founding Marketing Lead
jobs.description:
Meet Network Right

Network Right is the fractional IT team for fast-growing, venture-backed companies. We partner with startups as their dedicated IT function, handling the day-to-day support that keeps teams productive while guiding long-term IT strategy as they scale.

What truly sets us apart is not just what we do, but how we do it.

We operate as an extension of our clients’ teams. Our consultants integrate directly into their day to day, build real relationships with employees, and become trusted partners to founders and operators. We combine responsive, human support with strategic foresight, delivering enterprise-grade IT leadership without the overhead of building a full internal department.

Over the years, we’ve supported hundreds of startups through critical stages of growth, from seed to acquisition. Companies like Clearbit, Bubble, Eventbrite, and Hightouch have trusted Network Right to build a secure, scalable IT function that evolves alongside their business.

Founded by seasoned IT professionals, Network Right supports teams across the U.S., with offices in San Francisco and New York City.

About the Role

Network Right is seeking a Founding Marketing Lead to shape the future of our growing company as one of our first marketing hires. As a lead on the Revenue team, there’s an opportunity to create, build, and scale the Network Right marketing engine from the ground up, working closely with the founders and cross-functional stakeholders.

What You’ll Own:

Demand generation strategy and execution: Own and scale referral, outbound, SEO, and experimental channels. Drive full-funnel performance from awareness through pipeline generation in close partnership with sales.

Messaging and positioning: Define and refine core narratives, ICPs, and buyer-stage use cases. Own messaging across the website, proposals, case studies, sales collateral, outbound campaigns, and content.

Growth marketing infrastructure and analytics: Design, manage, and optimize the growth stack (HubSpot, Clay, Apollo). Build reporting, attribution, and data standards that provide clear visibility into pipeline, CAC, channel ROI, and conversion performance.

Content strategy and execution: Lead content development across website copy, case studies, blog posts, LinkedIn, presentations, one-pagers, and event materials, ensuring alignment with positioning and demand generation goals.

Channel experimentation: Identify, test, and scale new channels to reach our ICP, including partnerships, events, advertising, and emerging opportunities.

Performance management and vendor oversight: Establish and own marketing KPIs. Continuously test and optimize performance. Manage external vendors across SEO, design, and web to deliver measurable outcomes.

What We’re Looking For

6 to 8+ years of experience in B2B marketing, including experience as an early-stage or founding marketer

Demonstrated experience building and orchestrating multi-channel growth systems, aligning SEO, outbound, paid, and content into a unified go-to-market motion

Naturally curious and solution-oriented. You challenge conventional thinking, interrogate the why behind performance, and develop multiple paths to solving the how

Proven ability to rapidly test new ideas, pivot when necessary, and scale successful initiatives in a fast-paced environment

Detail-oriented with strong operational discipline. You maintain data accuracy, ensure messaging consistency, and tightly align execution with strategy

Experience operating in both PLG and SLG motions

BA/BS degree in Business, Marketing, or a related field preferred. A quantitative focus is a plus

Compensation Range: $150K - $225K

jobs.title: Director of Content Marketing
jobs.description:
San Francisco, California
Marketing – Marketing /
US Full-time Salaried /
On-site

Samba is a media intelligence company. We know what the world is watching, reading, and thinking about — in real time, at scale, across every screen. Our data exists with the consent of over a billion people, organized into the most complete picture of consumer attention ever built. The biggest brands in the world use that picture to make smarter decisions. We think it’s the most interesting data asset on the planet, because it’s the most culturally relevant.

Samba is looking for a Director of Content Marketing to lead and elevate our content strategy end-to-end. In this role, you will build a high-performing content engine, our own editorial newsroom, powered by Samba’s unmatched media intelligence across TV, web, and emerging platforms. You’ll shape the stories that define our brand, showcasing our industry-leading TV and digital viewership data, AI/ML innovations, agentic workflow solutions, and next-generation ad tech products.

As a senior leader operating at the intersection of advanced AI technology and programmatic advertising, you will translate complex data science concepts into clear, compelling content that engages advertisers, agencies, publishers, and technology partners. You will own the full content lifecycle from ideation to distribution, while partnering closely with Product Marketing, PR, Sales, Technical teams, and executive leadership to ensure alignment and impact across the organization. You will manage a small scale high performing team. Deliverables will depend on your storytelling skills and ability to project manage creation of snackable quick visual bites of insights (charts, infographics, etc), as well as shorter reports.
What You'll Do
Content Strategy & Execution
Build and lead a high-performing content engine, our editorial newsroom, leveraging Samba’s cross-screen media intelligence.
Drive a focused content strategy aligned with launches, GTM priorities, and audience needs.
Produce high-impact, multi-format content (reports, visuals, articles, social, case studies, videos, enablement).
Translate complex data and AI/ML concepts into clear, snackable narratives.
Create repeatable storytelling frameworks around cross-screen insights, measurement, AI audiences, and ad effectiveness.
Own the content calendar end-to-end for timely, high-quality output.
Collaborate with data science, product, and engineering for accuracy and clarity.
Thought Leadership & Brand Storytelling
Position Samba as the leader in AI-driven advertising, cross-screen measurement, and the future of streaming.
Partner with PR/Comms on executive thought leadership, media narratives, and analyst/conference content.
Identify content opportunities from trends, competition, customer insights, and internal innovation.
Ensure narrative consistency across touchpoints and develop strong customer stories that demonstrate ROI.
Social & Distribution
Manage and optimize distribution across LinkedIn, X, and other channels.
Lead social strategy to amplify thought leadership and product news.
Monitor engagement and performance to refine content decisions
Collaborate with Digital Marketing to support GEO, SEO, SEM, and paid promotion.
Who You Are
7–10+ years in content marketing; bonus if in ad tech, martech, data platforms, or SaaS.
Comfortable and excited about creating content topics around AI/ML, data science, predictive analytics, and AI-driven marketing.
Hands-on use of AI tools (e.g., GPT, Claude, autonomous agents, orchestration frameworks).
Can work independently driving projects end-to-end, while also managing a team of high - performers.
Exceptional writer/storyteller able to simplify technical concepts for varied audiences.
Full-stack creator for visual form content, long-form reports, and social posts
Curious, collaborative, and effective across Product, Sales, Marketing, and Exec teams.
Learns quickly and thrives in fast-changing environments.
Bias for action—ships, learns, iterates.
Writes in a clear, human, on-brand voice.
Comfortable speaking at conferences.
BA in Marketing, Communications, Journalism; advanced degree a plus.

#LI-AH1
$170,000 - $180,000 a year
Samba expects to offer a base salary between $170,000 - $180,000 per year for roles to be performed in New York or California; actual base salary offered will depend on various factors, including but not limited to, location, experience, and performance. Base salary is just one component of Samba’s total compensation package for employees. Other rewards may include bonuses, short-term incentives, and long-term incentives. In addition, Samba provides health insurance, wellness offerings, life and disability insurance, a retirement savings plan, paid holidays and paid time off (PTO), and other employee benefits.

Samba is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. We strive to empower connection with one another, reflect the communities we serve, and tackle meaningful projects that make a real impact.

Samba may collect personal information directly from you, as a job applicant, Samba may also receive personal information from third parties, for example, in connection with a background, employment or reference check, in accordance with the applicable law.
We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

jobs.title: Founding Content Marketing Lead
jobs.description:
TL;DR: We’re hiring our first Content Marketing Lead to work directly with our Head of Marketing to help scale Listen from $XXM $100M+ ARR. This role is for someone who loves the craft and the strategy: you’re interviewing customers, writing the scripts, penning research papers, and stepping in front of the camera when needed. Expect to move fast, run tight experiments, scale what works, and grow your career!

Min. 8+ years in B2B SaaS content, product marketing, editorial, or brand storytelling roles. Comfort with video, social-first content, and customer storytelling is important. Prior experience as a social media creator is a plus.

Background

Listen Labs is an AI-powered research platform that helps teams uncover insights from customer interviews in hours — not months. We help customers analyze conversations, surface themes, and make faster, smarter product decisions.

Company highlights — entirely product-led:

World-Class Team: Founded by serial entrepreneurs (previous AI exit), former co-founders, and talent from Jane Street, Twitter, Stripe, Affirm, Bain, Goldman Sachs, and many more Sequoia-backed startups (plus IOI/ICPC backgrounds).

Hypergrowth: We're a fast-growing team backed by top-tier investors that's grown several-fold in the past year, and we're set on multiplying that again. We move fast, care deeply about craft, and love working with people who take ownership.

Traction: Rapid growth across segments with enterprise wins at Google, Microsoft, Nestlé, and P&G.

Performance: Industry-leading win rate driven by a highly differentiated product.

Market Validation: Consistently winning customers across all segments with over six-figure lands that lead to quick expansions.

Viral Product: Interviews are shared with tens of thousands of viewers, fueling PLG, organic expansion, and daily inbound from Fortune 500s.

What You'll Do

Own content strategy and execution across case studies, webinars, blogs, and social

Create content researchers love and that shows how Listen helps them do better work

Turn complex topics into clear, compelling stories and standout B2B content

Write high-quality assets including whitepapers, reports, launch materials, and scripts

Interview customers and translate insights into content that drives credibility and demand

Test and scale new formats including video, webinars, podcasts, and social-first content

Partner with founders and internal teams to shape narratives and bring ideas to market

Use performance data to iterate quickly and scale what works

Who You Are

Strong writer, storyteller, and editor with excellent taste

Curious and articulate

Comfortable across multiple content formats, including video

(Preferred) Confident on camera, or excited to grow into it

Fast, scrappy, and energized by early-stage ambiguity

Creative and metrics-driven

Motivated by craft, impact, and building something from the ground up

Life at Listen Labs

Competitive Compensation: We're backed by world-class investors, including Sequoia Capital, Ribbit, and Conviction, alongside Evantic, AI Grant, and Pear VC, and offer competitive compensation packages with meaningful equity ownership in a market where over 30B has been created in adjacent industries (Medallia, AlphaSense, GLG, Ipsos, Kantar). Our Sequoia partner, Bryan Schreier, was the first investor in Qualtrics—a $12B company tackling similar problems to ours.

The range for this role is $120,000 – $160,000 OTE. Actual compensation is influenced by a wide array of factors, including but not limited to skill set, experience, and work location. If this range doesn't match your expectations, we still encourage you to apply.

Benefits that Support You: Comprehensive healthcare and dental coverage, flexible time off to recharge, and an environment that values balance and trust.

Room to Grow: As an early member of the team, you’ll have the opportunity to have end-to-end ownership over new responsibilities, shape processes from scratch, and grow alongside the company. We value people who want to stretch beyond their role and build something lasting.

jobs.title: Content Lead
jobs.description:
Hilbert is a scalable, data science-first growth infrastructure that gives B2C teams predictive clarity into user behavior, revenue drivers, and the actions that move sustainable growth. Fully agentic by design, Hilbert compresses months-long decision cycles into minutes.

From Fortune 10 enterprises to beloved brands like FreshDirect, Blank Street, and Levain Bakery, operators run their growth on Hilbert. We're also co-building alongside leading AI companies.

A small, select group of growth operators sit at the core of our company. These minds shape how Hilbert's intelligence layer evolves, how customers grow, and how the company goes to market.

THE ROLE

You will build and run Hilbert’s content engine from the ground up: editorial strategy, video and media programming, sales enablement materials, customer stories, website copy, and the thought leadership that makes Hilbert the most credible voice in the room on B2C growth intelligence.

Core Responsibilities

Brand, Thought Leadership & Organic Presence

Own Hilbert’s editorial voice and brand positioning across all channels. Ensure that everything published under the Hilbert name reflects a clear, earned, and consistent point of view on B2C growth intelligence.

Write and publish original long-form content: essays, frameworks, and category-defining pieces that position Hilbert as the authoritative voice for operators who take B2C growth seriously.

Drive Hilbert’s organic content strategy: identify the topics, formats, and channels where the audience is paying attention, and build a presence that compounds.

Own the LinkedIn presence for both the company page and founder voices — content strategy, publishing cadence, and amplification logic.

Video & Media Programming

Own end-to-end production of Hilbert Space, our videocast series featuring growth leaders. Handle guest pipeline, pre-production research, editorial direction, distribution, and clip strategy.

Build and execute an education video strategy for Hilbertl: explainer content on B2C growth mechanics, product walkthroughs, use case breakdowns, and concepts like cohort economics, LTV modeling, channel attribution, and agentic growth infrastructure.

Sales Enablement & Customer Evidence

Produce the content the sales team needs to close: one-pagers, pitch narratives, competitive positioning documents, objection-handling resources, and tailored collateral for specific verticals or account types.

Build Hilbert’s customer story library: case studies, outcome-driven proof points, and reference materials that translate what Hilbert has done for customers into language that resonates with new prospects.

WHO THRIVES IN THIS ROLE

This is not an entry-level content role. It is not a role for someone who needs to be trained on what B2B SaaS marketing looks like. It is for someone who has already run this function, or a large piece of it, and knows both what works and what is overrated. The bar is: you have shipped content that changed how an audience thought about a problem, and you can prove it.

You have 5+ years of content and marketing experience at a B2B technology company, with at least part of that time spent in a founding or near-founding content capacity — building from scratch, owning outcomes, not inheriting infrastructure.

You have produced content across the full B2B stack: thought leadership and brand, SEO and organic, sales enablement, video, customer evidence, and website copy. You know how these functions connect and how they break down when no one is holding them together.

You understand B2C growth mechanics well enough to write about them accurately and translate them for an audience of practitioners. Cohort economics, paid acquisition against LTV, retention curves, channel mix, agentic decision-making — you do not need to have run these plays yourself, but you need to be fluent enough to engage with experts and produce content that earns their respect.

You have owned a video or media program end-to-end: not just writing scripts or managing a YouTube channel, but building and running a format that attracted a real audience over time.

You think in systems: content calendars are table stakes. What you care about is the architecture behind them — which formats compound, which audiences you are actually building, what the flywheel looks like at scale.

You are a sharp editor who raises the quality floor for everything that goes out. Vague claims, hollow thought leadership, and content that could have been written by anyone are things you treat as professional failures.

You have worked closely with sales and GTM teams and understand that content is not a function that operates independently of the commercial motion. You have built enablement materials that people on the sales team actually used.

You are comfortable in a fast-moving, small team environment where the scope is real and the feedback loop is short.

ABOUT HILBERT'S AI

Hilbert is a scalable, data science-first growth engine built on four intelligence layers: Detect, Reason, Act, Optimize, running across acquisition, retention, and monetization simultaneously. No dashboards. No manual analysis. A short data ingestion and the engine is live, SOC 2-compliant, and getting sharper with every outcome. The product is proven. The customer base is growing. The decisions made in the next 18 months will define what Hilbert becomes.

jobs.title: Content & Brand Marketer
jobs.description:
Founding Content Marketer

Location: San Francisco, CA
Type: Full-Time
Work Arrangement: In-Person

About Flai

Flai is building an AI communications platform for car dealerships. Dealerships use Flai to handle inbound calls, lead follow-up, and customer communication across service and sales, helping them capture more demand, book more appointments, and deliver a better customer experience.

This is a massive, operationally complex market where communication problems directly translate into lost revenue. Dealers are missing calls, customers are getting stuck on hold, and service teams are stretched thin. Flai gives dealerships a better way to manage customer communication at scale.

We're backed by First Round, Y Combinator, SV Angel, Liquid 2, Toyota Ventures, and other leading investors, working with dealership groups across the U.S. and growing quickly.

About the Role

We're hiring our first Content Marketer to help shape how Flai shows up in the market.

This is a hands-on content role. You'll turn customer results, product launches, market insights, and dealership pain points into content that makes Flai's value clear. This includes case studies, customer videos, blog posts, LinkedIn content, website copy, sales collateral, launch content, event materials, and more.

This is a hands-on role for someone who loves writing, storytelling, and shipping work. You'll have strong raw material: a massive market, real customer pain, measurable ROI, and a product dealers immediately understand.

What You'll Do

Interview customers and turn their results into case studies, testimonials, blog posts, and social content

Produce customer testimonial videos by coordinating interviews, videographers, editors, and agencies

Write across blog posts, product launches, website copy, one-pagers, emails, sales collateral, and event materials

Turn customer wins, product updates, and company milestones into LinkedIn content for Flai and the founders

Translate dealership pain points and product value into clear, credible messaging

Partner with sales and customer success to create content that supports the buying process

Work with SEO partners or agencies to keep content accurate, useful, and on-brand

Build a reusable content library for sales, customer success, recruiting, events, and brand

What We're Looking For

2 to 6 years in content marketing, brand marketing, customer marketing, product marketing, or a similar B2B role

Strong writing ability and a portfolio of work you personally created or directly owned

Experience creating customer-backed content such as case studies, testimonials, customer stories, or sales narratives

Experience producing video content with freelancers, agencies, editors, or videographers

Strong judgment for what makes content specific, credible, and differentiated

Ability to turn rough input from customers, sales calls, product conversations, or founder ideas into polished external content

Comfort learning a complex industry and making it accessible

High ownership, strong follow-through, and a bias toward shipping

Nice to have

Experience at an early-stage B2B startup

Experience marketing to traditional or operationally complex industries

Experience creating LinkedIn content for founders, executives, or B2B brands

Familiarity with AI, automation, vertical SaaS, or voice AI

Why Flai

Flai sits in a huge market with a clear problem, measurable ROI, and customer stories ready to be told. This is a foundational marketing hire. You won't be handed a stale content calendar and asked to fill it. You'll help define the voice, build the proof, and shape how the market understands Flai.

What We Offer

Competitive salary and equity. Full benefits including medical, dental, and vision. Flexible PTO. A small, high-trust team where your work has real impact from day one.

Compensation Range: $80K - $130K

jobs.title: Global Event and Trade Show Content Lead
jobs.description:
Our vision is to transform how the world uses information to enrich life for all.

Micron Technology is a world leader in innovating memory and storage solutions that accelerate the transformation of information into intelligence, inspiring the world to learn, communicate and advance faster than ever.

The Micron Event and Brand Experience team brings Micron’s story to life on the world’s biggest stages. We connect innovation, brand, and business priorities through high-impact event experiences that matter to customers, analysts, and leaders alike. If you love turning complexity into clarity and shaping how a global technology company shows up, this team delivers!

This role is the connective tissue of Micron’s event strategy. As Event and Tradeshow Content Lead, you shape the narrative spine of each show—aligning business unit priorities, corporate messaging, and booth experiences into one clear Micron story. You influence senior stakeholders, guide content decisions, and ensure every event delivers relevance, consistency, and impact.

Responsibilities:
Define and own the end-to-end show narrative, aligning BU product priorities with corporate and brand messaging

8+ years of experience in event marketing, experiential marketing, content strategy, or brand experience roles

Strong editorial and narrative skills translating technical content into audience-ready stories

Preferred Qualifications:
Experience supporting major B2B trade shows such as GTC, CES, Supercomputing, or Embedded World
Background in semiconductor, memory/storage, AI, or data center technologies
Experience developing booth staff training programs and enablement content
Comfort synthesizing event performance data into executive insights and recommendations
Proficiency with project management tools and Microsoft PowerPoint, SharePoint, and Excel

jobs.title: Senior Data Automation/Marketing Ops Lead
jobs.description:
Santa Clara, California, United States IT Ref ID: JR-015292

Our Mission

At Palo Alto Networks®, we’re united by a shared mission—to protect our digital way of life. We thrive at the intersection of innovation and impact, solving real-world problems with cutting-edge technology and bold thinking. Here, everyone has a voice, and every idea counts. If you’re ready to do the most meaningful work of your career alongside people who are just as passionate as you are, you’re in the right place.

Who We Are

In order to be the cybersecurity partner of choice, we must trailblaze the path and shape the future of our industry. This is something our employees work at each day and is defined by our values: Disruption, Collaboration, Execution, Integrity, and Inclusion. We weave AI into the fabric of everything we do and use it to augment the impact every individual can have. If you are passionate about solving real-world problems and ideating beside the best and the brightest, we invite you to join us!

We believe collaboration thrives in person. That’s why most of our teams work from the office full time, with flexibility when it’s needed. This model supports real-time problem-solving, stronger relationships, and the kind of precision that drives great outcomes.

Job Summary

We are seeking a highly strategic and visionary Senior Data Automation/Marketing Ops Lead to design and oversee our global marketing technology stack and data platform ecosystem. Firmly seated within the digital marketing organization, this role is explicitly differentiated from traditional IT functions; instead, you will serve as the critical bridge, partnering closely with IT for flawless technical execution. Reporting to the Director of Marketing Data and Operations, this leader will drive digital acquisition capabilities, focusing on integrating artificial intelligence, enabling end-to-end performance tracking, and optimizing operational workflows for unprecedented scale and efficiency. The successful candidate will be a creative problem-solver who thrives in a fast-changing environment, acting as the primary technical architect to ensure our technology foundation actively supports aggressive global business growth.

Key Responsibilities

Be the principal marketing architect for the marketing technology stack, actively partnering with IT to design and execute the full end-to-end (E2E) vision.

Lead the MarTech council, serving as the primary technical Subject Matter Expert (SME) in marketing for the rigorous technical evaluation of proposed vendors and marketing use cases.

Act as the primary technical interface between Marketing and IT ensuring seamless solution design, implementation, and the proactive communication of MarTech stack requirements to Engineering/IT stakeholders.

Be the marketing lead, guide and direct the solution design and development of a fully integrated MarTech ecosystem, in collaboration with IT, to enable real-time omnichannel journey orchestration and facilitate agentic automation.

Provide technical leadership and oversight to ensure successful, compliant, and efficient Customer Data Platform (CDP) development and implementation.

Champion technical best practices for real-time data processing, modeling, and seamless AI deployment, driving automation and replacing manual processes with intelligent workflows.

Design, document, and implement highly scalable operational processes (SLAs, governance) that optimize workflow efficiency, productivity, and quality across all global digital operations.

Enforce rigorous data quality, governance, and operational excellence across all acquisition processes (data pipelines, tag management) with a mandatory zero-error mentality.

Qualifications

Required Qualifications

Extensive progressive experience in MarTech, Marketing Operations, or Digital Marketing roles, with a proven track record of leading technical initiatives.

Extensive experience driving design and implementation of scalable data infrastructure that meet the demands of real-time decisioning.

Expert-level knowledge of technical integrations across the MarTech stack, including CDPs, CRMs (e.g., Salesforce), and internal data warehousing solutions (e.g., Snowflake, BigQuery).

Deep technical fluency in leveraging AI/ML, Generative AI, and predictive modeling for real-time data orchestration.

Proven ability to translate complex business objectives into detailed technical requirements and user stories (PRDs), managing a development backlog alongside internal engineering teams.

Strong understanding and practical application of global data privacy and compliance standards (GDPR, CCPA, etc.) and their real-world impact on data usage.

Preferred Qualifications

Exceptional program management skills, utilizing agile methodologies to drive large-scale, complex cross-functional technical projects.

Ability to lead at the intersection of business and technology with a passion for marketing technology, clearly differentiating marketing ecosystem needs from traditional IT infrastructure.

Demonstrated success integrating and scaling comprehensive technology solutions: Marketing Automation, CRM, Predictive Analytics, and Data Enrichment.

Strong cross-functional relationship development skills, specifically in establishing effective program management processes between Marketing and IT.

Experience leading an organization through rapid growth as well as acting as a change agent and transformation leader.

Comfort with accountability and the ability to navigate ambiguity and manage competing priorities.

Compensation Disclosure

The compensation offered for this position will depend on qualifications, experience, and work location. For candidates who receive an offer at the posted level, the starting base salary (for non-sales roles) or base salary + commission target (for sales/com-missioned roles) is expected to be the annual range listed below. The offered compensation may also include restricted stock units and a bonus. A description of our employee benefits may be found here.

$167,000.00 - $270,500.00/yr

Our Commitment

We’re trailblazers that dream big, take risks, and challenge cybersecurity’s status quo. It’s simple: we can’t accomplish our mission without diverse teams innovating, together.

We are committed to providing reasonable accommodations for all qualified individuals with a disability. If you require assistance or accommodation due to a disability or special need, please contact us at accommodations@paloaltonetworks.com.

Palo Alto Networks is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity in our workplace, and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, ancestry, color, family or medical care leave, gender identity or expression, genetic information, marital status, medical condition, national origin, physical or mental disability, political affiliation, protected veteran status, race, religion, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, or other legally protected characteristics.

All your information will be kept confidential according to EEO guidelines.

Is role eligible for Immigration Sponsorship? No. Please note that we will not sponsor applicants for work visas for this position.

jobs.description:
Senior Social Media and User Operations Specialist PICO- San Jose

Location:

San Jose

Team:

Marketing & Communications

Employment Type:

Regular

A248030A

Responsibilities

About Team
The Social Media & User Operations Manager will be responsible for building PICO’s digital presence and fostering a high-growth user community in the U.S. market. You will sit at the intersection of content creation and community management, turning everyday users into brand advocates and ensuring PICO is a trending topic in the XR space

Responsibilities
- Social Strategy & Execution: Develop and manage PICO’s U.S. social media calendar across TikTok, X (Twitter), Instagram, and YouTube.
- User Growth & Retention: Design and execute "User Ops" campaigns (challenges, giveaways, and beta-testing programs) to increase Daily Active Users (DAU) within the PICO ecosystem.
- Community Building: Lead the engagement strategy on platforms like Discord and Reddit, acting as the primary bridge between PICO’s engineering team and its power users.
- Content Production: Collaborate with internal creative teams and external creators to produce "Mixed Reality" (MR) captures and viral-ready video content that showcases PICO’s hardware.
- Data & Sentiment Analysis: Monitor community sentiment and social analytics to provide weekly "Voice of the User" reports to the Global Product and Marketing teams.
- Trend Jacking: Identify and pivot on real-time cultural trends to keep PICO relevant in the fast-moving tech and gaming conversation.

Qualifications

Minimum Qualifications
- +3 years in Social Media Marketing or User Operations, ideally within the Gaming, Tech, or Entertainment industries.
- Native-level fluency in leading short-form video platforms and community-building ecosystems, with a deep understanding of engagement algorithms, viral trends, and modern moderation practices.
- Proficient in industry-standard video editing software and digital content creation, with a proven ability to translate complex technical features into compelling visual narratives.
- Copywriting skills with a "brand voice" that is authentic, witty, and tech-savvy.

Preferred Qualifications
- Proven track record of growing a community from "zero to 100k+" members through organic and viral strategies.
- Experience working within a Global/Asia-based tech firm; ability to align U.S. community trends with global brand directives.
- knowledge of social listening tools (e.g., Brandwatch, Sprout Social) and user retention metrics (LTV, Churn, NPS).
- Experience directing "Mixed Reality" content creators to showcase the immersive nature of VR on 2D social feeds.

Job Information

【For Pay Transparency】Compensation Description (Annually)

The base salary range for this position in the selected city is $107520 - $288000 annually.

Compensation may vary outside of this range depending on a number of factors, including a candidate’s qualifications, skills, competencies and experience, and location. Base pay is one part of the Total Package that is provided to compensate and recognize employees for their work, and this role may be eligible for additional discretionary bonuses/incentives, and restricted stock units.

Employees have day one access to medical, dental, and vision insurance, a 401(k) savings plan with company match, paid parental leave, short-term and long-term disability coverage, life insurance, wellbeing benefits, among others. Employees also receive 10 paid holidays per year, 10 paid sick days per year and 17 days of Paid Personal Time (prorated upon hire with increasing accruals by tenure).

Founded in April 2015, Pico is a VR company committed to developing immersive and interactive VR experiences for people around the world. Pico also provides tailor-made solutions for our enterprise clients in the fields of education and healthcare.

Why Join ByteDance

Inspiring creativity is at the core of ByteDance's mission. Our innovative products are built to help people authentically express themselves, discover and connect – and our global, diverse teams make that possible. Together, we create value for our communities, inspire creativity and enrich life - a mission we work towards every day.

As ByteDancers, we strive to do great things with great people. We lead with curiosity, humility, and a desire to make impact in a rapidly growing tech company. By constantly iterating and fostering an "Always Day 1" mindset, we achieve meaningful breakthroughs for ourselves, our Company, and our users. When we create and grow together, the possibilities are limitless. Join us.

Diversity & Inclusion

ByteDance is committed to creating an inclusive space where employees are valued for their skills, experiences, and unique perspectives. Our platform connects people from across the globe and so does our workplace. At ByteDance, our mission is to inspire creativity and enrich life. jobs.title: Social and Community Manager
jobs.description:
Dexmate is building the foundation for physical AI — combining a new generation of robots with a universal Physical AI OS, making robots as easy to build and deploy as software. Today, robotics is fragmented, slow, and closed: most builders are forced to reinvent the same stack again and again, and most ideas never make it past the prototype stage. We exist to change that. Our mission is to democratize robotics by lowering the barrier to entry, delivering a plug-and-play platform for developers, researchers, and enterprises, and cultivating an open ecosystem that accelerates the evolution of physical AI. If you want to help shape the next layer of human capability — and believe the future of robotics should be built together, not in isolation — we'd love to build it with you.

About the Role

We're looking for a Social & Community Manager to amplify Dexmate's voice, build a strong industry presence, and engage with our growing audience.

This role will own how we show up across social channels and within technical communities, helping translate our technology into engaging, accessible content while building long-term trust with developers, engineers, and enterprise buyers.

You will work closely with Product Marketing, Content, and Leadership to ensure our messaging is consistently delivered and effectively reaches the right audience.

What You'll Do
Social Media Strategy & Execution

Own and manage Dexmate's presence across key social platforms

Plan and execute a consistent content calendar aligned with overall marketing priorities

Publish and optimize posts to drive engagement, awareness, and traffic

Identify potential high-impact or viral content opportunities and collaborate with the Content team to develop and amplify them

Content Amplification

Collaborate with Product Marketing and Content teams to distribute:

Product launches

Case studies

Demo videos

Thought leadership content

Adapt technical content into engaging, platform-native formats

Community Building & Engagement

Develop strategies to grow the community while maintaining quality interactions and alignment with Dexmate's brand

Build and nurture a vibrant community of developers, engineers, and industry professionals

Actively engage with community members online to foster meaningful interactions

Monitor, respond to, and facilitate discussions to maintain a healthy, active community

Identify, cultivate, and maintain relationships with early adopters, advocates, and partners

Design and execute activities, events, and initiatives that drive engagement, retention, and growth

Brand Voice & Storytelling

Help define and maintain a consistent, authentic brand voice

Ensure all social communication reflects Dexmate's positioning and values

Contribute ideas to make the brand more relatable and visible in the industry

Performance Tracking & Optimization

Track key social metrics:

Engagement rates

Follower growth

Traffic and conversions

Analyze performance and continuously optimize content strategy

Required Qualifications

3–6+ years of experience in social media, community, or content marketing, preferably in B2B or tech environments

Strong understanding of X, LinkedIn, Discord and other relevant platforms for B2B audiences

Ability to translate complex technical topics into engaging and accessible content

Experience setting up and managing paid social campaigns to drive broader reach and generate high-quality leads

Excellent writing and communication skills

Experience managing content calendars and social tools

Self-starter with a proactive mindset and attention to detail

Preferred Qualifications

Experience in robotics, AI, hardware, or developer-focused products

Familiarity with technical communities or developer ecosystems

Experience working with video content

Understanding of enterprise audiences and long sales cycles

jobs.title: Growth Lead
jobs.description:
The Mission

At Nace AI, we are building the cognitive infrastructure for the modern professional. Our platform empowers professional knowledge workers like auditors and accountants to get 1000 hours of productivity back.

Backed by General Catalyst and Intel, we have the capital, the pedigree, and the technical edge to define the future of work. We’ve found our early "true believers," and now we are looking for a Performance Marketing Team Lead to scale our reach to millions of professionals globally.

The Role

As our first dedicated Performance Lead, you will bridge the gap between high-level growth strategy and tactical execution. You’ll be responsible for building a world-class acquisition engine that targets high-value professionals, ensuring Nace AI becomes the default tool in their workflow.

What You’ll Do:

Scale Multi-Channel Growth: Own the strategy and execution across Search, Paid Social (LinkedIn/Meta), and high-intent platforms like Twitter/X and newsletter sponsorships.

Build the "Nace" Brand Engine: Lead a small team of growth marketers and creative specialists to produce ads that don't look like ads, but like essential tools for knowledge workers.

Rigorous Optimization: Manage substantial budgets with a relentless focus on unit economics—optimizing for LTV:CAC and payback periods.

Data Architecture: Partner with Engineering to ensure perfect attribution, building a data-forward culture where every creative experiment is measured and learned from.

In-Person Collaboration: Work side-by-side with our founders and product teams in our Palo Alto office to tighten the feedback loop between user acquisition and product development.

Who You Are

The Operator: 6+ years in Performance Marketing, with a proven track record of scaling B2B or "Prosumer" SaaS products from $0 to $10M+ ARR.

The Leader: You have experience managing high-performing teams or agencies and know how to get the best work out of creative and technical talent.

Analytical Rigor: You are dangerous with SQL and data visualization tools; you don't wait for reports, you build them.

AI-Native: You understand the AI landscape and are already using LLMs to optimize copy, analyze sentiment, or automate workflows.

Palo Alto Based: You value the "war room" energy of a physical office and are committed to being on-site with the team.

Why Nace AI?

Pedigree: Work with a team from top-tier institutions and companies, backed by the best VCs in the world.

Impact: You are joining early enough to shape the culture and the growth roadmap of a company aiming to be the "OS" for professional knowledge.

Competitive Package: Silicon Valley-standard salary, significant equity, and premium benefits.

jobs.title: Marketing Email and Content Manager
jobs.description:
*Job Summary: *

As a Content and Email Marketing Manager at Amen Clinics, you’ll play a vital role in our marketing team, driving our content and email marketing strategies with the intention of driving traffic, leads and enhancing organic search engine optimization (SEO) efforts. You’ll be responsible for planning, executing, and optimizing content and email campaigns, which includes working with our writers, designers and have a solid understanding of Email Service providers to create and schedule multiple emails per week. Your passion for marketing and commitment to staying updated with industry trends (including AI-driven innovations) will be crucial in promoting and engaging our target audience effectively.

*Who We Are:*

Amen Clinics is one of the largest and most respected mental health private practices in the country. Our team is composed of the country’s most talented psychiatrists, psychologists, psychotherapists, and compassionate healers. Our marketing team works in a collaborative environment and are passionate about mental health.

*Essential Duties and Responsibilities: *

_*Content Marketing:*_

· Craft and execute a comprehensive content marketing strategy in alignment with our company’s strategic goals.

· Lead the way in curating, editing, and publishing captivating content across diverse digital platforms, from engaging blogs, to eBooks, listicles, video, social media posts, and many other types of compelling materials.

· Collaborate closely with our talented writers and design team to bring your ideas to life, creating visually stunning infographics and visuals.

· Keep a finger on the pulse of industry trends, ensuring our content remains current, relevant, and competitive.

· Leverage AI-powered tools to enhance content ideation, drafting, and optimization, improving team efficiency and output quality.

_*Email Marketing:*_

· Implement impactful email marketing campaigns that spotlight our products, services, and content with the intention of driving relevant traffic to our website.

· Master the art of email list segmentation, using customer behaviors like past engagement and website interactions to deliver the right messages to the right people.

· Dive into the data to track and analyze campaign results, from open rates to click-through rates and conversions.

· Ensure strict adherence to email compliance regulations including the CAN-SPAM Act, HIPAA considerations relevant to healthcare communications, and applicable state-level privacy laws (e.g., CCPA). Maintain proper unsubscribe workflows, consent documentation, and data handling practices.

· Consistently cultivate an irresistible brand voice across all email communications.

· Design and manage marketing automation workflows to nurture leads, re-engage inactive contacts, and support patient acquisition journeys — including triggered sequences, drip campaigns, and behavioral automations.

· Utilize Ortto to build, deploy, and optimize automated campaigns; experience with Ortto is a plus.

_*AI & Marketing Technology*_

· Apply a working understanding of AI tools and platforms (e.g., generative AI for copywriting, AI-powered analytics, predictive segmentation) to elevate campaign performance and content quality.

· Stay current on emerging AI applications in digital marketing and proactively identify opportunities to integrate new technologies into workflows.

· Use AI responsibly and in compliance with company guidelines, ensuring accuracy, brand alignment, and appropriate human oversight of AI-generated content.

_*Collaboration and Team Leadership:*_

· Forge strong connections with our team, including others in marketing, as well as other departments, including operations, clinic directors, and clinic outreach managers.

· Lead a team of content creators (writers, designers, video production) and others in the marketing team, providing them with invaluable feedback and guidance to nurture a culture of continuous growth and improvement.

· Utilizing Project Management Software to keep the team up-to-date with projects.

*Qualifications and Requirements: *

* A bachelor’s degree (ideally in Marketing, Communications, or Journalism).
* 3–5 years of experience in content marketing and email marketing.
* Proficiency in email marketing software (Klaviyo preferred).
* Experience with marketing automation platforms; Ortto knowledge is a plus.
* Demonstrated understanding of email compliance regulations including CAN-SPAM, HIPAA-adjacent communications standards, and applicable privacy laws (e.g., CCPA).
* Working knowledge of AI tools used in marketing contexts, including generative AI for content, AI-powered analytics, and automated personalization.
* Superb writing and editing skills with an eagle eye for details.
* The analytical prowess to decipher web traffic and email campaign metrics.
* Adeptness with SEO, keyword research, and the invaluable insights of Google Analytics.
* Be a team player and bring innovation to the table in the fast-moving industry.
* Ability to work both independently and in a team-oriented, collaborative, fast-paced, results-driven environment.
* Exceptional organizational and time-management abilities.
* Proficient with Microsoft Office Suite.

*Physical Demands:*

The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.

· Frequent typing and viewing of computer screen.

· Frequent use of hand and fingers with machines, such as computer, copier, fax machine, scanner, and telephone.

· Frequent hearing, listening, and speaking by telephone and in person.

· Occasionally required to stand, walk, reach with hands and arms, stoop, or bend.

· Occasionally required to lift objects up to 15lbs. with the ability to lift multiple times per day.

Work Environment:

This position is a remote position so you should have the appropriate accommodations for being able to work at home, including WiFi, and a quiet appropriate environment for working. It will be required to be able to take work calls from your environment, including Zoom and Google Meet.

You may also be asked to come into the office. The work environment described here are representative of those that an employee encounters white performing the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.

· Work indoors in temperature-controlled environment.

· The noise level is usually moderate.

Job Type: Full-time

Pay: $75,000.00 - $95,000.00 per year

Benefits:
* 401(k)
* Dental insurance
* Employee discount
* Health insurance
* Life insurance
* Paid time off
* Vision insurance

Work Location: Remote
