jobs.description:
Job summary

We’re seeking a creative and results-driven Social Media Manager to strengthen Raphael Group’s digital presence across our brands. This role involves creating engaging content, managing social media platforms, responding to inquiries, and driving brand awareness and conversions across our health, wellness, education, and service-based businesses.

 Min Qualification: Degree Experience Level: Mid level Experience Length: 3 years
Job descriptions & requirements
Content creation and storytelling Social media platform management Community engagement and customer interaction Data analysis and performance reporting Graphic and video design/editing skills Strategic planning and campaign execution

Responsibilities:

Develop and implement social media strategies for all Raphael Group brands.
Create, curate, and schedule engaging content (posts, videos, reels, stories, graphics) tailored to each platform.
Respond promptly to inquiries, comments, and messages across social platforms, ensuring top-notch community engagement.
Monitor social media trends, competitor activity, and audience preferences to inform content strategy.
Track, analyze, and report on social media performance metrics (engagement, reach, conversions, ROI).
Collaborate with internal teams (marketing, sales, content, and design) to align campaigns with business objectives.
Optimize social media campaigns for maximum reach, engagement, and conversion.
Maintain a consistent brand voice and identity across all platforms.
Manage social media advertising campaigns, including targeting, budgeting, and performance tracking.
Ensure content and campaigns comply with copyright, privacy, and industry standards.

Requirements:

Degree in Marketing, Communications, Media, or a related field. (Preferred).
A minimum of 4+ years of experience managing multiple brands or diverse business sectors. Knowledge of SEO, SEM, and digital marketing principles (preferred).
Proven experience as a Social Media Manager or similar role.
Strong portfolio showcasing content creation, campaign management, and engagement growth.
Proficiency with social media platforms (Instagram, Facebook, LinkedIn, TikTok, YouTube, Twitter/X).
Excellent writing, editing, and communication skills.
Strong design sense and familiarity with basic graphic/video editing tools (Canva, Photoshop, CapCut, Adobe Suite).
Data-driven mindset with experience in social analytics tools (Meta Business Suite, Google Analytics, Hootsuite, Buffer).
Minimum of 50 Mbps to ensure smooth content upload, live streaming, and platform management.
Highly organized with strong attention to detail.
Ability to work independently and lead campaigns from concept to execution.
Flexibility to adapt to evolving digital trends and brand priorities.

Benefits:
Free monthly access to:

Netflix, Spotify, and Duolingo.
Access to paid online courses.

jobs.title: Social Media Manager
jobs.description:
*Social Media Manager*

Wings Over is building one of the most exciting social brands in food and we’re hiring!

Over the last year, we’ve grown to ~50K followers and generated 20M+ views across Instagram + TikTok. We’re moving fast, experimenting constantly, and building a brand people genuinely love.

This is not a corporate “manage the content calendar” role.

You’ll help shape the voice, look and personality of Wings Over by creating content, jumping on new viral trends, working with influencers, and helping grow a cult favorite brand into a nationwide phenomenon.

If you’re obsessed with internet culture, constantly sending TikToks to your friends, and know how to make content people actually want to watch, we want to hear from you.

*What You’ll Do*

* Create, shoot, edit, and post short-form content
* Jump on trends, memes, and viral moments quickly
* Help shape the creative voice of the Wings Over brand
* Work with influencers and creators on partnerships and content
* Capture content at restaurants, events, and store openings
* Engage with our community across social platforms

*You Should*

* Be chronically online
* Understand TikTok, Instagram, and internet culture
* Have experience creating or growing social accounts
* Know how to shoot and edit content quickly
* Have strong creative instincts and good taste
* Be proactive, organized, and excited to move fast
* Ideally have experience with food, lifestyle, or consumer brands

*Why This Role Is Different*

* Real ownership and creative freedom from day one
* Opportunity to help shape a fast-growing brand early
* Fast-moving team with significant room for growth
* Chance to create content seen by millions

*Location*

Based near one of our markets, ideally in CT or MA

Send examples of content, accounts, or brands you’ve worked on or helped grow

Pay: $75,000.00 - $80,000.00 per year

Benefits:
* 401(k)
* 401(k) matching
* Dental insurance
* Flexible spending account
* Health insurance
* Health savings account
* Vision insurance



Application Question(s):
* Do you have experience creating or growing social accounts?
* Do you live in CT or MA?
* Do you have experience with food, lifestyle, or consumer brands?

Work Location: Remote

jobs.title: Ecommerce and Digital Marketing Manager (Remote)
jobs.description:
PRIME Division

JOIN US AND "CREATE YOUR VISION"

PRIME LINE - POWERED BY S&S ACTIVEWEAR

Prime Line, the hard goods division of S&S, brings promotional product distributors top products in the best-selling categories. Backed by high achieving commercial groups, Prime Line is here to service every need a customer may have.

ABOUT US

S&S Activewear is a leading North American, tech-enabled specialty distributor of imprintable apparel, including t-shirts, fleece, athletic wear, headwear, workwear, scrubs and accessories. We pride ourselves on being the “critical link” that enables our 100+ brand partners such as Adidas, Columbia and Gildan to reach our hyper-fragmented base of customers, comprised of apparel decorators, eRetailers and promotional products distributors that embellish apparel items for diverse end consumer use cases (e.g., everyday wear, concerts, fitness attire, life events, team sports, etc.). At S&S, we differentiate in the market through a customer service focus, strategic product portfolio, world-class distribution infrastructure, a collaborative growth culture and a relentless focus on technology and innovation.

Our success has compounded since the Company’s humble beginnings in 1988 as a regional t-shirt distributor to a leading, multi-billion-dollar value-added distribution platform that drives market penetration for our suppliers while enabling the growth, operations and fulfillment for a diverse range of customers across channels and business sizes. We strive to be the apparel distributor of choice while inspiring and fostering growth for customers, employees and suppliers through our core values of service, deep relationships, innovation, continuous improvement and accountability.

This position is part of our PRIME division, operating under the S&S Activewear parent company. PRIME plays a key role in delivering specialized solutions while aligning with the broader mission and values of S&S Activewear.

ABOUT THE ROLE

Prime Line is seeking a strategic Ecommerce and Digital Marketing Manager – Prime Line to lead the growth and evolution of our Shopify-powered digital presence. While our site does not currently utilize direct e-commerce, it serves as the vital hub for our brand, product education, and lead generation. You will be responsible for driving qualified traffic and ensuring that our digital catalog provides a high-end, seamless experience that converts visitors into high-value leads for our commercial teams.

This role is responsible for driving qualified traffic to the website (digital marketing), and ensuring that once users are on the website, they have the best experience and can complete their goals for visiting (e-commerce). The role reports to the Director of Marketing and works cross-functionally with Marketing, Technology, Customer Service, Sales, Operations, Merchandising, External agencies, and other business stakeholders.

COMPENSATION & SCHEDULE

Salaried: based on experience

Monday - Friday (9am - 5pm eastern) Full-Time, exempt

Some travel will be required.

WHAT YOU WILL DO

Shopify CMS: Act as the strategic owner/liaison with IT of our Shopify-powered site. Manage the planning and delivery of all website operations, ensuring the digital catalog is accurate, brand-aligned, and optimized for a "browse-to-inquiry" user flow.
Lead Generation & UX Optimization: Create a roadmap for web content and UI/UX improvements. Focus on streamlining how customers discover products and interact with our team, ensuring a frictionless path from landing page to lead submission.
Search Visibility (SEO): Use resources to increase organic growth. Implement a comprehensive Shopify SEO strategy—focusing on technical site health, collection page optimization, and content marketing to dominate industry search terms.
Analytics & Performance Insights: Utilize GA4 and Shopify’s backend reporting to monitor traffic patterns and user behavior. Translate data into actionable insights that help us understand which products and content are driving the most engagement.
Shopify App & Tool Management: Manage and optimize the Shopify app stack (e.g., forms, search filters, live chat, or catalog tools) to ensure a high-performance, mobile-responsive experience across all devices.

WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

Education: Bachelor’s degree
Ecommerce Experience: 3+ years’ experience in managing ecommerce and digital marketing operations, ideally in the promotional products or related industry. Strong understanding of eCommerce platforms, user experience design, technologies, and best practices.
Marketing Expertise: Strong knowledge of digital marketing, including SEO, SEM, and other paid media channels. Demonstrated ability to effectively manage agency partners in this space.
Communication: Outstanding communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to build relationships and influence stakeholders at all levels of the organization
Technical Skills: Proficiency in variety of digital technologies (e.g., Google Analytics, NetSuite, Shopify, Content Management Systems)
Getting Things Done: Excellent analytical skills with the ability to interpret data and generate actionable insights. Strong project management skills with the ability to prioritize tasks and meet deadlines in a fast-paced environment
This role is based remotely, with typical working hours M-F 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM ET. Some travel will be required.

EQUAL OPPORTUNITY EMPLOYER

S&S Activewear welcomes people of all backgrounds and experiences and are committed to creating a diverse culture and supporting human growth. We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer and is committed to providing equal opportunity for all employees and applicants. We conduct business without regard to sex, race, creed, color, religion, marital status, national origin, citizenship status, age, pregnancy, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, genetic information, disability, military status, status as a veteran, or any other protected characteristic. Our policy is to recruit, hire, train, promote, assign, transfer, and terminate employees based on their own ability, achievement, experience and conduct and other legitimate business reasons. S&S participates in E-Verify and will provide the federal government with your Form I-9 information to confirm you are authorized to work in the U.S.

This job offer is contingent upon the completion of a satisfactory background check.

jobs.title: Senior Digital Growth & SEO Manager (Marketplace / Search)
jobs.description:
*Reports to: Chief Marketing Officer (CMO)*

*Overview*

Zencare is hiring a Senior Digital Growth Manager to own and scale our highest‑intent consumer acquisition channels, with a primary focus on SEO, landing pages, directory/search performance, and consumer referral growth.

Reporting directly to the CMO, this is a senior‑level, hands‑on role for a performance‑oriented digital marketer who combines strategic judgment with the ability to execute. You will be responsible for building and optimizing the full organic and referral funnel, from first search impression through therapist message or booking, and treating SEO and referrals as performance channels with measurable ROI.

This role is ideal for someone who has worked on search‑driven marketplaces or directory sites, knows how to scale landing pages and technical SEO, and can turn high‑intent demand into repeatable, compounding growth.

*Scope*

* End‑to‑end ownership of organic and referral‑driven consumer growth:
* search → landing page → directory/search experience → message or booking
* Ownership of technical SEO and landing page performance for a large, dynamic marketplace
* Ownership of consumer referral growth as a scalable, performance channel
* Drive prioritization, experimentation roadmap, and channel tradeoffs
* Accountable for traffic quality, conversion rates, and downstream marketplace impact

*Key Responsibilities*

*Performance‑Driven SEO & Acquisition*

* Own and execute the SEO growth strategy, with a strong emphasis on traffic quality, conversion, and long‑term ROI
* Drive keyword and demand opportunity analysis, mapping high‑intent queries to landing pages, directory pages, and on‑site search experiences
* Treat SEO as a performance channel: forecast impact, measure outcomes, and continuously optimize

*Technical SEO & Marketplace Foundations*

* Lead technical SEO execution for a large directory marketplace, including:
* o Site architecture and internal linkingo Indexation and crawl optimizationo Page templates and structured datao Core Web Vitals and performance
* Partner closely with Engineering and Product to ensure new features and templates scale cleanly for search

*Landing Pages & Programmatic Growth*

* Design, launch, and optimize high‑conversion landing pages aligned to consumer intent (location, specialty, need state, etc.)
* Own programmatic SEO initiatives, balancing scale with quality, relevance, and conversion
* Continuously test and improve page structure, messaging, CTAs, and trust signals

*Directory, Search & Conversion Optimization*

* Own the growth performance of directory and on‑site search pages
* Partner with Product to improve filters, ranking logic, navigation, and UX to increase engagement and bookings
* Run structured CRO experiments across organic entry points and high‑intent flows

*Consumer Referrals as a Performance Channel*

* Own and scale consumer referral growth, treating referrals as a measurable, optimizable acquisition channel
* Test and iterate on referral mechanics, incentives, placement, and messaging
* Partner with Product and Marketing to integrate referrals naturally into the consumer journey
* Measure referral contribution to CAC, conversion, and LTV

*Measurement & Insight*

* Own growth reporting and analysis for SEO and referrals, tying channel performance to bookings and marketplace health
* Diagnose underperformance, form hypotheses, and execute focused experiments
* Surface insights and recommendations clearly to the CMO and cross‑functional partners

*What We’re Looking For*

* 7-10+ years of experience in digital growth, performance marketing, or SEO with direct ownership of results
* Deep, hands‑on experience with technical SEO and landing page optimization for large sites
* Experience treating organic and referral channels as performance channels
* Strong understanding of search intent, directory sites, and marketplace UX
* Demonstrated ability to balance strategy and hands‑on execution
* Data‑driven, experimental mindset with comfort operating in ambiguity
* Experience partnering closely with Product and Engineering

*Bonus:*

o Experience with two‑sided marketplaces or search‑driven platforms

o Experience in healthcare, mental health, or trust‑sensitive consumer categories

*What Success Looks Like*

* Measurable increases in high‑intent organic traffic and referral‑driven acquisition
* Clear improvements in conversion from visit to message or booking
* Scalable landing page and directory foundations that support continued growth
* A prioritized, performance‑oriented experimentation roadmap with documented wins
* Strong, trusted partnership with the CMO, Product, and Engineering teams

*Compensation:*

This role is budgeted for a competitive, senior‑level base salary, with additional performance upside. The expected range for U.S. remote candidates is $135,000 – $150,000, depending on experience and skill depth.

Pay: $135,000.00 - $150,000.00 per year



Application Question(s):
* Do you have 7+ years of experience in digital growth, performance marketing, or SEO with direct ownership of results?
* Have you had hands-on ownership of technical SEO for a large website (e.g., site architecture, indexation, internal linking, page templates)?
* Have you worked on a marketplace, directory, or search-driven platform where users browse listings (e.g., Yelp, Zillow, healthcare directories, SaaS marketplaces)?

Work Location: Remote

jobs.title: Digital Marketing Manager (Remote US)
jobs.description:
Directive Consulting is the leading B2B marketing agency for brands that are serious about the metrics that matter most.

With over a decade of expertise and 420+ B2B brands under our belt, we've earned our reputation on real outcomes. We run through 3 divisions: Performance, Communications, and Commerce, all wired to share intelligence and answer to revenue. That system spans everything from Paid Media and Content Marketing, to PR and Paid Social, to Marketplace and Lifecycle Marketing. Tying it all together is our methodology, DiscoverabilityOS, which aligns brand and demand to guide your ICP to choose you across every decision point.

We move fast. We think boldly. We hire people who challenge the status quo, build campaigns worth talking about, and make bold brands impossible to ignore. If you live for performance, love solving hard problems, and want your work to actually mean something, you'll fit right in.

*This role is listed internally as Demand Generation Manager*

About The Role

We are looking to offer a compelling and competitive new demand gen offering for our customers here at Directive. In this role, you will directly work with no more than seven accounts and be directly responsible for their performance marketing.

The ideal candidate for this role is an exceptionally strong T-Shaped Marketer with deep expertise in the SaaS marketplace across Paid, SEO, and CRO. A crucial part of this role will not only be executing campaigns; but also, interfacing with customers, pitching strategy, and ensuring that results are exceeded.

We value your ability to drive client results as an individual contributor and believe that the ideal candidate revels in owning the success of their client portfolio. You will only be successful in this role if you are strong at marketing strategy, execution, communication, and project management.

Roles & Responsibilities

Own the success of SMB clients, across channels

Handle client relationships with poise, confidence, and empathy

Project manage and organize your accounts

Build relationships with your point of contacts

Deep technical knowledge of Strategy, Paid Media, SEO, and CRO for tech companies

Strong SaaS background preferred

Skilled at having a large impact in a very specific role

Creative spark for marketing + deep passion for getting results

What You Offer

2+ years of experience in agency-side marketing

2+ years of experience in performance marketing

Brilliant strategist and a truly T-shaped marketer

World-class project manager who knows how to get things done on time and below cost

Availability to travel

Driven to stay ahead of industry trends, including actively learning how AI and automation can enhance marketing and operations.

What We Offer

 We have a set living wage at Directive; The annual base salary range for this position based in the United States is $80,000- $85,000 USD with potential for bonus eligibility. This salary range is an estimate, and the actual salary may vary based on Directive's compensation practices, job related skills, and depth of experience.

 Medical, dental, vision plans, disability, and life insurance coverage for you and your family that fit your lifestyle

Including a 100% employer-paid plan for you and a 50% employer contribution for your dependents

Benefits to Support the Whole Person:

 Mental - Access to certified therapists through Spring Health, membership to Headspace

 Physical - Gympass

Time Off - Unlimited PTO (2-week minimum), Paid Company Holidays, Your Birthday Off, End of Year Recharge (Closed December 24 - January 1), Paid Parental Leave

Financial - Traditional and Roth 401(k) with a 3% company match

 Bonus - Annual bonus based on tenure, which scales in total amount over time

 Annual Anniversary Trip to Newport Beach, CA with peers and executive leadership for fun and entertainment!

Work Environment Requirements

As a remote-first company, you’ll have the ability to work from anywhere in the US, with the option to enjoy our state-of-the-art offices in Irvine, California. For some positions, as posted, we will accommodate global opportunities where we have established businesses, including Canada, the UK, and Australia. For global locations, you must have established and current work authorization and permanently reside in that country.

This role has the opportunity to operate 100% virtually from your home office. We primarily collaborate with our colleagues through virtual meetings (Zoom), and Slack. In this role, you will be required to operate a laptop computer (PC or Mac available), computer software platforms, and other office productivity tools as necessary. Due to the nature of this role, you must be able to remain stationary for extended periods, must be able to observe and interpret written and/or verbal communication, must have reliable internet access, and a professional background.

To perform this job successfully, an individual must be able to perform each essential job duty satisfactorily. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable qualified individuals with disabilities to perform essential job functions.

If you require reasonable accommodations in completing this application, interviewing, completing any pre-employment testing, or otherwise participating in the employee selection process, direct your inquiries to careers@directiveconsulting.com.

Additional Information

At Directive, one of our core values is People First. We’re committed to fostering a more diverse and inclusive culture in the digital landscape. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status.
